Dialogue
In
Hoelbrak
A smith's work is never finished...and the more I talk to you, the less I get done.
What's the word around the campfire?

Hunters are saying there's been a change in the wind. It's too warm. Too moist.
What do you think it means?

Spirits
and signs.
Do you believe that?
Yes. I believe they're right. The Elder Dragons are changing the
world

Well enough. I've been bashing on a downed copter. Hope to get her in the air.

Vanhe

Historical dialogue
Before "Battle for Lion's Arch—Aftermath"
I hear you trained Scarlet Briar.
That crazy sylvari? Yeah. Her name was Ceara back then. Could keep a fire going better than most of my other forge-tenders. Quick learner. It made up for what she lacked in brawn.
Why did she leave?
From the moment she arrived, she wanted more than I could give her. She didn't like the metal. She didn't love the ring of iron on steel. She wanted answers to bigger questions.
What do you think of what she became?
I...suppose I failed her. She had so much potential, such a natural talent for ore and fire. I tried to keep her grounded, but...I couldn't do it.
That's all. Thanks.
Interesting. Good-bye.
Interesting. Good-bye.
During "Battle for Lion's Arch—Aftermath"
You've heard the news of Scarlet's
death
?
No. She's dead? How did this happen?
She and her
army
have destroyed
Lion's Arch
. It was necessary.
Some apples have worms in them. She was one of those. I appreciate that you made the journey to tell me. Thank you.
I have another question.
It was no trouble. Good-bye.
Later.
After "Battle for Lion's Arch—Aftermath"
You heard Scarlet is dead?
I heard. And I wasn't surprised. Ceara took a wrong turn into
[
sic
]
very dark forest. I had little hope she would emerge intact. Did you have a hand in her death?
I did.
She had to be put down. Her mind had gone rabid, and she knew only how to bite and claw. If a creature like that wanders into a populated area, there's only one choice.
What do you think happened to her?
Evil on that scale doesn't just happen. I've seen it with the
Sons of Svanir
. True evil.
I've seen it too.
I have another question.
I agree.
Not directly.
Whoever it was did the rest of us a huge favor. She was rabid, killing and infecting others with her malice. I've seen true evil among the Sons of Svanir, those corrupted by Jormag.
You didn't see her madness when you knew her?
No, I did not. But, even with the Sons of Svanir, they start normal. The corruption is gradual, insidious. Eventually, it consumes them.
I have another question.

Notes
He is encountered by choosing "The Green Knight" in the
Biography
section of Sylvari
Character creation
.
Bercilak is one of the few
champions
fought in chapter 1 of the
personal story
which has not been reduced to a veteran rank.
Trivia
Bercilak is a reference to a character from the Middle English romance,
Sir Gawain and the Green Knight
.
The Green Knight in said story is garbed in green armor, seemingly immortal, and has the same name (though the name has varying spellings and pronunciations).
The player character's line from the
intro cinematic
"Somewhat. In my dream, a champion in brilliant green armor defeated all challengers. He seemed invincible, yet a small mouse nipped at his heel." most likely is a reference another near immortal warrior
Achilles
. In Greek mythology, Achilles's only vulnerable spot is his heel.
Bercilak wears full
heavy Nightmare Court armor
, wields a
Nightmare Sword
and
Warden Shield
.
Bercilak is
voiced
by
Gideon Emery
.

Quotes
Greetings
Get out of my sight. I got nothing to say to you.
Aggro
You are dead!
The fun's over here!
Plan!
Death
Now I'll never...
Trivia
Big Nose Ted is
voiced
by
Jason Miller
.
Notes
Anomalies
:
Ted and his gang are green allies until they reach their positions. If you attack the bandit mobs and immediately run to the third floor, there's a good chance Ted will get hit by their attacks while they pursue you up the stairs. He will continue to fight all enemy NPCs as long as he's in combat, including Twitchy Jake and Uptown Johnny. Keeping Big Nose Ted as an ally however, means you will have to restart the instance, as his defeat is crucial for completion.
Immediately after the first conversation, when the NPCs reposition for combat - if you run up the stairs to the table on the top floor where Big Nose Ted usually stands, and then back down, there is a very large chance all mobs will end up fighting you downstairs. Big Nose Ted will have his say and then follow after you. However, if you do this too quickly, you may end up in combat with nameless bandits before Ted has a chance to turn hostile, resulting in him remaining friendly and you requiring a restart to the instance, as his defeat is crucial for completion.
See also
Associated items
Mini Big Nose Ted

======================================================================
PAGE: Big Snaff
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Big_Snaff
======================================================================

Big Snaff
Location
Crystalline Memories
(
Prophet's Fall
)
Type
Golem
Interact
Yes
Destructible
No
Big Snaff
was an eighteen-foot tall
cephalolithopathic golem
designed by
Snaff
, and with a head carved by
Eir Stegalkin
. It was lost in the
Dragonspawn
's inner sanctum. The destroyed golem was later found by the newly-formed
Destiny's Edge
, many miles from the lair of the Dragonspawn. Its stanchions were shattered and the servos were split, although
Zojja
suspected some of the thylid crystals and gear work would be salvageable.
A second Big Snaff was later built in preparation for the battle with the
Destroyer of Life
. He had a water cannon mounted above his left hand and rock drills inserted into his right. His chest was an armored cockpit in which Snaff hung on a harness, sending signals through a
powerstone
laurel. When
Destiny's Edge
traveled to the
Crystal Desert
to defeat
Glint
, it had been completely redesigned. In the battle with
Kralkatorrik
, the dragon's second breath—the one that left the starburst shape in the desert, visible even on the world map, turned all things nearby, including Big Snaff and the dragon's own minions, to stone. When more minions came, Snaff was occupied in the dragon's mind, having stepped into it using a powerstone portal. He was not prepared for the minions when they converged on Big Snaff, toppling the golem and tearing its creator to pieces.
Big Snaff was left in
Glint's Lair
, its surface crystallized by Kralkatorrik.
Story involvement
Path of Fire story
Act II
Crystalline Memories

======================================================================
PAGE: Big Zojja
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Big_Zojja
======================================================================

Big Zojja
was an eighteen-foot tall
cephalolithopathic golem
designed by
Snaff
, and with a head carved by
Eir Stegalkin
. It was lost in the
Dragonspawn
's inner sanctum when Snaff,
Zojja
,
Eir Stegalkin
, and
Garm
made their first attempt to kill the creature, and the wreckage was found when the newly-formed
Destiny's Edge
returned to the site of the battle. With parts from
Big Snaff
, it was able to be salvaged, before going out of commission again when falling down a slope.
A second Big Zojja was under construction at the time of the battle with the
Destroyer of Life
, but was far from complete. When Destiny's Edge traveled to the
Crystal Desert
to defeat
Glint
, it had been completely redesigned. Big Zojja survived the battle with
Kralkatorrik
, but what happened to it after is unknown.

was a
Priest of Balthazar
who served at the temple near
Old Lion's Arch
in 1219
AE
. He was about seven feet tall, and he fought with a giant war hammer in each hand.

======================================================================
PAGE: Biviane
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Biviane
======================================================================

Biviane
(1215
AE
-1219
AE
) was the younger sister of
Cobiah Marriner
. Her mother was an alcoholic who became abusive after Biviane's father's disappearance at sea, leaving Cobiah responsible for her welfare. She carried her favorite doll,
Polla
, everywhere.
In 1219
AE
, Cobiah left her with Romy while he went to go and talk to their mother, after telling her a story about a mermaid he saw aboard the
Indomitable
and giving her a silver for some breakfast. Romy took his eyes off her for a moment and she was off, saying that she wanted to see a mermaid. She climbed one of the dock pylons, slipped, and fell to a quick death beneath the pier.
See also
Family trees

Blighted Pale Tree
Race
Mordrem
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Hearts and Minds
(
Dragon's Stand (Heart of Thorns)
)
“
The dragon created us. It sustains us, defines us. Without it, we wither and die.
—
Blighted Pale Tree
Blighted Pale Tree
is a mental corruption produced by
Mordremoth
to fight
Caithe
in the
Dream of Dreams
.
Story involvement
Heart of Thorns story
Act IV
Hearts and Minds
Combat abilities
Abilities
Ring Attacks
Summons
Behavior
Summons
Firstborn Punishers
,
Stalkers
,
Snipers
, and
Tormentors
at 75%, 50%, and 25% health.
Eight and twelve are summoned at 75% and 50% health, respectively. Continuously summoned in sets of five at 25% health and below.
Effects
Weakened
— Occurs when defiance is broken.
Determined
(at 75%, 50%, 25%, and 1% health)
- Determined to survive and immune to damage.
Power
(upon consuming a Firstborn)
- Each consumed Firstborn empowers all skills.
Weakened
(at 1% health)
- Invulnerable to all but
Rift Overcharge
.
Skills
Blighted Beam
- Auto-attack. Inflicts
Knockdown
(1s)
if empowered with
Power
.
Draining Bramble
- Summon an expanding AoE under an enemy that inflicts damage and
Crippled
(9s)
.
Nightmare
(variant 1)
- Summon an eye under an enemy that periodically fires a circle of orb projectiles.
Nightmare
(variant 2; at 75%, 50% , and 25% health and below)
- Unleash a devastating attack that inflicts
Launch
. Consume all stacks of
Power
.
Blighted Ring
(starting at 75% health and below)
- Concentric AoE rings that expand outwards and inflicts
Float
(1s)
. Expansion speed increased after each phase.
Stolen skills
Branch Bash
Throw Gunk

======================================================================
PAGE: Blish
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Blish
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Blish
Race
Golem
,
Asura
Location
Allied Encampment
(
Domain of Kourna
)
Organization
College of Synergetics
Inquest
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
Life as a machine is incomprehensible to living beings.
—
Blish
Blish
is an
asuran
portal specialist, the older brother of
Gorrik
,
and a
college
friend of
Taimi
's. He has transferred his consciousness into a
Mark I Golemite
body.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
.
After leaving the Inquest with Gorrik to aid Taimi, the
Pact Commander
and their allies in the fights against the
lich
Palawa Joko
and the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, Blish ultimately
sacrifices himself
in
the Mists
to power up a tracker attached to
Balthazar's Sword
so that the allied forces can monitor Kralkatorrik's movements and bring about the Elder Dragon's downfall.
Biography
Early years
Blish became a member of the
College of Synergetics
like
Taimi
and got to know her during their studies in
Rata Sum
. It is hinted that, similar to Taimi's degenerative disease, he suffered from a disease that would prove fatal in time.
During Taimi's time in the
Heart of Maguuma
, Blish and his brother
Gorrik
joined the
Inquest
. Blish eventually transferred his consciousness into a
Mark I Golemite
body. This technology was developed by the Inquest, but until the
M.I.G.
project by
Hronk
's
krewe
, always proved fatal. Due to the generic appearance of his
golem
body, Blish wore a lab coat to distinguish himself.
Living World Season 4
A Bug in the System
In 1331
AE
, Blish and Gorrik were working in the Inquest base of
Rata Primus
on the
Scarab Plague
samples taken from
Fahranur, the First City
and discovered the
Awakened
invasion that was being covered up by the megacomplex's upper echelons. They took it upon themselves to send out distress signals and barricade the plague samples, as well as attempted to tamper with the Primus gate network that allowed travel without a receiving gate in order to hamper with Awakened attempts to use the gates for their own purposes.
Blish and Gorrik were contacted by the
Pact Commander
and
Braham Eirsson
who had spearheaded an
Olmakhan
assault on Rata Primus to investigate what the Awakened and Inquest were up to. Blish was surprised to hear Taimi's voice coming out of the Commander's personal remote communicator but quickly set the surprise aside as the brothers instructed the team where to find the gate hub so it could be shut down.
The brothers kept Awakened invaders from reaching the bottom lab and hid there long enough for the Commander and Braham to come to the rescue. Blish introduced himself to the newcomers, but before Taimi could inquire why his voice sounded different, the Commander suggested that they keep the reveal of Blish being a golem from Taimi until after they had reunited with her in person. Blish and Gorrik revealed that the Awakened presence had triggered the decontamination protocol which would make the complex self-destruct with no way of stopping it.
Teaming up with the Commander, they rushed to the vaults containing the plague samples, only to discover the samples had already been taken. They headed for the gate hub next, witnessing
Palawa Joko
and
Commander Lonai
in possession of the samples and about to flee through an asura gate. Joko taunted the Commander by promising to unleash the Scarab Plague on
Central Tyria
before entering the portal with the samples, leaving Lonai behind to deal with the group. During the resulting clash the gate, which Joko had tampered with to prevent anyone from pursuing him, began malfunctioning and sucked the Commander, Braham and Lonai in.
Blish and Gorrik began working on the gate to stabilize it and return the Commander and Braham from wherever the gate had sent them, using Lonai's energy signature to direct a portal to the trio's location. However, the attempts failed at first as the battling trio were being teleported around Tyria before they ended up in an unknown location somewhere in
the Mists
where Lonai was finally defeated. The brothers were ultimately successful in teleporting the Commander and Braham aboard
Sayida the Sly
's airship outside of Rata Primus and fled the complex with Gorrik's pets just as it was destroyed in an explosion.
After reuniting with Blish, Taimi expressed fascination with his condition, a welcomed change to the usual disgust or horror the brothers had seen. Blish was happy to be with Taimi again, admitting that he had missed her mind. He revealed that he had managed to back up Inquest data on the Scarab Plague and the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, and Gorrik warmed up to the idea of working with Taimi as long as they discussed authorship hierarchy for any published research and stipulated that Taimi did not have supervisory authority.
Long Live the Lich
After traveling to the
Free City of Amnoon
, Blish and Gorrik began researching the Scarab Plague. Gorrik discovered that the scarabs only infected
humans
and planted
Petey
, another species of beetle, under his skin to test another theory of his, but locals discovered what he was up to and arrested him after mistaking him for planning to unleash some pestilence in the city. While Gorrik was put on trial, the
Cavaliers
impounded his materials at the docks, including Blish who was shoved into one of the crates with his neural net cut off.
After the Commander and Taimi had helped clear the charges against Gorrik, the trio went to the docks and eventually found Blish. Upon being rebooted, Blish returned to consciousness with a horrified scream, letting his companions know of his lack of sensory experiences in what he described as nothingness while he had been shut down. Taimi and Gorrik hastily promised never to let him experience that kind of state again and monitored him while the Commander left them to meet with
Lord Faren
and
Councilor Mayameen
who were waiting for a Tyrian supply ship to arrive at the docks.
Blish, Gorrik and Taimi went to the docks later and learned that the Commander had barely prevented an outbreak of the first generation of Scarab Plague carried by the crew of the supply ship whom Joko had secretly infected. Upon realizing that Joko was breeding a more potent third generation of scarabs, which would be ready to be unleashed on Elona and Tyria in a mere few days, and that Joko's
stronghold
in the
Domain of Kourna
would take weeks to reach, Blish offered a faster way to reach their destination. He had a portable base prototype of the gate system, which Joko had used earlier to send Awakened invasion forces to Tyria, and could calibrate an instant entry point to Kourna behind enemy lines. Seeing the benefits of Blish's suggestion, the Commander asked him to set up a portal near the
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
as they would need help from all the people sympathetic to their cause in Elona, including the
ghosts
, if they were to fight through Joko's army to get to Joko himself.
Upon setting up the portal, Blish lost his arm into it, and the Commander followed it to Kourna. The portal closed soon after, and Blish began working on stabilizing it to turn it big enough for their allied army to get through safely. Blish was able to continue communicating with the Commander and instructed them to use the gadgets on his severed golem arm during the scouting mission. He turned the portal into a permanent rift and sent in troops in waves at the Commander's suggestion.
Upon entering Kourna through the rift, Blish participated in securing the area with the rest of allies, turning the overtaken garrison and its nearby holdings into the
Allied Encampment
. After listening to the Commander addressing the gathered allied army, Blish asked to have his arm back and began working on reattaching it to his golem body. While staying in the allied base, Blish witnessed the Commander, Gorrik and Taimi discuss Petey's recent growth spurt and how this
roller beetle
could be used against the scarabs if properly mutated and incubated. He helped build a
saddle
for Petey after having received relevant parts and schematics for it from the Commander who scoured Kourna for them.
Blish continued working on his arm during the
Battle of Gandara
which ultimately led to the
deaths
of Palawa Joko and the third generation Scarab Plague. Once he had fully repaired his golem body, he focused on keeping an eye on the rift near the allied base and began wondering whether it had been a wise idea to rip a hole in the fabric of space-time as it could have unforeseen effects on Tyria.
A Star to Guide Us
While the rift anomalies of
Jahai Bluffs
were being investigated, Blish was with Taimi, observing the data obtained by the Commander and theorizing with Taimi and Gorrik over what might have caused them. After the allied forces had claimed
Sun's Refuge
for their base of operations in the region, Blish was the one to calm down all the refugees by saying he already had a plan and was in the midst of enacting it with the Commander. He thought that he could place a tracker on
Kralkatorrik
if the dragon were tricked into eating a magically-rich item, such as
Balthazar's Sword
. He proceeds to follow the Commander around the world until finding out that a
sniper priestess
had taken the sword and was hiding out in an abandoned citadel.
Once the sword was retrieved and reignited, with
Rytlock Brimstone
's help, Blish and the Commander took the sword through a rift tear into the Mists and placed a tracker on it. However, the sword started reacting to the presence of Kralkatorrik and began attacking wildly, heavily damaging the tracker's power core. Unable to fix the power core in time, Blish told the Commander to go as he stayed behind to fix it, claiming that being a golem, Kralkatorrik could not brand him, and asked the Commander to come back for him after the storm was over. As the Commander retreated, Blish revealed that he had lied to the Commander, and could not fix the power core, but would have to use his golem body to power the tracker indefinitely-at the cost of turning off his higher level functions. As Kralkatorrik grew closer, he said good-bye to the Commander and asked them to give his apology to Gorrik and Taimi, before shutting off.
Legacy
End of Dragons
When the allied forces of Tyria, Elona, and
Cantha
faced off
against the
Void
-maddened Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
at the
Harvest Temple
in 1335
AE
, the Pact Commander and several of their allies' souls were separated from their bodies while shielding their comrades from the Water Dragon's attacks. As their disembodied spirits tried to climb up the Harvest Temple to return to their bodies, memories of the past which belonged to allies and enemies alike whispered to them to encourage them to keep going. One of these whispering memories was Blish who once again asked the Commander to give his apology to Gorrik and Taimi, take care of Gorrik, and stop keeping secrets from one another.
What Lies Within
After a harrowing escape from an
oni
known as the
Ravenous Wanderer
which weaponized its victims' traumas deep within
Gyala Delve
, the Commander
ventured across Tyria
with the goal to revisit some crucial and emotional moments from their past and harness these memories' emotional power to charge Gorrik and Taimi's new invention, the Recollector, with which to turn the tables against the Wanderer.
During this journey the Commander and Gorrik visited Rata Sum where they perused Blish's old writings detailing his past. While reliving the guilt of not being able to save Blish, the Commander and Gorrik witnessed brief manifested memories of Blish. The visions reminded them not only of Blish's apology to Taimi and Gorrik and his request for all of them to stop keeping secrets from one another but also Blish's wish to not relive the existential horror of being shut down, how him being able to live on as a golem had given him to resolve to make his second chance at life count for something, and him asking the Commander to come back for him once Kralkatorrik's storm had passed as they had come too far to give up. Gorrik comforted the Commander by stating that Blish would be proud of what their group had accomplished and that the Commander still remembered him after so many years since his passing.

PAGE: Bowmaster Echo

Charr

80

Victory or Death
(
Fort Trinity
,
Victory or Death
)
Organization
Pact
Braccus Jawsmash
is a
charr
found with
Explorer Bekk
,
Explorer Urond
, and
Magister Galen
during the celebration of
Zhaitan
's defeat
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 8: Victory or Death
Victory or Death
Notes
Anomaly
:
During Volcanic Extraction, the team is comprised of Explorer Bekk, Explorer Urond, Magister Galen, and
Agent Furius
. However, the team appears again during
Victory or Death
, with the NPCs named Bekk, Hester Galen, Urond Ramshearne, and
Braccus Jawsmash
. It is unclear if Agent Furius is Braccus (especially since their appearances are identical) or was replaced or died between the two story steps.

- Branded wyvern is healed for 3% of its maximum health every second until Recharging Crystals are destroyed.

from the

to bring down Mazdak.

and

======================================================================
PAGE: Bronn Svaard
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Bronn_Svaard
======================================================================

Stub. Key elements of this npc article are incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
can be expanded
Bronn Svaard
, along with his twin brother
Grymm
, was a
norn
sword-for-hire aboard the
Salma's Grace
in 1229
AE
. He is described as having salt-gold hair that capped a broad face, and muttonchops and a thick moustache adorning a wide jaw. The brothers were actually
Priory Explorers
, with the mission of learning all they could about the
Orrians
. After the
Salma's Grace
joined forces with
Cobiah Marriner
to fight the Orrian xebec,
Harbinger
, they remained with Cobiah's crew until hired by
Captain Yomm
in 1237
AE
.
He had married
Hedda
and with her had three sons:
Teir
,
Tryggvi
, and
Kaive
.
During the
Great Krytan Blockade
of 1256
AE
, they joined
Captain Isayeh
aboard the
Nomad II
to fight
Captain Whiting
's Risen aboard the
Indomitable
. Bronn was killed by Whiting during the battle and turned into an undead abomination himself. He was put down by his brother.

golems
participating in the fighting tournament during the mission
Short Fuse
.
Story involvement
Personal story

======================================================================
PAGE: Cadeyrn
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Cadeyrn
======================================================================

Cadeyrn
Race
Sylvari
Level
50
Rank
Champion
Location
Hall of Heart's Remorse
(
Twilight Arbor
)
Organization
Nightmare Court
Image(s)
Newly awakened in 1304
AE
at
The Grove
.
“
I will make you hear me, Mother, like it or not. When I am finished and you are free at last, then I will be first in your heart!
—
Cadeyrn
Cadeyrn
, born of the
Cycle of Noon
, was the first of the
secondborn
sylvari
to awaken. In time, as he witnessed the harm of the world and heard the darker emotions of his heart, he would come to see the teachings of
Ventari
as a shackling weight on the sylvari. Because of this, he established the
Nightmare Court
with the belief that in darkening the
Pale Tree
's pool of memories, the sylvari will be free of the
Tablet's
impositions and finally reach their true potential.
Biography
Prideful even as he awakened, Cadeyrn showed signs of having shadows close to his heart. In time he became a capable warrior, but he was one whose versions of logic and justice were different from other sylvari. It was this difference in ideology that would eventually turn his blade against both
The Grove
and the world. He was there to witness the aftermath of
Malomedies
' first bitter contact with the
asura
, and in battle he saw no hope in mercy.
He grew jealous of the attention the Pale Tree had given to the firstborn and came to believe that the secondborn should have as much a say in matters as their elder siblings did. In each occasion, Cadeyrn only saw peace through aggression, and as each turn was stymied, his enmity of the Tablet grew. Despite a supplication to the Pale Tree, Cadeyrn began to see one thing clearly: the sylvari, with hands tied by the Tablet's teachings, would never be whole. With this thought in a jealous heart, he began his campaign to make the Pale Tree listen to him, and to unleash the sylvari from false morality.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 2
.
Cadeyrn began holding meetings with other like-minded secondborn sylvari, even inviting firstborn
Faolain
who sympathized with his views. Following the liberation of
Canach
and other sylvari captives from
Vorpp
and the asura, Faolain got in touch with Cadeyrn and asked him and his fellow sylvari followers to accompany her and Caithe on their journey to a
centaur
camp in the
Silverwastes
to bring back firstborn
Wynne
and to lend assistance if the centaurs Wynne was visiting turned out to be hostile.
Due to misunderstandings, suspicion on both sides and Faolain's aggressive actions, the meeting with the centaurs escalated into a battle during which Cadeyrn's forces massacred the centaurs while Caithe and Faolain chased after Wynne to a nearby cave. Following Wynne's death at Caithe's hands, Faolain emerged from the cave alone and left with Cadeyrn to turn their group of followers into what would come to be known as the Nightmare Court. Faolain would eventually assume command as Grand Duchess and turned Cadeyrn into one of her lieutenants.
In 1325
AE
when the Nightmare Court was at the peak of its power, Caithe and a group of adventurers, including the
Pact Commander
, invaded
Twilight Arbor
to end Faolain's reign of terror. The Grand Duchess lured Caithe deeper into the arbor in hopes of using her manipulations and illusions to turn her former lover to her side. Faolain sent various champions after Caithe to test her skills and wear her down both emotionally and physically, and Cadeyrn was among these champions. Cadeyrn was eager to battle Caithe, unleashing all his hatred of the firstborn on her but was ultimately bested and slain before Caithe's party pressed on to confront Faolain herself. Cadeyrn, the founder of the Nightmare Court, had fallen with little fanfare, and his legacy would be overshadowed by Faolain's deeds in the minds of sylvari for years to come.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Twilight Arbor (story)
Hall of Heart's Remorse
Story involvement
Living World Season 2
Chapter 7: Seeds of Truth

swiftness

======================================================================
PAGE: Canach
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Canach
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
Living World
and all
expansions
.
Canach
Race
Sylvari
Rank
Champion
Locations
Dragon's Stand (Heart of Thorns)
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Allied Encampment
(
Domain of Kourna
)
The Grotto
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Pact Command
(
Dragonfall
)
Dragon Response Mission: Caledon Forest
(
Caledon Forest
)
Arborstone
(
Cantha
)
Breezy Cay
(
Shipwreck Strand
)
Organization
Consortium
Shining Blade
Club Canach
Services
Renown Heart
Image(s)
Canach's business appearance.
Interactive map
“
It's high time I walk as my own sylvari for a while.
—
Canach
Canach
, born of the
Cycle of Night
,
is a
Secondborn
sylvari
who was initially a
Consortium
mercenary, arrested for his actions in
Southsun Cove
and imprisoned in
Vigil Keep
. More recently, he has served as an operative for the
Shining Blade
and as an occasional ally to
Dragon's Watch
.
In
Shipwreck Strand
, Canach serves as a
renown heart
vendor.
Biography
Early years
As a
newly awakened
sylvari
, Canach and a few other
Secondborn
were captured by an
asuran
research team lead by
Vorpp
around 1304
AE
. He was cruelly experimented upon by the curious asura, and even used to power a
golem
with his life-force. Although Canach was
rescued
by
Caithe
and
Faolain
, he was greatly rattled by his experience.
Living World Season 1
Canach in a cage at
Fort Marriner
The Lost Shores
Canach was hired by the
Consortium
in 1325
AE
to map, explore, and begin construction of settlements and tourist facilities at the newly-discovered
Southsun Cove
. Although his contract prevented him from exploiting animal, mineral, or
magical
sources on the island, it did not mention local flora, and Canach ordered his team to collect samples of
Passion Flowers
as they explored.
The team's actions provoked the local
karka
, which followed the fleeing survivors of the expedition back to
Lion's Arch
, where they attacked and destroyed
Lion's Gate
. Blamed by the authorities for his role in the karka invasion, Canach was apprehended in
Garenhoff
by the
Lionguard
. To avoid punishment, he offered to help fight the
Ancient Karka
, but fled at the first opportunity.
Facing life as a fugitive and the freelance "decommissioning" team sent by the Consortium's
Subdirector Noll
, Canach made a concerted effort to change. He cast himself as a champion of the weak, and, to prove it, sought out and destroyed a
Molten Alliance
weapons facility
.
Last Stand at Southsun
Canach intended to get even with Noll and liberate the refugees from the Molten Alliance who had been tricked into Consortium settlement contracts on Southsun Cove. After
learning of Noll's plans
and taking
Blimm
's golem
Subdirector NULL
which he reprogrammed to help him with his scheme, Canach headed back to the island and spread a toxin that aggravated the local wildlife. The civil unrest induced there attracted the attention of a Lionguard inspector,
Ellen Kiel
, who
tracked down
the sylvari with the help of the
Pact Commander
, arrested him, and jailed him at
Fort Marriner
, before Canach could destroy any more evidence of Consortium wrongdoing.
Battle for Lion's Arch
During
Scarlet Briar
's
attack on Lion's Arch
, Canach was moved from his cell and incarcerated at
Vigil Keep
. Shortly afterward, an unnamed official from Divinity's Reach offered to buy his release, and the sylvari left the keep around the time that
Mordrem
invaded the
Iron Marches
.
Living World Season 2
By 1327
AE
,
Countess Anise
bought Canach's billet and turned him into an agent of the
Shining Blade
to serve his sentence. Anise used him to
distract guests
from her
mesmer
clone
during a party in
Divinity's Reach
, leaving her free to eavesdrop. She took Canach with her to
the World Summit
, teasing him mercilessly, much to his annoyance.
After the attack on the summit by the
Shadow of the Dragon
, Anise ordered Canach to join the
Pact
in
Fort Trinity
to learn more about their plans for the
Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
whose Mordrem had been appearing in various locations throughout
Central Tyria
. Canach later accompanied Caithe on
a scouting mission
in
the Silverwastes
, but they were routed by
Mordrem
until the Pact Commander came to their rescue.
Heart of Thorns
Once the news of Mordremoth ambushing and crashing the Pact fleet spread to the Silverwastes, Canach traveled to the
Heart of Maguuma
where he joined the Pact Commander and their party in their search for missing Pact survivors including Marshal
Trahearne
and
Destiny's Edge
. Like all sylvari, he struggled with the Elder Dragon's call, but his strength of will kept his mind from succumbing. His curiosity about the
Mordrem Guard
made him test how exactly the dragon's call affected a sylvari's personality when the party
encountered a wounded Mordrem Guard
deep within the lost asuran city of
Rata Novus
, only to be forced to finish off the guard who succumbed to the dragon's call.
Within
Mordremoth's inner sanctum
, Canach offered to join the Commander as they delved into the Elder Dragon's mind for a final battle. Mordremoth spawned a
Blighted illusion
of Canach to goad his being forced to be a servant one way or another, but Canach declared that he would redeem himself and be his own master. The party eventually slew Mordremoth's mind in the
Dream of Dreams
and finished the dragon off in the physical realm by killing the imprisoned Marshal Trahearne in whom Mordremoth had planted one last seed of its consciousness.
Living World Season 3
Even after Mordremoth's fall, Canach remained under Anise's command for a time. He led a Shining Blade operation in
Bloodstone Fen
in 1329
AE
, tracking down
Minister Caudecus
who had fled
Krytan
custody. After being cornered, Caudecus revealed his true colors as the leader of the
White Mantle
and
evaded capture
much to Canach's dismay.
Caudecus and the White Mantle eventually
laid siege
to Divinity's Reach, and Canach was sent to
infiltrate
Caudecus's Manor
. The sylvari was captured and held captive for a time, but was ultimately freed when the Pact Commander stormed the manor with Anise and
Demmi Beetlestone
. Together they were able to bring down Caudecus albeit at the cost of Demmi's life, and Anise made good on her promise to declare Canach's debt paid. Canach declined the Commander's invitation to a new
guild
,
Dragon's Watch
, because he wished to remain independent for a time. However, he mused that his and the Commander's paths would cross again.
Path of Fire
Along his travels, Canach headed to the
Free City of Amnoon
after a large number of
Elonian
refugees had fled to Lion's Arch from the rogue
Balthazar
's invading
Forged
army. He began enjoying gambling at
Zalambur
's
casino
, where he was
reunited
with some members of Dragon's Watch, including the Pact Commander, in 1330
AE
. Having placed a bet that the Commander could fell the former
god
Balthazar
whose Forged were terrorizing the desert, he joined the Commander,
Kasmeer Meade
, and
Rytlock Brimstone
in fighting the Forged.
During their campaign in the
Crystal Desert
, Canach developed a friendly rivalry with Rytlock, witnessed the
sacrifice
of
Glint
's first
scion
Vlast
and
explored
Glint's lair
with the party. Using Kasmeer's mesmer magic to masquerade as Awakened, they took control of the
Awakened
army to oppose the Forged and participated in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
in which the Commander
killed
Balthazar although the resulting explosion of magic was absorbed by the
dragon
Aurene
and the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
who flew off. After returning to a victory celebration in Amnoon, the party were
reunited
with recently arrived Dragon's Watch members
Marjory Delaqua
and
Taimi
although the festivities were cut short when a
Brandstorm
conjured by Kralkatorrik hit Amnoon.
Living World Season 4
After the Pact Commander's victory over Balthazar, Canach went all in with his winnings and bets that the Commander would defeat Kralkatorrik as well. When the Commander arrived to
help Amnoon
during the Brandstorm, Canach assisted in defeating the Branded and rescuing the civilians. After the battle, he witnessed a vision from Aurene along with other party members but remained in Amnoon for a time to help clean up while the Commander headed for the
Domain of Istan
to investigate Aurene's premonition of a threat to Tyria originating there.
Eventually, Canach and Rytlock traveled to Istan as well and fought alongside the Istani
Sunspears
as they
stormed
the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
to rescue both the
Spearmarshal Zaeim
and the Commander who had been imprisoned there by the
Awakened
. The sylvari then joined the Commander in pursuit of Taimi into
Fahranur
where he planted explosives to destroy a bone wall that blocked their progress only to cause a cave-in if not for the timely intervention of
Rox
and
Braham Eirsson
who cast a barrier that protected the party. As the two parties exchanged information, they joined forces and eventually saved Taimi from a death trap. They learned that
Palawa Joko
, the undead tyrant of Elona, had seemingly returned to Elona and was planning an Awakened invasion of Tyria in retaliation for the Commander taking control of his army during the campaign against Balthazar.
Upon learning that Joko's Awakened invasion was a ruse and the lich king's real goal was to unleash a revived
Scarab Plague
upon Tyria, Canach joined the Commander's growing army of Elonian and Tyrian volunteers to take the battle to Joko in the
Domain of Kourna
. After the Commander had
scouted
Zelbahn Garrison
and cleared it of hostiles, the sylvari helped grow bramble walls around the garrison to give the allied forces a well defended staging ground for their campaign against the Awakened.
Canach joined the allied forces in the
assault
on
Gandara
where Joko was incubating the deadly third generation of Scarab Plague. The explosives provided by
Lord Faren
allowed Canach to blow up the sentry towers, which guarded the bridge leading to the fortress, as well as the main gate. However, the party soon realized that Joko had lured them into a trap: the third generation of Scarab Plague was already ready to be unleashed as human hosts incubating it were lying in wait on the other side of the gate. The timely intervention of the
Primeval
ghosts
led by Queens
Dahlah
and
Nahlah
managed to contain the plague for a time, and Canach and most of the allied forces provided assistance to the ghosts while the Commander and Braham used a sewer entrance to sneak into the fortress to stop Joko once and for all.
Following Joko's demise in the jaws of Aurene and the end of the Scarab Plague, Canach was one of the allies who entered the fortress to check up on the Commander and Braham. Troubled by Aurene's violence, Canach wondered if absorbing some of Balthazar's magic had changed the dragon somehow or if it was simply her nature as the granddaughter of Kralkatorrik.
Canach participated in the assault on Kralkatorrik in the Thunderhead Peaks, heading a team which planted explosives in order to pin the dragon down with the side of a mountain.
Canach attended the wake for Aurene and witnessed her miraculous resurrection and subsequent departure into the Mists in order to flush out Kralkatorrik. He met the Pact Commander again in the newly created landmass called
Dragonfall
. Canach stayed with Almorra and Gorrik during the final assault.
The Icebrood Saga
Canach made an explosive reappearance during
the attack on Caledon Forest
by
Icebrood
. He aided in the evacuation of the survivors to
Caledon Haven
.
Canach joined the Pact Commander to aid the
Dominion of the Winds
after
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
asked for help. He assisted in both destroying the
Corrupted Ice Wall
as well as the
Frost Colossus
that appeared as a final attempt to conquer the Dominion of Winds.
Afterwards he revealed that he was there on a brief stopover for his own journey, spending the money he collected from the bets on the Pact Commander's victory over Kralkatorrik. This required him to stay out of world-ending events, but he promised to bet on the commander's victory.
End of Dragons
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing involvement from EoD

Castora
, and the sinking of his ship the
Martingale
, Canach opened a new establishment in
Breezy Cay
on
Shipwreck Strand
called
Pub Canach
.

- Immunity to damage from incoming attacks.

- Basic melee attack.

-
Leaps
to target, afflicting
launches
.

Dialogue
For previous dialogue:
Canach/dialogue
Dragon's Stand (Heart of Thorns)
Before completing
Hearts and Minds
I've spent much of my life hating the firstborn, always being compared to them. In our mother's eyes, Trahearne was perfect and none of us could live up to him. But now...
Your opinion has changed?
With Mordremoth pounding against my willpower, I'm barely holding on. And yet, Trahearne...he flew his airship straight at it. I can only admire him now. We must find him.
Don't worry. We won't abandon him.
I worry that it's already too late. Mordremoth will have its hooks in Trahearne's body and mind much deeper than it will in Zojja and Logan. Its what we sylvari were designed for.
We'll find a way. We...have to.
After completing Hearts and Minds
Mordremoth is gone. I no longer hear its summons. I no longer feel the scratch of its will against my mind.
(If sylvari)
We sylvari have grown beyond what we were created for.
(If not sylvari)
It would seem you sylvari have grown beyond what you were created for.
So it would appear. The Pale Tree endowed us with the willpower to choose our own fates. This victory will bring on a great change for the sylvari...for me.
What will you do now?
(sigh) I must return to Countess Anise and report our victory. She'll be pleased, and I'll take advantage of her goodwill to broach the topic of releasing my billet. I yearn to be completely free.
Good luck, my friend. I wish it for you.
Domain of Kourna
Before completing
Be My Guest
Canach:
The bramble walls were a stroke of genius. If I do say so myself.
After completing Be My Guest
Canach:
Change is in the air, Commander. Can you smell it? Smells like dead Joko. I like it.
Thunderhead Peaks
Commander.
I hear you're running a tournament.
Just a friendly competition to help bolster the troops' morale and keep them sharp for the battle to come. Also—I enjoy watching Logan and Rytlock yell at each other. Care to place a bet?
And how do I do that?
Simple. Talk to my friend here and make your selection: Logan or Rytlock. Winners achieve the satisfaction of knowing they have an eye for talent. Losers are shamed relentlessly.
I wanted to ask about something else.
I don't lose.
I must be going.
How's the training for the Corsairs going?
Well enough. Logan is leading that assignement. Rytlock and I are assisting where we can. We'll leave shortly to do another exercise—perhaps you should join us. We could use an extra pair of hands.
Something else I wanted to ask...
I see if I can swing by.
I need to go, actually.
Arborstone
Commander! I trust you're enjoying the club.
Life as a club owner seems to be treating you well.
Indeed. What began as a wager between Sayida and I turned into an interesting challenge. Though I find that now that it's well established, my interest is...waning.
Does a "social club" feel like a good fit for the area?
But of course! We've been busy ever since we opened the doors. People need distractions, Commander, especially in dire times. You should know that better than anyone.
You're getting bored with it already, though?
(same as "Life as a club owner seems to be treating you well.")
I suppose you're right.
Bored already?
Perhaps a little. There's an excitement in the challenge of a creation of a thing that doesn't exist in its maintenance. On the other hand, money is nice.
It seems life as a business owner is treating you well.
(same as "Life as a club owner seems to be treating you well.")
Does a "social club" feel like a good fit for the area?
(same as "Does a "social club" feel like a good fit for the area?")
It does solve a lot of problems.
I see.
Are you sure a social club is really a fit for this place?
(same as "Does a "social club" feel like a good fit for the area?")
Absolutely.
Shipwreck Strand
(
Breezy Cay Renown Mastery
not started)
Commander! Fancy meeting you here! Welcome to Pub Canach! Get it? Club, pub... Never mind. So, the
Martingale
left a bad taste in my mouth. I decided to cleanse my palate with a new business venture.
That was...quick. Give me the rundown!
Paxx is slinging top-notch drinks for the locals, Daxx has concocted some fantastic plates with his usual panache... Lend them a capable hand or two! Or bounce a few rowdy patrons if you're feeling frisky!
Maybe later.
Well, you seem on top of things! Any more news about the area?
“
There are certainly a multitude of things you can do!
Help Daxx serve fine cuisine to hungry patrons.
Assist Paxx with mixing drinks and serving them to parched customers.
Give the rowdier patrons some commander-style encouragement to settle down.
Wash dishes. Yuck, I know. But it has to be done, friend.
Resupply the barrels. After all, if the drinks don't flow, neither does the coin, eh?
— Canach
So, how am I doing with the pub? (Show Renown Mastery Achievement)
Talk to you later, Canach.

Ah, there you are. This place is really growing on me, I must say. Perhaps the Martingale's sinking was a blessing in disguise, hm? As they say, "When life gives you fish...make money."
Nobody says that. So, what's going on?

Notes
Canach has had numerous armor and character appearance changes throughout the story:
During
Meeting the Asura
his character model had green skin and white foliage.
During
The Lost Shores
he is seen having a mixture of various pieces of the sylvari
Cultural armor
and wielding a
Pirate Cutlass
and
Kryta's Embrace
.
After
Last Stand at Southsun
his character model changes to his current appearance and is wearing
Oaken armor
(without the helmet) and a pair of
Heavy Fused Gauntlets
.
During
Living World Season 2
, Canach loses the gauntlets and his night appearance glow changes from orange to green. (Historically his glow remained orange at this point until the End of Dragons update)
During
Guild Wars 2: Heart of Thorns
, Canach wields a
Chain-Whip Sword
and a
Shield of the Moon
.
Past
Living World Season 3
, Canach wears the
Verdant Executor Outfit
.
In
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, he wears the
Exemplar Attire Outfit
, and his glow has changed from green to yellow/green. This glow change also affect his previous appearances.
In
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
, he wears the
Verdant Executor Outfit
and wields a
New Kaineng Sword
and a
Shield of the Moon
in combat, while wearing the
Exemplar Attire Outfit
as casual attire.
Trivia
Canach was originally
voiced
by
John DiMaggio
. Since the release of
Champions
he is voiced by
Matthew Mercer
.
See also
Blighted Canach
Canach's Story: An After-Hours Meeting

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Captain Ellen Kiel
Race
Human
Profession
Warrior
Guardian
Locations
Captain Kiel's Office
(
Commodore's Quarter
)
Eastern Ward
(
Memory of Old Lion's Arch
)
Bazaar Docks
(
Labyrinthine Cliffs
)
Organization
Captain's Council
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Ellen Kiel as a child.
Inspector Ellen Kiel in Lionguard armor.
Ellen Kiel in her Southsun gear.
Ellen Kiel projection after winning the election.
Ellen Kiel during
Festival of the Four Winds
.
“
We can stop this together. Fight for your homes, for your future. Fight because it's the right thing to do.
—
Captain Ellen Kiel
Captain Ellen Kiel
is the newest member of the
Captain's Council
after winning the
election
against her opponent
Evon Gnashblade
. She helms the
Phoenix Dawn
. Cunning but honest, Ellen does everything in her power to keep
Lion's Arch
safe from harm.
Biography
Early years
Ellen Kiel was orphaned as a young girl by a shipwreck, and only
escaped with her life
thanks to the actions of
Captain Magnus Irondawn
. When she grew older, she joined Captain Irondawn's
Lionguard
, the defenders of
Lion's Arch
against the
Risen
menace and eventually rose to the rank of Inspector. Her role as Inspector was to back up Captain Irondawn, protecting him against threats.
Living World Season 1
The Lost Shores
After the defeat of
Zhaitan
in 1326
AE
, the
Consortium
discovered the island of
Southsun Cove
and provoked the territorial
karka
to attack Lion's Arch. Ellen Kiel led the investigation into the attacks and the
counterassault
against the karka, and was instrumental in the
defeat
of the
Ancient Karka
.
The Secret of Southsun
and
Last Stand at Southsun
Ellen remained on the island to aid with research and oversee the arrival of refugees from the
Molten Alliance
raids. When crazed wildlife started attacking the new Southsun settlements, she was able to identify the former Consortium employee
Canach
as the instigator, and ensured that he was brought to justice with the
Pact Commander
's help. As a follow-up, she managed to
destroy the contracts
that had the refugees indentured to the Consortium, putting an end to the civil unrest on the island.
Sky Pirates
For her work on Southsun Cove, Ellen was given time off and put in charge of security at the 1326
AE
Dragon Bash
. After the effigy lighting ceremony was
attacked
by
Aetherblade
pirates and
Captain Theo Ashford
was killed, Ellen opened an investigation on the pirates to discover their hideout. With the help of the investigator
Marjory Delaqua
, she was able to locate the
Aetherblade Retreat
where the Aetherblades' apparent leader,
Captain Mai Trin
, was apprehended. Mai Trin had intended to exploit the seat on the Captain's Council opened by Captain Ashford's death to give the Aetherblades some political power in Lion's Arch, but her plan failed.
During capture, Mai Trin accidentally let the name Scarlet escape from her lips, making Ellen realize that someone else was behind the Aetherblades' operations. This later proved to be the case when the enigmatic
Scarlet Briar
became known as the mastermind behind not only the Aetherblades but also Molten Alliance and
Toxic Alliance
activities throughout
Tyria
.
Cutthroat Politics
With the Captain's Council in need of a new member after Theo Ashford's murder, Magnus
put Ellen's name forward as a contender
in the upcoming council election. He also commandeered a captured Aetherblade
airship
for Ellen, thus making her eligible. Ellen was able to gain the support of the council by securing a trade agreement with the
Zephyr Sanctum
, winning out over
Evon Gnashblade
of the
Black Lion Trading Company
. Her replacement on the Aetherblade investigation became
Deputy Turma
.
Fractured
As the newest member of the Captain's Council, Ellen kept her promise to the asuran members of the council to investigate the
Thaumanova Reactor
disaster. She took note of the Consortium's involvement with the
Fractals of the Mists
and sent a team to learn more about the fractals. She collaborated with the asuran researcher
Dessa
and sent a team including the Pact Commander into a fractal depicting events leading to the Thaumanova Reactor disaster which revealed that Scarlet Briar had meddled with the
Inquest
at Thaumanova in the past. Believing Dessa knew more, Ellen
ordered her to travel to Lion's Arch
to answer some questions, only to witness Dessa not only being unable to leave the fractal observatory but also the asura losing her memories as well. Ellen began suspecting Dessa's true nature as a fractal anomaly but chose to leave the matter be for the time being.
Battle for Lion's Arch
Scarlet Briar broke out Mai Trin from Lionguard prison in 1327
AE
, and Ellen eventually received reports from the Pact Commander's party about Scarlet's potential next target being Lion's Arch. Although Ellen tried to convince the rest of the Captain's Council to take heed of the Commander's warning, she was ignored. The council's decision cost Lion's Arch dearly when Scarlet Briar's
alliances
combined their forces and
assaulted the city
with both armies and a toxic
miasma
.
Despite losing many lives and the city to the invaders, Ellen remained adamant and began rallying forces to reclaim Lion's Arch from Scarlet's army. She had Evon
conscripted
into the
Lionguard
under General Emergency Statute 14 and forced him, along with all other Black Lion Trading Company merchants, to act as a deputy quartermaster for the Lionguard under penalty of death.
Ellen was joined by surviving Lionguard as well as other defenders of Tyria who assembled a strike force. Curiously enough, her main assistant among the many heroes and soldiers was a
golem
designated
Heal-o-Tron
. They returned to the city when a gust of wind blew the miasma, which Scarlet had unleashed earlier, to the sea. Determined, the strike force
fought their way into the city
and aboard Scarlet's flagship,
the Breachmaker
, where the Pact Commander ultimately
delivered the killing blow
on Scarlet. However, Scarlet succeeded even in death as her flagship managed to redirect the
ley lines
underneath Lion's Arch west towards the
Heart of Maguuma
, resulting in a loud roar of an
Elder Dragon
being heard in the aftermath.
Following their victory, the Lionguard drafted paid workforce and volunteers to help rebuild Lion's Arch. Ellen Kiel was among the representatives of Lion's Arch, alongside the Pact Commander's party, who
welcomed
the visiting
Zephyrites
to the city. She spoke with the
Master of Peace
who assured her that the trade agreement remained intact and promised to donate supplies to the rebuilding. During this time Ellen and Magnus made Ellen's earlier golem ally, now known as Hero-Tron, a free citizen of Lion's Arch and recruited it to aid in the reconstruction effort via gathering donations and helping citizens get back to work after the golem's helpful actions during Scarlet's initial invasion and battle for Lion's Arch.
Living World Season 2
By the time Lion's Arch was rebuilt with the charity proceedings by
Queen Jennah
of
Kryta
as well as the sponsoring of Evon Gnashblade and the Consortium, Ellen became busy with administrative matters and was unable to oversee the retrofit of the airship
Havoc's Heir
. She voiced her discontent to Magnus about rampant black market trading and smuggling and asked for customs inspectors at the docks.
She eventually named her repurposed airship the
Phoenix Dawn
.
Path of Fire
By the time news of the
god
Balthazar
's return had spread throughout Tyria in 1330
AE
, Ellen requested Sheriff Turma to return to Lion's Arch to help with the sudden influx of refugees from the
Crystal Desert
which Balthazar's
Forged
army was ravaging. She
met with the Pact Commander
at her new office and offered to have the Commander flown to the
Free City of Amnoon
aboard the
Phoenix Dawn
.
Ellen also instructed her
assistant
to deliver food, medicine and clothes to the refugees. Although she did not approve of Balthazar's violent actions against the population of the Crystal Desert, she admitted not knowing why the God of War was acting so recklessly after his surprising return. Ellen nevertheless trusted that the Commander would discover more about the god's motives and reason for returning to Tyria after millennia of absence. She and Magnus acted behind the backs of the other councillors to deliver aid to Amnoon, choosing to confront them about it later than ask their permission that could otherwise delay much needed action on behalf of the needy.
After she had sorted everything out at home, Ellen eventually arrived in Amnoon as well as the head of the Lion's Arch trade delegation to provide humanitarian support to the struggling city. Following Balthazar's demise in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
, Ellen was among the crowd welcoming the Commander back to Amnoon to
celebrate the victory
.
While waiting for the Commander to give a speech, she conversed with the notable Amnoonian businessman
Zalambur
and flirted with him while proposing a continuous trade agreement that could benefit both city-states. Zalambur suggested that he and Ellen should continue the discussions in private, leading to a beginning of what could be a mutually beneficial friendship or more. However, the celebration came to a sudden halt when a
Brandstorm
conjured by the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
hit Amnoon.
Living World Season 4
Although the initial Brandstorm was over quickly, another storm hit Amnoon soon after and brought with it a horde of
Branded
. Ellen, notable Amnoon officials and the Pact Commander's allies from
Kryta
evacuated a number of civilians via airship while the Commander, local defenders and the
dragon
Aurene
fought off the invaders.
Upon returning home, Ellen found herself busy with politics again. The Captain's Council and Queen Jennah collaborated in bringing back the
Festival of the Four Winds
in 1331
AE
to provide supplies and funds for the displaced Zephyrites who had once aided Lion's Arch in its time of need. Learning of the council's plans, Evon pulled the supplies together and accompanied Ellen to the Labyrinthine Cliffs where they welcomed visitors and also talked with one another. Evon refused to take part in the festivities himself, instead offering sound economic advice to Ellen about how to handle the trade renegotiations with the Zephyrites to make the percentage better for Lion's Arch.
During their conversations Ellen revealed that she had not forced Evon to aid the Lionguard out of spite during Scarlet's invasion of Lion's Arch but to keep the Black Lion Trading Company in business after the Captain's Council had considered breaking up the company because of Evon sabotaging the asura gate which had left many civilians stranded in the invaded city. Surprised, Evon questioned whether Ellen saving his company had been an altruistic act or for more selfish reasons, but he was eventually satisfied by her answer where the financial well-being of Lion's Arch was tied to how well Evon's company was faring. Evon let Ellen know in return that he knew about her affair with Zalambur. Although he refused to let her know how he had learned of the affair, he voiced his concerns over whether the relationship would affect their business arrangements, only to hear Ellen reassure him that everything would proceed as usual.
End of Dragons

Captain Ellen Kiel/dialogue
Lion's Arch
Getting myself elected to this city council has turned out to be a "careful what you wish for" scenario.
How so?
I'd hoped we could start fresh with the recent rebuilding, but already some of our
city
's less savory legacies are popping back up.
Like what?
Since being driven from their caves to the northwest, our resident
black
marketeers
relocated
near the docks
to sell off "misplaced" incoming shipment.
Why not just haul them in?
Even though we risk the appearance of sanctioning their activities, allowing the
pirates
to ply their trade in public lets the Lionguard keep tabs on them.
I guess that makes sense.
You can all be proud of how Lion's Arch rebuild turned out, at least.
Good luck dealing with that.
You can all be proud of how Lion's Arch rebuild turned out, at least.
Sorry to hear that. Good luck.
You can all be proud of how Lion's Arch rebuild turned out, at least.
Glad to hear you think so. I just wish I could get some time to enjoy it — maybe stroll by the
frog pond
or all those new fountains.
Sounds like you've already got your hands full.
(same as "How so?" above)
With
The Dreamer III: The Lover
active
What goals do you still have for Lion's Arch?
I'd love to see Lion's Arch free of crime and full of opportunities for everybody who comes here.
(Adds
Kiel's Dreams for Lion's Arch
to your collection)
I hope you have the chance to realize that dream.
Hang in there.
Sorry to hear that.
Memory of Old Lion's Arch
I hope you're enjoying your time visiting our fair port city. As a newly elected member of the Captain's Council, I welcome you to Lion's Arch.
Thanks. At least this place is friendlier than the
Thaumanova Reactor
.
It's a place that offers more questions than answers.
Dessa's
...situation needs more thought, but the
Consortium
is the important matter here.
What's the next step?
I don't think Dessa's working for them, but I'm not sure of the Consortium's connection to the
Fractals
. I'm not done investigating them. Not at all.
Take care.
(If you did not vote)
There was an election? Oh, congratulations, I guess.
Er...thank you. Please enjoy yourself, but not too much. As a former Lionguard, I assure you that our brig always has room for one more.
Got it. Thanks.
(If you voted for Ellen)
Thanks, Kiel. Glad to see my vote helped you win the seat.
And I thank you for your support. I regret that my getting this job was due to Theo Ashford being murdered by Scarlet's goons, but I promise I'll so my best to keep this city safe.
Good to hear. Take care.
(If you voted for Gnashblade)
Huh. I thought a Captain's Councillor had better things to do than be a seasonal greeter. Glad I voted for Evon.
Just because you voted for him doesn't mean I'm turning my back on what you want. That may be how Gnashblade runs things, but not me.
Yeah, we'll see.
(If you voted for both)
Good job on winning the election. Although, in all fairness, I also voted for both you and Evon.
I know the election rules were...porous. It's your choice to vote for both of us, but doing so shows a lack of conviction.
Maybe, but I know someone I voted for won.
Good-bye.
The Battle For Lion's Arch
When we clear the path, go make Scarlet suffer for what she did to Lion's Arch. Ashford's murder, the destruction, everything.
You're not coming with?
I won't lie; I'd love to chop Scarlet's crazy head off of her shoulders, but the city isn't secure. I'll stay behind, make sure nothing follows behind you.
Thanks, Captain. Scarlet's not getting away this time.
Will do.

Notes
Ellen Kiel was first introduced as "Inspector Ellen Kiel" with
The Lost Shores
.
Trivia
Ellen Kiel is
voiced
by
Courtenay Taylor
.
Ellen Kiel's character design originated as a male named
Elim Kiel
, and was changed due to voice actor availability.
Kiel's Outpost
on
Southsun Cove
is named after her.

Captain Emund
is a
norn
member of the
Astral Ward
found at the
Skyward Marches
fractal
. He is later promoted to major and assigned a team of volunteers heading into
Inner Nayos
to take the fight to the
Kryptis
.
In
Starlit Weald
, he serves as a
Renown Heart NPC
(as Major Emund).

======================================================================
PAGE: Captain Fa
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Captain_Fa
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
In this
Canthan
name, the
family name
is Fa.
Captain Fa
Race
Human
Profession
Berserker
Rank
Champion
Location
Dragon's End
(
Cantha
)
Organization
Xunlai Jade
“
There is nothing belligerent about my position. I am enacting my employer's orders, which fall within her legal rights.
—
Captain Fa
Captain Fa Yang
is the head of
Xunlai Jade
Security. A stern rationalist, he follows orders diligently but is willing to consider logical arguments from his opposition.
Biography
Early years
Fa Yang worked as a dockmaster in
New Kaineng City
's
harbour
for twenty years before joining the
Xunlai Jade
corportation in 1837
CC
(1327
AE
).
Yu Joon
noted that Fa's loyalty to her corporation was unequaled among stuff, and he had even volunteered to head the Xunlai summer picnic committee. Fa appears to share an old bond with Joon, to whom he is known as "uncle Fa," and affectionately addresses her as "Joon-ee", though she discourages use of such familial terms during work.
Working diligently for Xunlai Jade as the Head of Security, Captain Fa often butted heads with
Detective Rama
who both viewed Fa as a corporate stooge and wanted to knock his teeth in after their many disagreements. Despite his position as a company man, Fa earned a reputation of being a pragmatic rationalist. He respected figures of authority, showing courtesy to people in
Empress Ihn
's court such as the advisor
Navan
.
End of Dragons
Act 2
When the enraged
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
broke free
from Xunlai Jade's damaged
Yong Reactor
under unclear circumstances in 1845
CC
(1335
AE
), Fa used radio to introduce himself through speakers and ordered anyone who was still inside to surrender or face the consequences once Fa and his unit entered the facility. However, when he got inside, he found no one to arrest.
Act 3
Following the reactor's destruction, the upset Joon ordered Fa to find and arrest the
Pact Commander
for questioning as the Commander had been the last person seen entering the facility before the fallout. Fa eventually
located
the Commander in the presence of both Navan and Rama at the
Nika's Blade
restaurant and informed them of Joon's demands. Navan tried to argue in the Commander's defense, pointing out that they had just been discussing a civil resolution to the crisis. While being respectful of Navan, Fa reminded her that he had to carry out Joon's orders and argued with Rama about whether he had legal right to arrest the Commander despite not being a member of the
Ministry of Security
. He also denied the Commander's accusation of Xunlai Jade holding an Elder Dragon in the research facility.
Navan pleaded Fa to hear the Commander out before deciding on a course of action. Fa relented and, depending on the Commander's persuasiveness, was either swayed to let the Commander find the real culprit
Ankka
thanks to logical arguments offered during the debate, or tried to bring the Commander back with force. Either way, the Commander managed to evade capture, and Fa returned to Joon empty-handed. He sent Joon a report on his failure and told her that he had turned his attention to the efforts to contain the rampaging Soo-Won in
the Jade Sea
. Fa also asked Joon to be careful as he worried that the outsiders might be playing them just like Joon's former employee
Mai Trin
had turned out to be untrustworthy.
Act 5
After the Commander had successfully cleared their name by
presenting Joon with evidence
of Ankka's involvement in the destruction of the facility and Soo-Won's release, Joon told Fa to keep assisting the Commander's
Dragon's Watch
guild
in any way he could and help contain the maddened Elder Dragon and the
Void
horde following in Soo-Won's trail. Upon arriving on the scene, Fa met with Navan and the Commander's
sylvari
ally
Caithe
and learned much to his horror that they had recruited the notorious gangs known as the
Jade Brotherhood
and the
Speakers
to bolster their forces against the encroaching Void. Fa objected to the sudden alliance, reminding the two women that the gangs were self-serving and violent and could not be trusted. However, he was eventually convinced to lend his aid as one of the generals of the allied forces once he found out that they would also have the benevolent Elder Dragon
Aurene
fighting by their side to save
Cantha
and the
world
from destruction.
The Canthan armies and their unlikely allies
fought against the Void
horde across
Dragon's End
, ultimately reaching the
Harvest Temple
where they confronted the Void-influenced Soo-Won. After a
fierce clash
, they were able to weaken the Water Dragon enough for the Commander's party to attempt to drain the Void from Soo-Won and purify her. However, once the corrupted
magic
was out, it coalesced to form its own being—
the Dragonvoid
—and attacked the allied forces. The
apocalypse
was averted when Joon and the
asura
Taimi
's devices weakened the Void, the Commander and Aurene joining forces to defeat the Dragonvoid, and Fa and the armies of the Jade Sea bought them the time they needed by battling the invading the Void horde around the temple. Once the battle was over and Soo-Won's magic had been transferred to Aurene upon her death, Fa and the others attended the
victory celebration
at
Arborstone
and raised a toast to Aurene who had become the One Elder Dragon.
Location
Cantha
Dragon's End
Outer Terrace
Quarry Passage
(before
The Battle for the Jade Sea
)
Echovald Fringe
(only during related events)
Jade Promenade
(only during related events)
Harvest Complex
(only during related events)

(only during related events)

Trivia
Captain Fa is
voiced
by
Paul Nakauchi
.
Designer
Matthew Medina
wanted more representation for older men in
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
and suggested the aged look and receding hairstyle for Fa which character designers implemented.
Medina has suggested that Fa may have once been a police officer in his younger days before going into private security.

======================================================================
PAGE: Captain Magnus the Bloody-Handed
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Captain_Magnus_the_Bloody-Handed
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
,
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
, and
Living World Season 4
.
Captain Magnus the Bloody-Handed
Race
Norn
Profession
Warrior
Rank
Champion
Location
Western Ward
(
Lion's Arch
)
Organization
Captain's Council
Image(s)
Magnus with both eyes
Interactive map
“
Today's a good day to be afraid. You have tomorrow and the rest of your life to be brave.
—
Magnus the Bloody-Handed
Captain Magnus "the Bloody-Handed" Irondawn
is the
norn
captain of the
Cormorant
, and the current leader of the
Lionguard
. A member of the
Captain's Council
, he is responsible for protecting the merchants and enforcing the law in
Lion's Arch
. Before his current post, he was an adventurer who gained great fame for himself. Magnus
lost his eye
during the
Battle for Lion's Arch
.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Magnus
saves
young
Ellen Kiel
.
In the past, Magnus
came across
a shipwreck where he discovered the young
human
survivor,
Ellen Kiel
. He brought Ellen to
Lion's Arch
where she would grow up and eventually join the ranks of the
Lionguard
with him as her mentor.
Edge of Destiny
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
In 1319
AE
, Magnus was indirectly responsible for the formation of
Destiny's Edge
, and traveled with the
guild
to defeat
Morgus Lethe
, his former captain and a
lieutenant
of
Zhaitan
.
Living World Season 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography with ambient dialogue lore reveals from Season 1.
In 1325
AE
, Magnus led the
defense
of Lion's Arch against the
karka
, eventually invading
Southsun Cove
alongside the Lionguard to
defeat
the
Ancient Karka
which had been leading the karka attacks.
Following the rogue ex-
Consortium
member
Canach
's attempt to rile the karka and local wildlife to attack the Consortium, Ellen Kiel was sent to arrest him. Ellen, realizing that Canach had only wanted to free the settlers from the binding Consortium contracts, subtly helped him by
rigging
the Consortium ship carrying the deeds with explosives. By the time Magnus arrived at the scene, he lamented having to deal with Consortium complaints although he was happy that Canach was in custody, the Consortium had been humbled, and the settlers were happy. He also ordered Ellen to take a few days off and enjoy herself as he did not want to see her working all the time.
In 1326
AE
, Magnus helped organize the
Dragon Bash
festival in Lion's Arch to celebrate
Tyrians
' resistance against the growing threat of the
Elder Dragons
. He hosted the opening ceremony while welcoming cultural representatives from various races to take part in the festivities. However,
Captain Theo Ashford
was
assassinated
during the effigy lighting ceremony which also claimed other lives. Furious at the deaths, Magnus wanted to find the culprit responsible for ruining the festival.
Prompted into action by the enigmatic spymaster
E
,
Marjory Delaqua
and the
Pact Commander
assisted Inspector Ellen Kiel in investigating the crime scene and interrogating witnesses. They eventually
found out
that the culprit was the seemingly benevolent onlooker
Mai Trin
who was revealed as the leader of cutthroat pirates known as the
Aetherblades
. However, they were unable to apprehend the exposed Mai who unleashed her minions on the party while escaping in the ensuing chaos. The Pact Commander and Ellen's Lionguard force eventually tracked down Mai Trin to the
Aetherblade Retreat
and captured her for interrogation.
With the Captain's Council in need of a new member after Theo Ashford's murder, Magnus
put Ellen's name forward as a contender
in the upcoming council election. He also commandeered a captured Aetherblade
airship
for Ellen, thus making her eligible. Ellen was able to gain the support of the council by securing a trade agreement with the
Zephyr Sanctum
, winning out over
Evon Gnashblade
of the
Black Lion Trading Company
. Her replacement on the Aetherblade investigation became
Deputy Turma
.
In 1327
AE
, Magnus ignored warnings from Ellen and the Pact Commander about the
sylvari
terrorist
Scarlet Briar
intending to assault Lion's Arch with her
alliances
even after he had been made aware that Scarlet had been behind Mai Trin's actions. Magnus and the council defended their stance by stating that since the Aetherblades had already assaulted Lion's Arch and failed, they would not dare attack the city again even with more allies by their side.
Magnus soon realized that he had been wrong about Scarlet's tenacity, however, when her alliance assaulted the city with the combination of a mixed army and a devastating
miasma
. The
attack
led to many casualties as citizens of Lion's Arch either died to the invaders or the sickness that Scarlet was spreading.
Magnus, Ellen and the remaining Lionguard organized a counterattack alongside several adventurers to reclaim their lost city from Scarlet's forces. In the process of attempting to hold
Fort Marriner
, Magnus
lost one of his eyes
fighting a
Molten Berserker
, and was evacuated aboard Ellen's airship. He eventually learned that the Pact Commander had
delivered the killing blow
on Scarlet and congratulated the Commander for the deed as he began the lengthy rebuilding process in the ruined Lion's Arch.
Despite Lionguard ranks being thinned by Scarlet's assault, Magnus had to deal with an increasing number of smugglers and black market traders. Ellen kept reminding him to find a way to handle the smugglers at the port, and Magnus promised to do his best to settle the issue.
Path of Fire
In 1330
AE
, a growing number of refugees from
Elona
appeared on Lion's Arch aboard ships. Magnus learned that they had been fleeing the destruction caused by the former
god
Balthazar
's
Forged
invasion, and he tried to figure out where to shelter the refugees until the rest of the council would be made aware of the existence of the refugees and what to do with them.
Magnus
welcomed
the Pact Commander's assistance and wished them luck in the campaign against Balthazar. He also invited Sheriff Turma back to Lion's Arch to help with the refugee crisis. Turma, however, found out that Magnus and Ellen Kiel were not working with the full council, and she suspected the pair had their reasons to act behind the council's back although she could not be certain if those reasons were noble or selfish.
Living World Season 4
Magnus was seen
defending
various locations in
Gendarran Fields
from
Palawa Joko
's
Awakened
attackers alongside members of the
Lionguard
in 1330
AE
. He prepared the defenses of Lion's Arch in case Joko attempted to open portals in the city, and assured worried onlookers that Lion's Arch would not fall to the
lich
king's army.

Dialogue
For previous dialogue, see
here
.
The Battle For Lion's Arch
Now this is a fight. I've spent too long in
council
meetings. Feels good...no, it feels right to raise a ruckus once in a while. Especially when it comes to your city.
How's the fight going?
We're gaining ground, but Scarlet's still alive on that
drilling ship
. Until we gut her, we're going to be in a stalemate.
How can I turn the tide?
Board Scarlet's airship somehow and end that crazy plant. How you do it...well, that's up to you. If it were me, I'd use a rusty blade and a low flame.
Understood.
Take care.
What can I do to help?
Board Scarlet's airship somehow and end that crazy plant. How you do it...well, that's up to you. If it were me, I'd use a rusty blade and a low flame.
How's the fight going?
We're gaining ground, but Scarlet's still alive on that drilling ship. Until we gut her, we're going to be in a stalemate.
Understood.
Take care.

Captain Rahim
Race
Human
Locations

(

)
The Grotto

)
Organization
Cavaliers
Captain Rahim
is the captain of the
Cavaliers
in the
Free City of Amnoon
. He is stationed at the
Amnoon Cavalier Station
.
Biography
Stub. Key elements of this npc article are incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Sometime before 1330 AE, Rahim ascended to the position of captain of the Cavaliers. Captain Rahim met the diplomatic team from
Divinity's Reach
prior to meeting the
Pact Commander
outside the northern gate of the Free city of Amnoon in 1330 AE. He was pleased to meet the famous Dragon Slayer and gave them permission to enter the city.
He talked with the Pact Commander again at Amnoon Cavalier Station. He pointed to three people, Deputy Qais, Deputy Ayoub and Chief Councilor Imann as potential sources of information on the location of Balthazar.
Captain Rahim attended the celebration of Balthazar's defeat, where the commander thanked him for his advice. He witnessed the
Brandstorm
over the sea and knew that
Kralkatorrik
had come to Elona.
