Jituya Itemo
Captain Theo Ashford
was a member of the
Ship's Council
and the
Ascalonian Ashfords
, a family of cut-throats. He was an old family friend of
Logan Thackeray
's. Ashford was assassinated by
Aetherblades
during the Dragon Bash effigy lighting ceremony as part of a plot to install
Captain Mai Trin
onto the Captain's Council, but this plan was foiled by
Inspector Ellen Kiel
, who would later take Ashford's place on the Council.

Dialogue
Before Dragon Bash
Welcome to the greatest city in the world, me hearty.
Quite a boaster, mister. Can you back it up?
I'm Captain Theo Ashford...formally of the Ascalon Ashfords. We bring money, prestige, and charisma everywhere we go. And now I'm here!
Keep up the good work, Captain.
Thanks, uh...matey.
During Dragon Bash
You look like someone who enjoys a party. You've come to the right place. It's Dragon Bash time, and I know everything happening in this city.
Tell me about Dragon Bash.
We were inspired by the old Dragon Festival, but made it a real party. We've got attractions and distractions, but the effigies take the cake. Well, if you can make a cake out of a massive dragon statue, that is.
Tell me about the effigies.
We're building giant dragon statues, all so we can wreck them in a big ceremony. And there'll be effigies around the world for you to bash too. Be on the lookout for them.
And you are?
Captain Theo Ashford, of the Ascalonian Ashfords, captain on the Ship's Council. Between you and me, it's about time the dullards on the council threw a party like this.
Why are you out here?
You're joking, right? I'm the life of this party. Who better to tell the people about Dragon Bash?
Take care.
Good-bye.
Thanks for the information.
Thanks for the information.
Good-bye.
Notes

Molten Alliance
, so Ashford's death triggered the
Captain's Council Election
.
Ashford
was the name of an
Ascalonian
town, and the birthplace of
Gwen Thackeray
.
He had a husband,
Quinn Ashford
, and a son,
Finnian Ashford
.

November 15th, 2012
update.

Episode 5:
Champions
,
Chapter 1: Truce
Primordus Rising
Dialogue
I see you're admiring my life's work. I don't blame you; they're beautiful vessels. Captain Trixx at your service.
It must be exciting being a ship captain.
On one occasion, we ran out of water for three days. I started seeing
pink moas
everywhere. Delirious, we wrecked on a small island off
Bloodtide Coast
. Things got even more bizarre there.
How so?
It turned out the island was actually covered with pink moas. Even more alarming, we discovered a
pirate
ship closing in on the beach where we went aground.
How did you escape?
I will simply say that, in the end, we learned how to efficiently herd wild
moas
, and the pirates learned that a flock of moas loose on your ship can be problematic.
Brilliant.
Assuredly so. I recall a particular moment when one of my
golems
fell off the ship. As we were fishing it out, a
shark
latched onto its leg, and then a second shark grabbed the first one's tail.
So, did you rescue the golem
Of course, although it took two days to get all three of them on the ship. The sharks wouldn't let go, and I wasn't about to leave one of my crew behind. I can personally attest to the immensity of a shark's weight.
That's quite a story.
That's quite a story.
Nice to meet you. I'll be going now.
Notes
Captain Trixx has a random chance of telling two different stories upon interacting in dialogue.

Verdant Warhammer
, meaning she is either a
guardian
or a
warrior
as other professions only unlocked the hammer with elite specializations, none of which were available in the personal story.
Carys will appear in the player's
home instance
after completing
The Steel Tide
and
Victory or Death
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Castaway Westergard
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Castaway_Westergard
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Castaway Westergard
Race
Norn
Level
80
Location
Pilgrim's Rest
(
Eternity's Garden
)
Organization
Hullgardeners
“
Westergard threaten to throw you back into the bay? (laughs) Yeah, she does that. And she means it. We all do.
—
Mayor Osborne
in
Solid Ground
Castaway Westergard
is a
norn
Hullgardener
. She is a no-nonsense woman who is protective of her fellow castaways and is suspicious of newcomers. She does not hold the
Inquest
or the
Freebooters
in high regard.
Biography
Early years
While being stranded on
Castora
, Westergard made herself useful and became one of
Mayor Osborne
's most trusted allies among the
Hullgardeners
. Using her
norn
strength and her no-nonsense attitude, she had the grit to protect her fellow castaways from trouble coming from within and without their community while dealing with the ever-increasing threat posed by
Captain Jessica Hammerhart
's
Freebooters
. She found an old
Orrian
journal at some point, speculating with
Captain Bidelia
about how it had ended up on Castora. Westergard came to believe that the journal might have been written by Orrian castaways from long ago rather than washing up on shore from the effects of the
Rising of Orr
a century prior, which made her speculate how many groups of castaways had actually existed on Castora before the Hullgardeners.
Westergard's finding also sparked an interest among Hullgardeners to begin documenting their history for future generations.
Visions of Eternity
Act 1
Hullgardeners witnessed two groups of newcomers arriving on Castoran shores in 1338
AE
. The first of these were the
Inquest
led by
Director Vloxx
whose fleet had suffered major damage upon reaching
Shipwreck Strand
. Vloxx tried to charm both the Freebooters and Hullgardeners to work as his muscle by offering them with some of the Inquest technology in exchange for services although Mayor Osborne refused an outright alliance out of suspicion of the newcomers' motives. The Hullgardeners would later learn that the Inquest had not only
augmented
local wildlife with cybernetic parts and turned the creatures more ferocious but had also provided similar technology to the Freebooters who were now using the same technology to harass the Hullgardeners.
The second group of newcomers arrived two weeks after the Inquest, having also suffered damage to their vessels on the way to Castora. After the Inquest had upset the balance of power between local factions and had disturbed natural harmony in the region, Westergard
approached
this latest group with suspicion, preparing to deal with them accordingly if they turned out to be yet another threat to her community. She overheard
Sayida the Sly
, who had led the group to Castora, talk to someone identified as the
Commander
, and initially thought that this Commander was the actual leader of these castaways, so she introduced herself to the Commander. Upon realizing that Westergard did not know who the Commander was, Sayida inquired how long the norn had been stranded on Castora, but Westergard deigned not to answer while voicing out her frustrations that the Inquest's weapons were being used by their enemies. Sayida continued attempting to sweet-talk Westergard and pry her for information, but the norn did not fall for the captain's words. She insisted that only the Commander would be initially allowed to visit
Hullgarden
and speak with Mayor Osborne so the Hullgardeners would figure out if the newcomers could be trusted or should be shoved back into the bay.
As days passed, Westergard and other Hullgardeners noticed how the Commander, Sayida and their allies were trustworthy enough as they fought against local threats while helping the castaway community with everyday chores. When Jessica Hammerhart and her brother,
First Mate Jimmy Hammerhart
, began
causing ruckus
with the devices which they had bought from the Inquest, Sayida gave Westergard a communicator and insisted that the norn woman should reach out to the Commander and ask for their help against the emboldened Freebooters. Although Westergard's plea for help was forced at best, she was nevertheless surprised when she learned that the Commander and other adventurers had successfully defeated the Hammerhart twins and thus diminished the Freebooters' grip over the region.
The Only Way

Osbourne
needs to head back to the
village

Trivia
Castaway Westergard is
voiced
by
Sharon Muthu
.
Westergard is an
anglicized
version of the
Danish
and
Dutch
Westergaard
and
Swedish
Westergård.
'Wester'
means 'west,'
'gaard'
means 'garden, yard,' and
'gård'
means 'farm, yard' in English.

Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity launch trailer
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Castor
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Castor
======================================================================

This article is about the
Edge of Destiny
character.  For the
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
region, see
Castora
.
Castor
was a
human
mercenary working under
Logan Thackeray
in
Ascalon
to protect human merchant caravans that supplied
Ebonhawke
. In 1319
AE
, she was in the group that fought with
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
's forces, taken over by
Rytlock Brimstone
, both of whom were then ambushed by
ogres
. She died in the conflict, and her body was burned in a makeshift pyre by Logan afterwards.

PAGE: Centurion Volante Tornpaw

Spoiler alert:

Centurion Volante Tornpaw
Race
Charr
Level
10, 80
Rank
Champion
Location
Lighthouse Point
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Dominion
Image(s)
Appearance in
Drizzlewood Coast
.
Interactive map
“
I don't snivel like a scaredy-cat at the first sign of danger.
—
Centurion Volante Tornpaw
Centurion Volante Tornpaw
is a fierce
centurion
of the
Blood Legion
. Due to this, the local
Flame Legion
has put her on their kill list. She can also be found in
Drizzlewood Coast
, having joined the
Dominion
.
Biography
Early years
Volante Tornpaw, at one point, dated
Maverick
, a soldier with a large ego. As she climbed the ranks to become a
centurion
in the
Blood Legion
, she became a mean officer who could not keep people in her
warband
.
Personal story
In 1325
AE
, Volante was selected for assassination by the
Flame Legion
in an attempt to start a war between the
High Legions
. She was unkind to members of her warband and thus could not keep anyone but
Wroda
, an undercover
Ash Legion
spy, and
Legionnaires
Castor Crushpaw
and
Septima Swiftpaw
.
The
legionnaire
, who had driven off
Ascalonian ghost
forces during the
attack
on
Smokestead
, was made aware of the Flame Legion assassination plot while undercover at a Flame Legion base. The legionnaire
warned
Volante of the attack, allowing her and the Blood Legion soldiers to drive off the Flame Legion. After learning that Wroda was an Ash Legion spy, Volante kicked her out of the warband.
The Icebrood Saga
In 1332
AE
, Volante supported
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
of the Blood Legion as he broke away from the other High Legions during the
All-Legion Rally
. In 1333
AE
, she was tasked to guard a cache in
Drizzlewood Coast
, located in a former
human
settlement
. Despite her efforts, Volante was killed when her cache was
assaulted
by adventurers and the
Pact Commander
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Champion Chief Kronon
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Champion_Chief_Kronon
======================================================================

Champion Chief Kronon
Race
Branded
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Locations
Diviner's Passage
(
Crystal Oasis
)
Maculate Fringe
(
Crystal Oasis
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
A Branded ogre is stalking the dunes. It must be Chief Kronon. Can you handle him?
—
Order of Shadows Agent
Champion Chief Kronon
(1078
AE
— 1330
AE
) is a former
ogre
chief who has been
Branded
by
Kralkatorrik
. He led the
Ogre Revolt
and assaulted the
Stronghold of Ebonhawke
with a Branded army until he was called south to the
Crystal Desert
to join Kralkatorrik's horde. Kronon now roams in the
Crystal Oasis
, fighting anything that is not Branded.
Biography
Edge of Destiny
Kronon was once the chief of the
ogre
tribes near the
Blazeridge Gap
. His son,
Chiefling Ygor
, was killed by
Logan Thackeray
and
Rytlock Brimstone
in 1319
AE
, and Kronon swore to avenge Ygor's death.
At the time Kronon was 240 years old, old enough to have buried many sons. He had been born the very year that
the Great Destroyer
,
champion
of the
Elder Dragon
Primordus
, had awakened. His great-great-grandsire had been born in the year that
magic
had come into the
world
. The greatest of his grandsires had been born before there were any
humans
.
In 1320
AE
, Kronon, his ogre warriors and their
hyenas
had penetrated deep into southwest
Ascalon
, only half a day's march from
Ebonhawke
which they intended to invade in retaliation for Ygor's death, when the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
awoke and flew overhead. Kronon and his army transformed into
Branded
by the Elder Dragon's breath, their muscles turned to crystals and their bones to stone.
The Branded chief followed his new master south, to Ebonhawke, where the ogres steamrolled the
Iron Legion
siege and stormed the gates themselves, laying waste to the
Ebon Vanguard
.
Queen Jennah
, who had been visiting Ebonhawke to open the new
asura gate
, summoned her champion
Logan Thackeray
to protect her, tearing him away from the battle with Kralkatorrik.
When Kronon arrived, he let out a roar of incoherent rage that shook the walls of Ebonhawke.
He led the charge in a pitched battle in the courtyard, with the other side led by Logan Thackeray, who had released a century of
charr
from the dungeons in order to aid in the defense. Eventually, Queen Jennah herself summoned a tremendously powerful
illusion
of an Elder Dragon, tricking the ogres into believing their master wanted them elsewhere, and holding the humans and charr transfixed with a
glamour
, so that the ogres would think that they, too, had been transformed into Branded.
Path of Fire
The Branded creature that was once Chief Kronon moved south and began wandering the northern areas of the
Crystal Oasis
, hungering to serve his Elder Dragon master.
In 1330
AE
, the refugees in the
Zephyrite
ruins of
Destiny's Gorge
placed a
champion bounty
on Kronon, which adventurers could pick up from the
Bounty Board
there. Kronon was eventually slain by bounty hunters.

Effects
- Becomes vulnerable after removing Crystalline Armor.
Crystalline Armor
— Damage against the ice beast is reduced by 98%. Normal means of clearing the ice beast's defiance bar will be ineffective.
Enraged
- After losing 50% health.
(unnamed effect)
Skills
Jab
- Melee auto-attack that deals damage and inflicts 8 seconds of
Bleeding
.
Maul
- The beast sweeps at its target, dealing damage, inflicting 8 seconds of
Bleeding
and knocking back the target.
Icy Charge
- The beast charges in the target direction, dealing damage, inflicting 8 seconds of
Bleeding
and launching foes in its path.
Rimebound servants
struck by the charge are destroyed. At the end of the charge, ice starts falling throughout the cave for a period of time.
Falling Ice
- Ice falls on random locations throughout the cave, dealing damage and knocking struck foes down for 3 seconds.
Ice Beam
- The beast fires beams of ice from its horns at its target,
turning them into ice
for 3 seconds.
Enrage
- The beast becomes enrages and stomps the ground in quick succession, causing ice to continually fall throughout the cave.
Darkrime Delves
(unnamed effect)
Skills
Maul
- Melee auto attack, also knocks back
Icy Charge
- Charges target in a line knocking them back.
Ice Shard Burst
- Pounds on the ground creating multiple aoe circles per target which an icy shard appears and then explodes.
Pulverizing Leap
- Jumps on target, knocking them down.
Glacial Gaze
- Fires a beam of ice.
Trivia

is repeated and attributed to Ryland.

Yes, traveler?
Can I ride one of these jackals?
Yes, though keep in mind that while they may look sustantial, they're still constructs of wind and sand. When you interact with one, don't fight its inner nature. Shift with it, like the sand.
Can I keep one? I promise to feed it and sweep up after it.
as below
Can I go through these portals on a jackal?
as below
I understand.
Can I keep one? I promise to feed it and sweep up after it.
The jackals are deadly to inexperienced handlers in the outside world. Train one to prepare it for cleansing, and prove your worth.
Can I ride one of these jackals?
as above
Can I go through these portals on a jackal?
as below
I'm the soul of responsible jackal care.
Can I go through these portals on a jackal?
You can, once you've bonded enough to learn how a jackal maintains momentum as it passes through a rift. A wise master can make great use of the phenomenon.

- The abomination slams its target with its left arm, dealing increased damage.

,
Fury
and
Might
every 10 seconds.
Smash
- The abomination jumps and slams the ground, dealing massive damage and knocking back foes in a large area.

PAGE: Chauncey von Snuffles III

Chauncey von Snuffles III
Race
Feline
Location
Party Politics
(
The Crown Pavilion
,
Plaza of Balthazar
,
Balthazar Low Road
)
Chauncey von Snuffles III
is "the runner-up in the
Divinity's Reach
Fanciest Cat competition,"
having lost to
Lady Wisteria Whiskington
from
Beetletun
. The competition was made for charity, and Chauncey was the crowd favorite. When he lost the crowd rioted against
Lord Faren
who was the announcer of the competition's winner. Though the crowd thought he had some influence in preventing Chauncey from receiving first place, Faren did not and was just as surprised by Chauncey's loss.
Chauncey's fans chased Faren out of the city for a temporary "sabbatical" in
Southsun Cove
.

Living World Season 2
Episode 4:
The Dragon's Reach: Part 1

in the

======================================================================
PAGE: Chief Dekkti
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Chief_Dekkti
======================================================================

Chief Dekkti
Race
Asura
Level
11
Location
Trader's Green
(
Caledon Forest
)
Part of
Seeking the Zalisco
Image(s)
Chief Dekkti during
Seeking the Zalisco
Interactive map
Chief Dekkti
is an
asura
that leads a krewe that primarily studies
skritt
, covering the area between the
Mabon Market
and
Hanto Trading Post
.
Location
Caledon Forest
Hanto Trading Post
Story involvement
Personal story
Seeking the Zalisco
Event involvement
Collect data cores from tracking devices for Apprentice Vee
(12)
Guard the mossheart carrying Widd's research
(11)
Protect the M.A.D. from skritt while Dekkti works
(11)
Dialogue
I was wary of starting a lab at a
tengu
outpost, but I must admit, they can supply some very unique research materials. Easy access to local test subject has also been beneficial.
Test subjects?
We're here to study the skritt. They're usually dumb, beastly little creatures. But in mass gatherings, they seem to exhibit intelligence far beyond that seen in most races. Other than asura, of course.
So, the skritt get smarter in groups.
A single skritt is borderline functional, but the more you bring together, the more alacrity they exhibit. My theory is that annoying sounds they make are really superaural signals for communication.
So, why is here good for your tests?
Well, there's a sizeable "community" of skritt nearby, so there are plenty of subjects. And the tengu manage to procure some rather rare materials for us to experiment with, so it's a worthwhile location.
The tengu don't mind?
Well, they don't let us stay here for free. We may have promised them we'd try to set up an
asura gate
to help their efforts. But only if our skritt research goes well, you see?
Why here, of all places?
(same as "So, why is here good..." above)
What are you hoping to learn.
Wow. Good luck, I guess.
The skritt volunteer for this?
Um, yes. Sure. Mostly. I mean, It's
[
sic
]
not like we kidnap them and bring them here. We're just working on some devices to...encourage them to arrive and participate. It's all part of our ongoing research.
And this location is good for that?
(same as "So, why is here good..." above)
Yeah, that doesn't sound sketchy at all.
I see.
You mean that chirpy buzzing sound?
Yes, yes, that. I hypothesize that it allows them to send and receive information much faster than we can speak and hear, which gives them the illusion of greater intellect. If I can figure out how it works...
Then, what, you could chirp and buzz?
No, that's be useless. But maybe we could learn how to disrupt them, or we could set our golems up to coordinate with each other better, or something. I'm sure I can find some way to profit from this.
And the tengu don't mind helping you?
(same as above)
Yeah, good luck with that.
And they just let you study them like that?
(same as "The skritt volunteer..." above)
That seems a little far-fetched. I'm out of here.
That's...interesting. Good-bye.
Why come here, though?
(same as "So, why is here good..." above)
The tengu don't mind?
(same as above)
Sounds like you're covered. Bye.
And they just let you work here?
(same as "The tengu don't mind?" above)
Yes, yes. Very interesting. Good-bye.
Notes
During the sylvari personal story
Seeking the Zalisco
, Chief Dekkti wears different clothing and has a slightly redder skin tone.

Hylek tribe, situated in the
Togatl Grounds
within
Kessex Hills
. He went into hiding in an underwater cave-system with his people because of the
krait
's invasion.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
In 1337
AE
, Tlatola arrives at the
Guild Initiative Headquarters
to represent the Togatl in the
Tyrian Alliance
. He can be seen speaking with
Kaana Miatli
.

Why are you here! You are not of the Togatl, and you are not welcome here!

Through your deeds, you've become a friend to my people. You will always be welcome within our grounds.
Tell me about the Togatl tribe.
The Togatl are as a part of this area as the lake or the sky. We have lived here for many generations. I never thought I would see the end of us in my lifetime. The krait have dealt us a blow.
Good-bye.
Tell me more about hylek.
Each tribe is unique. Some war. Some don't. You can always count on one thing though. Hylek care most about tribe, food and territory - in that order.

One day, they arrived at the lake from the south and swarmed it, feeding on our fish and killing all predators. There are so many of them. They do not share with other races.

Trivia
Tlatoa is the shortened form of the Classic Nahuatl word Tlahtoa meaning "issuer of proclamations".

Chromatic Cubozoa
Race
Jellyfish
Level
67
Rank
Veteran
Location
Wet Work
(
Treacherous Depths
)
The
Chromatic Cubozoa
is a large
jellyfish
which dwells in the depths of
the Mire Sea
. By 1325
AE
it had earned the attention of the
largos
Sayeh al' Rajihd
who decided to hunt it down to test her skills. While stalking her prey, however, Sayeh ran into
Crusader
Apatia
and the
Pact Commander
who were looking for information about the mysterious
Blue Orb
which the
Pact
was after. Before telling the pair what she knew, the largos challenged them to prove their worth by sending them to kill the jellyfish. Apatia and the Commander faced the Chromatic Cubozoa and defeated the beast. Impressed, Sayeh promised to present the jellyfish's carcass as a trophy to the
Tethyos Houses
in the pair's name.

Cin Fursarai

“
Reams of paper are like snowflakes. I know my pages as well as most people know their children.
—
Cin Fursarai
Cin Fursarai
is a maker of quality paper who makes a living in and around
Divinity's Reach
, though he hails from
Lion's Arch

Kryta
. He is part of the group of nobles who is found stranded in

Logan Thackeray

against the

and manages Seraph supplies around

Dialogue

Get me back to my printing shop in

======================================================================
PAGE: Cinder Steeltemper
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Cinder_Steeltemper
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Cinder Steeltemper
Race
Charr
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Forging Steel
(
Shiverpeak Mountains
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Dominion
Steel Warband
“
Cinder Steeltemper is my second-in-command. Deadly with her swords, and a little too trigger happy with her flamethrower. She loves a good barbecue if you know what I mean.
—
Ryland Steelcatcher
in
Forging Steel
Cinder Steeltemper
is the second-in-command of
Ryland Steelcatcher
's
Steel Warband
in the
Blood Legion
and a member of the
Dominion
. She is proficient with the flamethrower.
Biography
Early years
Cinder Steeltemper was found hidden near the corpses of presumed parents by a
Blood Legion
patrol. After this, she was brought to the
Blood Legion Fahrar
in
Grothmar Valley
where she met
Ryland Steelcatcher
.
The
primus
there used to beat the cubs with a metal rod until Ryland fought back and nearly killed the primus, though Cinder persuaded him to settle for keeping the primus alive but forcing him into retirement.
After graduating from the fahrar, Cinder became Ryland's second-in-command in the
Steel Warband
. They recruited other members such as
Ranoah Grindsteel
,
Vishen Steelshot
, and even the ex-
Renegade
Nicabar Steelweaver
. However, Cinder was disappointed to learn that the warband's first assignment was homeguard posting at Grothmar Valley instead of getting to fight enemies of the
charr
.
As the years passed, the Steel Warband got to participate in various battles, and their fame grew in the Blood Legion. One of their notable battles involved defending the
Brokentooth Maw
outpost from the
Branded
by holding a nearby pass.
Cinder began experimenting on tempering her weapons with primordial slag from
Destroyers
to make them more effective.
She also began developing feelings for Ryland.
The Icebrood Saga
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Cinder attended the
opening ceremony
of the
All-Legion Rally
in 1332
AE
, fighting the
branded devourer
alongside her fellow warband members Ryland and Nicabar shortly after
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
had promoted Ryland as
centurion
. After the
Elder Dragon
Aurene
had flown by and Branded the broodmother, Cinder walked away from the now crystallized creature.
The Steel Warband accompanied Ryland and several belligerent warbands from the
High Legions
and Renegades to
Shiverpeaks Pass
while most of the celebrating crowd remained unaware of what was happening. In a
clandestine meeting
, Bangar revealed his ambitions to subjugate the Elder Dragon
Jormag
as a counter to the growing power of Aurene and the
Pact
and asked the present warbands to assist him so they could elevate the charr to a position of power in
Tyria
. Ryland stood beside Bangar and cheered the gathered audience on. The charr destroyed the bridge behind them to prevent anyone from following them into the pass as they began the long trek north.
Visions of the Past: Steel and Fire
The Steel Warband was ordered to establish a supply line for Bangar's main forces through the countryside leading to northern
Bjora Marches
. However, the cold weather and the first snowstorms took the choppers out of comission, causing many pilots to panic and dump supply crates which scattered throughout the wilderness. While the warband prepared to escort the
TT6-B Devourer
tank and scout the way north, Cinder challenged warband recruits to various combat contests to pass the time.
Before the warband set out from camp, Cinder competed with the recruits as they killed nearby
Sons of Svanir
patrols while she joked about Ryland's privileged position as Bangar's latest favorite. Once the charr began the journey through the hostile northern landscape, she challenged the recruits to more contests which involved killing aggressive
Tar Elementals
and
Darkrime Skelk
. Her enthusiastic use of the flamethrower raised concerns within the warband.
As the warband realized that a new and even more fierce blizzard was headed their way and would make their advance more difficult, Nicabar suggested that they take a shortcut through a
ruined fortress
which had once belonged to the
Stone Summit
dwarves
. As they entered the ruins, however, they discovered much to their surprise that the Stone Summit were still guarding the area although these dwarves did not appear to be alive when they attacked the warband in rage.
After fighting through the ruins, the warband encountered the
Ancient Forgeman
, a dwarven construct which the Stone Summit had been using to repel trespassers. Although Ryland had initially not wanted to shoot the distress flare to summon reinforcements because he wanted to prove to Bangar that he could handle the mission by himself, he relented when he realized what the warband was up against. Despite using the flare, however, reinforcements did not arrive on time, so the warband had no choice but to engage the forgeman and defeated it after a fierce struggle.
By the time the battle had ended, the warband noticed Bangar himself appearing with an entourage to question them why they had not shown up at the rendezvous point. Because the blizzard was getting worse, Bangar ordered Ryland to follow him into
Darkrime Delves
so they could scout an alternate route through the pass. When Cinder suggested that she could send some of the warband members to assist the pair, Ryland told her that the imperator had already given his orders and the rest of the warband should stand by and wait for further instructions. While waiting for Bangar and Ryland's return, Cinder conversed with the rest of the warband and admitted that the recruits' actions had impressed her. The warband eventually united with the rest of Bangar's forces as they headed deeper into Bjora Marches.
Whispers that were originating from Jormag's
champion
Drakkar
began affecting Bangar's army by 1333
AE
, including the Steel Warband. Cinder noted that the warband members had begun eyeing each other suspiciously, and she resolved to end the influence of the whispers.
However, Bangar believed that most of the charr following him were too weak-willed to resist the whispers, so he and Ryland decided to confront Drakkar by themselves while ordering the rest of the army to stay behind.
Following Drakkar's
demise
, the two charr reunited with the rest of the army and continued their journey.
After an encounter with Bangar and Ryland in Bjora Marches, the
Pact Commander
used the
Scrying Pool
in the
Eye of the North
to witness
visions
of the past involving members of the Steel Warband and learned about the warband's struggles to establish a northern supply line for the rogue imperator's forces.
No Quarter
As Bangar established his
Dominion
and rallied more support behind his cause, the Steel Warband assisted him in his campaign. Although the
High Legions
organized into the
United Legions
to face Bangar's forces in combat, the Dominion won many of the battles it fought in while gaining more support in the process. Bangar eventually directed the Dominion to the
Drizzlewood Coast
where he had located Jormag in. Intending to awaken the Elder Dragon, the Blood Imperator ordered Ryland and the Steel Warband to direct the military effort in the south and exterminate the native human, norn and
tengu
populations in the region while the rest of the Dominion built the
Frost Citadel
near Jormag's resting place.
Ryland was promoted to
tribune
, and the rest of the Steel Warband received
centurion
ranks.
By the time the United Legions had tracked the Dominion forces and fought against them in the Drizzlewood Coast, the Steel Warband proved themselves as skilled combatants and strategists despite their youth. They managed to push the United Legions' forces down to the
Umbral Grotto
while using propaganda to sway demoralized legionnaires to join their cause. Instead of destroying the diminished legions' forces, however, Ryland arranged a parley with the legion leadership where he offered them mercy as long as the legions joined the Dominion.
At a
parley
, Cinder participated in the talks alongside Ryland and
Varinia Stormsounder
and witnessed the recovered Pact Commander joining the talks. Cinder was quick to correct the Commander about Ryland's rank during the heated conversation that ensued. The arrival of the humans
Logan Thackeray
and
Kasmeer Meade
angered Ryland who had wanted the parley to be between charr, however, so Ryland, Cinder and Varinia called off the talks and stormed off, and the two armies resumed hostilities.
Cinder returned to the Steel Warband's secret base in
Breakroot Basin
to direct the Dominion's assault from there. However, the Pact Commander, Rytlock Brimstone and the acting
imperators
of the United Legions had located the base and
stormed
it. The assault force captured Cinder after a fierce battle as a bargaining chip with Ryland for a second parley.
Smodur the Unflinching
kills Cinder during a
parley
with
Ryland Steelcatcher
.
After
Iron Legion
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
had utilized a
Searing Crystal
to
bomb
a research-and-development team with assistance from Rytlock Brimstone, the Pact Commander's assistance and
Efram Greetsglory
's atoning
Flame Legion
splinter group, Cinder witnessed Efram and Smodur arguing in the taken Steel Warband base about Smodur's secretive actions, and the Commander halting the hostilities between the two charr leaders as they headed for the
new parley
with Ryland.
Although Cinder was defiant at her captors at first and confident in her warband's eventual success, Smodur surprised her by telling her that everyone else in her warband had been killed in battle while she had been in captivity except for her and Ryland. Cinder recovered from the news, however, and insulted the Iron Imperator by revealing that during her fight against him she believed that he had not lived up to his name "the Unflinching," which amused Rytlock.
When Ryland arrived to the former Steel Warband base, Cinder witnessed him and Crecia arguing about the importance of Steel Warband and how Ryland and Cinder could still defect to the United Legions' side to convince other charr to do the same and stop the civil war. Bored of the negotiations and angry at the idea of Crecia potentially letting Cinder go free to convince Ryland to join the legions, Smodur stabbed Cinder in the neck while stating that the legions did not need traitors in their ranks.
Cinder uttered Ryland's name with the last of her strength before collapsing, and her death enraged Ryland who attacked Smodur in revenge. Crecia's flame shield protected the Iron Imperator, however, causing Ryland to flee the scene in anger and ending the parley. Crecia was just as angry about Cinder's death as Ryland had been, and she told Smodur that his actions had ensured that the civil war between the United Legions and the Dominion could no longer be ended with diplomatic means.
Legacy
The Icebrood Saga
Following the Elder Dragon Jormag and
Primordus
's demise in their
clash
at
Anvil Rock
in 1334
AE
, Ryland, who had become Jormag's champion,
attacked
Rytlock, Crecia and the Commander in rage over losing the Ice Dragon who had given him what he had wanted. Disgusted with Ryland's selfish views, Crecia tried to make him realize that he had only been a tool for Bangar and Jormag. When Ryland refused to consider his mother's words and claimed that he had not relied on anyone for his success, Crecia pointed out that Cinder would gut him if she had witnessed what Ryland had become after all the sacrifices their warband had gone through, further enraging Ryland to attack his parents until he perished to Rytlock's blade.
End of Dragons
In 1335
AE
, the Pact Commander decided to tackle unresolved feelings regarding people from their past in order to strengthen their resolve for a rematch against an
oni
which had been feeding on the Commander's emotional vulnerabilities. The Commander
traveled to Cinder's old post
at Brokentooth Maw where the Commander and their
Canthan
ally
Park Chul-Moo
discussed why Cinder's death, despite her having been the enemy, had affected the Commander while the Commander also interviewed local charr from the
Shatter Warband
about Cinder to learn more about her as a person and as a leader. Thanks to these discussions and Chul-Moo's encouraging words, the Commander was able to come to terms with the guilt of not being able to prevent Cinder's untimely death in the Drizzlewood parley and bottle these emotions for
Taimi
's latest invention which the team would use against the oni.

Clawspur
Race
Charr
Profession
Thief
Rank
Veteran
Locations
Hero's Canton
(
Black Citadel
)
Umbral Grotto
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Organization
Player's Warband
United Legions
Clawspur
is a
charr
that is originally part of a charr
player character
's
warband
. He takes part in the
battle
against
Duke Barradin
's ghostly forces in the
Village of Smokestead
. If the player does not choose him as their sparring partner in their
biography
, he dies during the battle. If he is the player's sparring partner, he survives and helps them along the rest of their
personal story
.
He is described as a quiet person, even for a
thief
, and prefers to make quick kills from the shadow.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
The Icebrood Saga
In 1333
AE
, Clawspur assisted the United Legions in their war against the rogue
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's
Dominion
forces in
Drizzlewood Coast
. While there, he had a chance meeting with the Legionnaire who had become known as the
Pact Commander
.
Clawspur explained that, unlike many charr, he had never viewed Aurene as a threat because he trusted in the former Legionnaire's judgement, and he even considered the Elder Dragon an honorary member of the warband.
Clawspur also revealed that he had done his best to be worthy of his mantle of Legionnaire in the warband. Despite recruiting more members and having adventures with them, he was unable to prevent some warbandmates from either dying or defecting to the Dominion. The defectors included potential warbandmates
Cerosi Breaksteel
,
Elexus Shredskin
,
Luccia Wildeye
, and
Yahuk Fellstrike
. Clawspur vividly remembered the deaths of Howl and the original warband, so he swore not to lose one more warbandmate to Bangar.

======================================================================
PAGE: Clement Forktail
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Clement_Forktail
======================================================================

Clement Forktail
Race
Charr
Location
Hero's Canton
(
Black Citadel
)
Organization
Flame Legion
Clement Forktail
is the sire of the player's character if the personal story option "Sorcerous Shaman" was chosen. He appears in the storyline
Searching for the Truth
.
Biography
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing lore
Clement Forktail was a soldier of the
High Legions
who, after shortly becoming
legionnaire
of his
warband
, met a
Flame Legion
recruiter alongside his warband after they began to contact the Flame Legion. He and his warband were manipulated by the recruiter for several weeks and his warband eventually defected to the Flame Legion, with Clement defecting due to feeling loyal to them. After defecting, he committed murder against officers of the
Black Citadel
and began training
Legionnaire
Mergath Flarekin
as his second.
While training Mergath, he developed several new rituals in his
workshop
, including one that could kill the high-ranking representatives of the Legions within the Black Citadel. Realizing his mistake in choosing Mergath as his second, he turned himself in to the Black Citadel and attempted to make contact with his cub. Sending a mail to his cub, he convinced them to meet him north of
Ascalon City Ruins
, where
Clavus the Breathstealer
, an acquaintance of Clement, was sent to assassinate him.
He was then escorted to the Black Citadel's stockade where he was captured by his former Flame Legion comrades and brought to
Silex Castrum
, where they attempted to make him perform the ritual. Rescued by his cub and
Seneca the Relentless
, Clement aided the cub regardless of whether they chose Seneca's or his plan and was left in the Citadel's stockade.
Location
Ascalon
Black Citadel
Hero's Canton
(Only after completing
Sins of the Father
)
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 2: Sins of the Father
Searching for the Truth
A Fork in the Road
Sins of the Father
Tribunes in Effigy
Combat abilities
Skills
Dragon Punch
- Clement
charges
toward an enemy and strikes them with an uppercut, dealing Point Blank
AoE
damage and
launching
.
Fire Grab
- Damage foes in a
cone
in front of you. Deal more damage to burning foes.
Drake's Breath
- Spray a
cone
of fire at foes while on the move.
Lava Jet
- Creates an AoE field that deal damage every second over 5 seconds before exploding and
launching
enemies.
Notes
In order to get Clement for your home instance, you must side with him during
A Fork in the Road
. He will be standing on the balcony overlooking the entrance to the main building; the quickest way to find him is to take the ramp immediately to your left as you walk in, then follow that up to the first accessible room on the 2nd floor. Enter that room, hang a left, and exit onto the balcony.
If you side with Seneca during
A Fork in the Road
, he will not show up in the stockade anymore, even after finishing
Tribunes in Effigy
.

Argus Foolkiller

======================================================================
PAGE: Cloudseeker
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Cloudseeker
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Cloudseeker
Race
Kodan
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Locations
Spirits' Refuge
(
Bjora Marches
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
It is customary for a Claw to step down without a Voice. But I fear if I do, my people will succumb. So I linger—a counterweight to keep what little balance remains.
— Cloudseeker
Cloudseeker
is the
Claw of Koda
of
Still Waters Speaking
, a
kodan
settlement in
Bjora Marches
, where he and his fellow kodan struggle against the temptations of the eerie whispers which plague the region.
Biography
Early years
As the
Claw of Koda
, Cloudseeker once led the
kodan
of
Still Waters Speaking
together with
Ushers-in-Spring
, his
Voice of Koda
. Back then,
Drakkar Lake
was a smooth glass and had a voice of its own, a soft whisper, almost inaudible. But as time passed, and
Elder Dragons
were felled, the whispers grew stronger.
Jormag
's
champion
,
Drakkar
, had awoken.
Ushers-in-Spring, being a Voice of
Koda
, was no stranger to voices in her head. She listened to the whispers, tried to reason with them, but that was her demise. She turned and became another servant of Jormag. The kodan of Still Waters Speaking had lost their Voice, and thus, their connection to
Koda
.
It is customary for a Claw to step down without their Voice, but Cloudseeker put his people's wellbeing above tradition. He continued to lead the kodan of Still Waters Speaking, standing strong, lest his people succumb to the Elder Dragon.
The Icebrood Saga
Shadow in the Ice
The kodan of Still Waters Speaking distanced themselves from other settlements, and became wary of any outsiders. When the
charr
Crecia Stoneglow
and
Rytlock Brimstone
, following
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's trail, came across the settlement in 1333
AE
, the kodan there imprisoned them until they could verify that the charr pair's voices "were their own", that they had not fallen to the whispers. Shortly afterwards, the
norn
Braham Eirsson
and the
Pact Commander
, after earning the trust of the kodan by slaying several
Sons of Svanir
in the area, entered the settlement,
had an audience
with Cloudseeker, and vouched for the two
charr
.
Cloudseeker explained to the party that Bangar and his army had been spotted in the region, and feared that his judgment was being clouded by the whispers. The Claw revealed the source of the whispers to be Drakkar. More than a champion, Drakkar is the
mouthpiece
of the Elder Dragon, amplifying Jormag's whispers. Without it, the Elder Dragon can not whisper beyond its lair. However, defeating Drakkar would not be an easy task, as Cloudseeker illustrated in his bonfire how the Lost
Spirits of the Wild
—
Eagle
,
Ox
, and
Wolverine
—had been
corrupted
by Jormag while Drakkar feasted on their infinite
magic
. If the Commander and allies agreed to aid the kodan of Still Waters Speaking, the kodan would then aid them in defeating Drakkar.
As the Commander made preparations to aid the kodan,
Jhavi Jorasdottir
appeared in the camp and offered her
Vigil
forces' aid to help the kodan against hostile forces in the region. While staying at Still Waters Speaking, Jhavi conversed with Cloudseeker and sometimes disagreed with him about the kodan way of doing things. Despite their arguments, both leaders acknowledged that they needed each other's respective strength to face Drakkar and its minions.
After
securing the kodan
of Still Waters Speaking, Braham
claimed
the power of the Lost Spirits of the Wild, and Cloudseeker honored his word and offered his aid in the battle to come. He instructed the party to
destroy
Jormag's profane idols, scattered all throughout the Marches, which Drakkar uses to heal itself from any damage. At the same time, Jhavi led a
direct attack on Drakkar
, forcing it to retreat away from the lake into a southern cavern surrounded by rocks. With Drakkar weakened and cornered between the rocks, the Commander's party rallied to face the dragon champion while Cloudseeker prayed that Koda, and the Spirits of the Wild, guide them to victory.
Following Drakkar's
demise
, the kodan began recovering in their settlement. Some kodan voiced their concern about Cloudseeker not stepping down from his role of Claw; believing that traditions should be honored even in difficult times, or else the kodan themselves would be lost from Koda's grace.
Champions
Chapter 1: Truce
As news spread about both Jormag's latest movements and the increasing activity of
Primordus
's
destroyers
later in the same year, Cloudseeker traveled to the
Eye of the North
to exchange ideas with other races' representatives. While conversing with the Pact Commander, Cloudseeker explained that he and many kodan were willing to join the fight to return the world to balance from the disharmony caused by the Elder Dragons. When the Commander inquired if Cloudseeker, and the rest of the kodan by association, were actually norn wearing bearskin, Cloudseeker calmly countered the claim by questioning whether the Commander was just someone pretending to be the
Pact
's
commander
.
Chapter 4: Judgment
Following the deaths of Jormag and Primordus in their
clash
at
Anvil Rock
, Cloudseeker was among the delegates as they
gathered
in Eye of the North to congratulate the Commander on ending the dragon menace.
Locations
Shiverpeak Mountains
Bjora Marches
Spirits' Refuge
Still Waters Speaking
Eye of the North
Hall of Monuments
(from
Tidings and Misgivings
to
Champion's End
)
Story involvement

— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Dialogue
Bjora Marches
The whispers tell me I should not trust you. Which is exactly why I dare to.
Where is everybody?

"Shadow in the Ice" Mastery
meta-achievement.)

My people have grown...distant to one another. Distant to
Koda
. Unbalanced.

Some left seeking food. Some to rebuild our defenses. Few have returned. Go—find them. Guide them back to Koda.

(After earning progress in the

Slowly. You have helped the kodan of Still Waters Speaking, but
Jormag

Drakkar
lives, my people will be tempted by the

Her name was

. She was our connection to Koda, as all Voices are.

When Drakkar awoke, and the whispers grew louder, she tried to respond. Tried to reason. She listened. And she was turned.
Cloudseeker:
It is customary for a
Claw

What exactly are the whispers?
Cloudseeker:
They are the ever-present temptation of Jormag. The Elder Dragon speaks to any who might listen.
Cloudseeker:
It is one thing to hear the whispers. It's another to heed them. Remember, Jormag does not take. Jormag turns.
Cloudseeker:
Jormag's truth becomes your truth. If you desire power, Jormag will give it to you. And you give yourself to Jormag.
Is Drakkar the source of the whispers?
Cloudseeker:
Jormag speaks. But Drakkar is its mouthpiece. The whispers radiate from it, like heat from the sun.
Cloudseeker:
Kill Drakkar, and you stop the whispers. It is as simple as that.

Trivia
Cloudseeker is
voiced
by
Tommie Earl Jenkins
.
Concept art from before the game launched suggests that all
Claws of Koda
were at first considered for using the same character model Cloudseeker uses. However, Cloudseeker is the second
kodan
to use this model, after
Veteran Kodan Shieldbearers
. All other Claws use regular kodan character models.
On the contrary, all
Voices of Koda
do share the same character model, inspired by
this concept art
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cobiah Marriner
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Cobiah_Marriner
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Sea of Sorrows
.
A statue of Cobiah Marriner, as it was seen in
Lion's Arch
, prior to
its destruction
at the hands of
Scarlet Briar
. The remains of the statue can now be found at the center of the
Field of the Fallen
.
“
I say we build a port of our own. We build it, and we defend it against Orr. We make it a free port, not beholden to any nation, open to anyone who sails the sea. We'll teach others how to fight against the dead ships, using charr weaponry, asuran innovation, and human courage. The port would be open to any and all, so long as they'll fight against Orr and help to keep the waters clear.
— Cobiah Marriner
Commodore Cobiah Marriner
(1203—1286
AE
) was the founder and first
Commodore
of the rebuilt
Lion's Arch
, and grandfather of
Commodore Lawson Marriner
.  A statue of him can be found in
White Crane Terrace
in Lion's Arch. He was buried on
Deverol Island
.
Marriner was raised in the poorer portions of Lion's Arch, living his childhood within a run down shack on the coast. His father, a former member of the military turned sailor, had disappeared at sea leaving Cobiah to live with his alcoholic mother who turned abusive after her husband's disappearance and his younger sister,
Biviane
. Before that, his grandfather had fought to save Kryta from the
White Mantle
. Due to the nature of his mother, Cobiah was forced to work multiple jobs to earn money for the family. In 1219
AE
, his sister died in an accident falling from the docks, leading Cobiah to sign up for service as a mariner aboard the
Indomitable
. While aboard the
Indomitable
, Cobiah had traveled to
Kaineng City
as part of official trade with
Cantha
, making him one of the last
Tyrians
to have set foot on the southern continent.
Ten months into his voyage, the
Indomitable
was sent into the waters over sunken
Orr
to investigate the disappearance of ships. At
Malchor's Fingers
, the ship came into contact with
the Maw
just before
Zhaitan
awoke and
Orr rose
, leading to the sinking of the
Indomitable.
Cobiah was the sole survivor of that ship, and was rescued by the
Havoc
, an experimental
Iron Legion
ship carrying the first prototype steam engine for the
charr
that was crewed by the Steam and Shroud warbands. Aboard he befriended
Engineer Steamshroud
and the stowaway
asura
,
Macha
. Cobiah made an agreement with the captain of the ship,
Centurion Harrow Shroudweather
, to lead them to
Lion's Arch
and negotiate safe docking for the ship. However, when they arrived the city was sunken and barren, and the
Havoc
was attacked by a Krytan vessel, the
Disenmaedel
. When Harrow was killed, Cobiah threatened to blow up the vessel and everyone aboard to stop the fighting, and ended up unifying the two crews of
humans
and charr to serve together.
Over time and after many adventures his pirate crew attempted to steal the
Capricorn
, escaped the
Risen
forces destroying
Port Stalwart
, amassed gold stolen from
King Baede
's ship,
Salma's Grace
, sunk the
Dead Ship
Harbinger
, and rebuilt Lion's Arch, among many other adventures and acts of piracy. Cobiah formed the first
Captain's Council
and dedicated years to Lion's Arch instead of sailing, earning the respect of the town and the honorary title of commodore. In 1256
AE
, the Krytan crown prince
Edair
led a large naval and land assault to reclaim Lion's Arch for Kryta, in defiance of his father who had recently passed away. Edair captured Cobiah, his crew, and his family, but his conquest was thwarted when the Krytan navy was attacked by Dead Ships. Cobiah once again led the defense, uniting the citizens of Lion's Arch with those of Kryta to stand against the Risen, destroying their fleet and officially earning his city's independence from the Krytan nation. After the battle he remained in Lion's Arch with his wife
Isaye
and his son
Dane
.
Related achievements
Explorer
:
History Buff
—
Find the
plaques
around Tyria about Cobiah Marriner's life.
(2
)
The Juggernaut I: The Experimental Hammer
:
Ponder the Cobiah Marriner Statue.
—
Ponder
the
Cobiah Marriner
statue in the
memorial to the fallen
in
Lion's Arch
.
See also
Marriner Plaque
Family trees

Commander Serentine
Race

The Commander
(
Royal Palace
)
Organization
Ministry Guard
“
Let's just say that Commander Serentine and I don't see eye to eye on how our nation should be governed.
—
Logan Thackeray
about Commander Serentine
Commander Serentine
was a recent
Commander of Divinity's Reach
.
Biography
Early years
Serentine was first noticed by
Minister Caudecus
as a lieutenant in the
Ministry Guard
when she began investigating
Minister Brios
's death. As she examined his body, lying dead in an alleyway in a poor area of
Divinity's Reach
. Noting the shattered glass, she found red wine spilt on the ground and shortly after, Captain
Logan Thackeray
demanded control of the crime scene, which Serentine disagreed with though she continued her investigation.
She managed to identify that the wine had been poisoned, though the reasons for Brios entering the alley remained unknown, and deduced that the poison had been supplied by smugglers. Wondering at the reasons of him entering the poor area without any guards, she pondered the idea that he had been engaged in treason. Shortly after, she rifled through his office for any evidence and found a slip of paper inviting him to afternoon tea with
Master Exemplar
Anise
of the
Shining Blade
. She then interrogated Anise, though the Master Exemplar told her nothing of value.
To attempt to continue with her investigation, she spoke with Cadeucus, who instructed her to drop the investigation as he would help the late minister's family. Cadeucus then smiled, stating that
Kryta
would win through as ever if she just left things to him.
Personal story
In 1325
AE
, it was discovered that the Ministry had an alliance with the
bandits
plaguing Kryta's countryside. The bandits would make the Seraph look incompetent, and in return, when the council took over Kryta the bandits would be able to do as they pleased. Furthermore, it became clear that Commander Serentine was disrupting the Seraph's investigations, and bailing the bandits out of prison early when they did get caught. Anise and Thackeray caught her trying to break
Big Nose Ted
out, and Serentine was killed in the ensuing scuffle.

Death Row

Commodore Lawson Marriner
Race

Commodore Lawson Marriner
of the
Ship's Council
, son of
Dane Marriner
and grandson of
Lion's Arch
founders
Cobiah Marriner
and

, is a man of contrasts. The finery of his greatcoat contrasts with the leathery skin of his seafarer's face, and his quick movements aboard ship belie his age. His ship is the
Pride II
.
Commodore
Marriner survived the
Battle for Lion's Arch
, and can be found in the
Crow's Nest Tavern
in Lion's Arch. He is sitting down on the ground near the feast on the table.

In
Memory of Old Lion's Arch
Welcome to
Lion's Arch
. I'm
Commodore
Lawson Marriner. I hope you're enjoying your time in our fine city.
Commodore? So you're in charge here?
By rights, the
Captain's Council
runs Lion's Arch, but the Commodore does enjoy certain priveleges
[
sic
]
and influence. Perks I've enjoyed for 25 years now. Much like my grandfather,
Cobiah Marriner
.
Your grandfather was a Commodore too?
In his later years. In his youth, he was a sailor. When
Zhaitan
rose from the depths and the
great waves
drowned the original Lion's Arch, he rescued many ships and crews that had lost their captains.
How did he become Commodore?
As many of the rescued sailors had been taken to sea against their will, they cherished freedom as only the formerly enslaved can. Under Cobiah's guidance, they began rebuilding Lion's Arch, naming him Commodore.
I appreciate the welcome, and your sharing your grandfather's story.
I will respect that legacy and try to stay out of trouble here.
I am. In fact, I'd like to get back to my exploring.

is the leader of the

. She is revealed to have operated a White Mantle cell from the shadows and that the Mantle were responsible for killing the parents of the
Hero of Shaemoor
as well as planning to assassinate many prominent allies of

. Many of the
Confessor

Shining Blade
raid to the White Mantle hideout in 1325
AE
, and Esthel herself met her end at the hands of the Hero of Shaemoor. The White Mantle survived Esthel's demise, however, and she was succeeded by a
new Confessor
who had much more ambitious plans in mind for the future of
Kryta
.

PAGE: Corporal Beirne
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Corporal_Beirne
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Corporal Beirne
Race
Human
Locations
Defending Shaemoor
(
Shaemoor Garrison
,
Village of Shaemoor
)
Marching Orders
(
Theater of Delight
,
Crusted Shoals
)
Armor Guard
(
Cathedral of Zephyrs
,
Valley of Lyss
,
Cathedral of Eternal Radiance
,
Karst Plains
,
Garden of Ilya
,
Drowned Brine
,
Snaketail Inlet
,
Whisper Bay
)
The Steel Tide
(
Pursuit Pass
)
Organization
Seraph
Pact
Vigil
Image(s)
Beirne as a Tactician in Orr.
“
Charr tanks, human escort, and a sylvari scout team. It doesn't get any better than this.
—
Tactician Beirne
Corporal Sam Beirne
is a member of the
Seraph
who has a
pet
coyote
named
Allie
. Inspired to fight against the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, he later joins the
Vigil
and the
Pact
in the assault on
Orr
.
Biography
Sam Beirne acquired his dog,
Allie
, in 1317
AE
, and the pair became inseparable. With his family long gone and his friends coming and going, he relied on his canine companion but maintained a positive outlook. After joining the
Seraph
, he was eventually promoted to the rank of corporal.
When the
centaurs
attacked the
Village of Shaemoor
in 1325
AE
, Corporal Beirne was in charge of getting the villagers to safety. During the attack he met a heroic citizen and instructed them to seek out
Sergeant Walters
. The attack was eventually repelled, and the citizen Beirne had conversed with became known as the
Hero of Shaemoor
.
By the time Beirne heard about the
Pact
's plan to take down the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, he figured that the assault force needed him more than
Divinity's Reach
did. Once the Pact agreed to let him bring Allie with him to
Orr
, he signed up and became a member of the
Vigil
, and was eventually promoted to the rank of
Tactician
.
The Pact breached Orr, and Tactician Beirne fought alongside various races in the assault force while coming to terms with collaborating with the
charr
against a mutual enemy. During this time he met and befriended
Deborah
who hailed from Divinity's Reach. The pair grew close during the campaign and fought under the
Pact Commander
with Beirne being praised for his skills.
While escorting
Ballista Geargrind
's heavy tanks into the heart of Orr alongside
Pale Reavers
and other Pact forces, Beirne met with
Priestess Amelia
and was happy to hear that the priestess also hailed from Divinity's Reach. The company came under assault by
Risen
led by
Archmage Harshaf
, however, and Beirne sacrificed himself to save Deborah's life. Beirne's death weighed heavily on both Allie and Deborah with the former being adopted by the latter. They would honor his memory by carrying on the fight.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Krytan Politics
Defending Shaemoor
Chapter 8: Victory or Death
Marching Orders
Armor Guard
The Steel Tide
Notes
Bug
:
Sometimes the cinematic will show him without a pistol but still using pistol firing animations.
Anomaly
:
Beirne can be revived with
reviving skills
, with no effect on the other NPCs, and having similar chat options as before dead.
Trivia
Beirne is named after one of the game designers at
ArenaNet
.
He wears full
Council Watch armor
, sans helmet, with
Seraph Sword
and
Seraph Shield
in early personal story. He wears
Sneakthief Shoulderguards
,
Emblazoned Coat
,
Sneakthief Gloves
,
Council Watch Boots
and wields
Vigil's Honor Sword
and
Vigil's Honor Warhorn
while in Orr.
However, during the cinematic in The Steel Tide, while saving Deborah, he uses a
Pact Handgun
.

PAGE: Corporal Jantzen
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Corporal_Jantzen
======================================================================

Corporal Jantzen
Race
Human
Level
26
Location
Tangle Root
(
Brisban Wildlands
)
Organization
Seraph
Image(s)
Interactive map path
“
Welcome to the edge of nowhere.
— Corporal Jantzen
Corporal Jantzen
is the
Seraph
officer in charge of commanding and securing
Tangle Root Outpost
, creating a passage to
Dry Top
and fending off
Inquest
saboteurs.
Biography
In 1326
AE
, Corporal Jantzen was in charge of a small expeditionary squad of
Seraph
, including
Belinda Delaqua
, which was tracking black market traders and thieves from
Divinity's Reach
to
Brisban Wildlands
. While trying to cut through new and strange vines blocking a secret ingress which the traders had been using to slip in and out of the wildlands, the corporal met with a group of adventurers led by the
Pact Commander
and learned that one of these adventurers was Belinda's sister
Marjory Delaqua
.
The meeting ended with an abrupt
Inquest
attack, but Jantzen's squad and the Commander's party fought them off. Realizing they the outpost could not withstand an all-out attack while the Seraph were busy investigating the smugglers, Jantzen ordered Belinda to hurry to
Fort Salma
. He told her to update the forces stationed there on what had happened and oversee getting soldiers posted at the outpost to keep the oddly invasive vines back.
Jantzen provided the Commander with what intel he had about the vines although he admitted not knowing what lay beyond in the
Maguuma Wastes
which he believed might have changed after the
Rising of Orr
. He remained stationed at the outpost for a time, sending others from his squad forward to investigate the black market in the meantime while waiting for reinforcements to arrive.

Maguuma Jungle

PAGE: Corporal Kellach

Corporal Kellach
Race
Risen
Rank
Champion
Locations
Kellach's Attack
(
Royal Palace
)
The Queen's Justice
(
Royal Palace
)
The Trap
(
Royal Palace
)
Image(s)
Kellach after falling to Zhaitan's corruption.
“
I need her blood! Royal blood. Don't you understand? It can cure the corruption. Make us all free!
— Corporal Kellach
Corporal Kellach
was a soldier in the
Seraph
guard. Being greatly devoted to
Kryta
, he sought a power that would allow him to safeguard
Queen Jennah
and his home. To do this, he sought the aid of a seer,
Alastia Crow
. She led Kellach to an old artifact from the
Ruins of Orr
which contained
Zhaitan
's influence and power. This power slowly corrupted Kellach, turning him insane and a beacon for the
Risen
to follow.
Kellach returned to Alastia for revenge and for a cure; to save her crew on the
Ravenous
and in revenge, she told Kellach a lie that to cure his "corruption" he must bathe in royal blood. In fury, Kellach killed Alastia Crow and headed towards the
Royal Palace
in 1325
AE
, entering the
Queen's Throne Room
through a secret passage near the Northern Waterways that led to the palace cellars. Queen Jennah attempted to reason with Kellach, but he was driven too far in insanity and attacked her.

======================================================================
PAGE: Councillor Haia
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Councillor_Haia
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
,
Living World Season 3
, and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Councillor Haia
Race
Asura
Locations
Council Level
(
Rata Sum
)
Rata Novus
(
Rata Novus
)
Organization
Arcane Council
Image(s)
Interactive map  (Upper level)
“
Nothing personal: this is merely how top-level Council politics plays out. If it helps, I'd be happy to feign remorse.
—
Councillor Haia
Councillor Haia
is a member of the
Arcane Council
who tried to kill the other councillors by modifying the
Snaff Savant
's
VAL-A Golems
. Although the Snaff Savant and
Zojja
made the council aware of Haia's scheme, they could not find solid evidence tracking back to her, so Haia was allowed to stay in the council although the councillors would keep an eye on her.
Biography
Personal story
In 1325
AE
,
Zojja
approached
the latest winner of the
Snaff Prize
and revealed that the
VAL-A Golem
models based on this
Snaff Savant
's design had gone berserk and killed the people they were supposed to be serving at
Mrot Boru
and
Desider Atum
.
After defeating the rampaging
golem
at Desider Atum and inspecting its remains, the Savant discovered that the golem had been tampered with because its aether couplings had been misaligned, its control parameters were all wrong, the logic arcanum had been twisted beyond recognition, and, most distressingly, it had only served cold beer instead of warm lager and fine wine.
While investigating the matter, the Savant discovered that three people had checked out the original model's blueprints in the Rata Sum archives: Councillor Haia,
Mink
, and
Keppa
. Further investigation revealed that each asura had modified the designs for their own reasons, and the Savant set out to learn more about these modifications. Haia gave the Savant a warm welcome during the investigation and, unlike Mink and Keppa, let the Savant peruse her version of modified blueprints without issue. She had baked cookies in the meantime and promised to send them to the Savant's lab. A closer look at the blueprints revealed that, unlike the other two asura's suspicious modifications, Haia had seemingly improved on the design by swapping the golem's power source with a new, more efficient source of the same size and weight.
However, investigating the two remaining suspects led to a dead end as neither asura's haphazard designs could have caused such a complex malfunction. Using the process of elimination, the Savant and Zojja came to the troubling conclusion that Haia had to have been behind the sabotage and had somehow covered her tracks despite her ostensible innocence. The pair
tested the theory
by building a VAL-A golem according to Haia's blueprints, which led to the golem going haywire. Zojja concluded that the changed power core had to contain some hidden exploit to drive the golems to murder.
Armed with this knowledge, Zojja, her golem
Mr. Sparkles
and the Savant rushed to the
Arcane Council
's chambers to meet with Haia but found her waiting for them along the way instead. Although the pair threatened to expose Haia in front of the entire council with the proof they had, Haia laughingly dismissed them by pointing out that she had already sent her underlings to retrieve the incriminating blueprints from the pair's lab. She also revealed that she intended to use the modified VAL-A golems to dispose of the rest of the council while framing the pair for the entire incident.
Realizing that they had been played and that Haia's plan was already underway, Zojja and the Savant left the councillor behind and rushed to the Arcane Council's chambers. Upon arriving, the pair heard the council discuss that the golems were a gift from an anonymous donor just as the golems went berserk. Rallying the councillors to fight beside them, Zojja and the Savant defeated the golems after a fierce battle without any casualties in the council.
After the battle, Zojja and the Savant confronted
High Councillor Flax
and told him about Haia tampering with the Savant's design and what her plan had been. Flax needed no more convincing as he was well aware of Haia's bold moves although he also knew that Haia was clever enough to never leave evidence behind that could be traced back to her. Flax thanked the pair for their assistance but insisted that they drop the subject and let the council handle Haia. According to Flax, the council would keep an eye on Haia for any future acts of treachery and might even send a "lethal gift" to her should she step out of line again. Zojja was more skeptical of the council's effectiveness, however, expecting Haia's power and wealth to allow her to get off with a mere slap on the wrist although the Savant pointed out that another councillor might very well do her in one day.
Living World Season 3
Haia
accompanied
other councillors on a tour in the recently discovered lost city of
Rata Novus
in 1329
AE
after the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
had been
slain
. While there, a tour guide informed them about the history of Rata Novus and how the fallen city's
asuran
denizes had made some breakthrough research on how to defend against the asura's old nemesis, the Elder Dragon
Primordus
. According to the guide, Councillor
Phlunt
and other
krewe
leaders were hard at work on uncovering new data on Primordus from the Rata Novan databanks that Rata Sum would use to its advantage in the future.
The Icebrood Saga
Following the Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
in 1333
AE
, the Ice Dragon's
champion
Ryland Steelcatcher
approached the Arcane Council with an offer to exchange knowledge and collaborate with the asura to defeat Primordus, Jormag's enemy, for good as the Fire Dragon's
rise
was inevitable. Haia and the rest of the council agreed to Jormag's terms and saw the truce as logical in order to finally destroy the Fire Dragon which had driven asuran ancestors from the
Depths of Tyria
centuries ago.
By the time the
Pact Commander
,
Braham Eirsson
and
Rytlock Brimstone
arrived at
Rata Sum
, they
witnessed
Ryland having a discussion with Councillors
Vark
and
Zudo
. Shortly after, Vark, Zudo and her sister
Ludo
attended
Taimi
's briefing at the
Advanced Metamystics Lab
alongside the Commander, Rytlock and Ryland. Taimi explained that she had been using her network to track the recent earthquakes happening throughout
Tyria
although she was unsure if all of it was related to Primordus or movements of
ley energy
. Vark found Taimi's findings odd, though, as the council had never seen any activity that far below the crust.
Suddenly, Taimi's readings indicated activity beneath Rata Sum. As if on cue, the city was invaded by Primordus's
destroyers
which proved to be stronger than previous destroyer generations. Haia fought alongside the rest of the Arcane Council, Ryland's
Frost Legion
, Braham, Rytlock, Taimi and the Commander and forced the destroyers into retreat once they had felled the destroyer lieutenant leading the invasion force.
Later, Haia and Vark were present with Taimi and
Gorrik
as they
relayed
their latest findings on Primordus to Ryland: the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
's
death
had amplified a feedback loop between Primordus and destroyers so that each of them strengthened the other in preparation for Primordus's rise.
By the time the Commander arrived and questioned the council on why Ryland had been allowed in the council chambers, Haia explained that it would take more than the
Pact
and the Commander to defeat as destructive a force as Primordus. While discussing how to cut the flow between Primordus and destroyers, Haia suggested boosting Jormag's power to level the field between the two rival Elder Dragons. Gorrik found Haia's suggestion a reasonable approach although he questioned whe wisdom behind it.

Dialogue
Welcome to the
Arcane Council
chambers. You appear inquisitive. Speak up. Please don't let my entourage intimidate you.
Can you tell me about your role on the Arcane Council?
I'm pleased to admit that I've demonstrated a talent for settling disputes among my fellow councillors.
What have you done to settle Council disputes?
My access to the
Inquest
has made it possible for me to outsource the Council's project vetting to them.
You're a member of the Inquest?
Indeed, and proud of it. My fellows happily share their vast, useful intellectual resources for good of our people.
If this is your attempt at recruiting me, I'm not interested.
I understand your concern, though you needn't fear. The Inquest doesn't insist on cooperation, only on noninterference.
I...see. With that in mind, can you tell me again about your role on the Arcane Council?
(same as "I'm pleased to admit...")
Oh, I'm not afraid of the Inquest.
The Council tolerates Inquest involvement in their decisions?
The Council has become stagnant and indecisive. The Inquest has stepped in at my request to provide valuable resolution.
What is it that you do for the Arcane Council, again?
(same as "I'm pleased to admit...")
That's disappointing.
Didn't the villain
Scarlet
once belong to the Inquest?
We knew that miserable, misguided
sylvari
as Ceara. A singularly brilliant student to be sure, but she was also dangerously defiant of authority and protocol. A pity. And a waste.
Is it true she studied at all three
colleges
?
Yes, and some of us are still bristling at the egregious absurdity of the Council's decision. She was brilliant for a sylvari, but compared to an asura? Scarcely more than a novelty.
For a novelty, she's done a lot of damage.
True. I'm proud to say the Inquest brushed her off faster than anyone else, but it still wasn't fast enough. Possessing knowledge in excess of one's cranial capacity is always dangerous.
What is it that you do for the Arcane Council, again?
(same as "I'm pleased to admit...")
I'll take your word for it.
What is it that you do for the Arcane Council, again?
(same as "I'm pleased to admit...")
Interesting.
What is it that you do for the Arcane Council, again?
(same as "I'm pleased to admit...")
Interesting.
This is a disturbing development.
The Council is deferring decisions on project proposals to the Inquest?
(same as "The Council tolerates Inquest involvement in their decisions?")
That's disturbing.
Interesting.
I take it from your attire and companions that you are a member of the Inquest?
(same as "Indeed, and proud of it...")
Don't worry, I'm not intimidated by the Inquest.

Unlike the other two councillors who follow the
Guide
,
Haia
and

, Yahk does not have any greets.

:

where she first appears.

Councillor Zudo
Race
Asura
Profession
Necromancer
Location
Council Level
(
Rata Sum
)
Organization
Arcane Council
Image(s)
Interactive map  (Upper level)
Councillor Zudo
, sister of
Councillor Ludo
, is a member of the
Arcane Council
. She is the dean of the
College of Dynamics
, specializing in
necromancy
.
Biography
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
The Icebrood Saga
Following the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
in 1333
AE
, the Ice Dragon's
champion
Ryland Steelcatcher
approached the Arcane Council with an offer to exchange knowledge and collaborate with the
asura
to defeat
Primordus
, Jormag's enemy, for good as the Fire Dragon's
rise
was inevitable. Zudo and the rest of the council agreed to Jormag's terms and saw the truce as logical in order to finally destroy the Fire Dragon which had driven asuran ancestors from the
Depths of Tyria
centuries ago.
By the time the
Pact Commander
,
Braham Eirsson
and
Rytlock Brimstone
arrived at
Rata Sum
, they
witnessed
Ryland having a discussion with Zudo and
Councilor Vark
. Zudo was the first to welcome the Commander to the city and introduced them to Ryland. When the Commander questioned why Ryland, who had helped Jormag awaken, was allowed in the asuran capital, Vark pointed out that Ryland's actions in the
charr
civil war at the
Drizzlewood Coast
did not concern the asura and that it was more important to focus on Primordus who was sure to rise again in Jormag's wake. Zudo backed up her fellow councillor's statement by mentioning that the council was focused on the present and the future and that Jormag could be reasoned with. The council believed that an alliance against a common enemy was logical and that the Commander should recognize the value of such an arrangement against the destructive Fire Dragon.
Vark, Zudo and her sister
Ludo
attended
Taimi
's briefing at the
Advanced Metamystics Lab
alongside the Commander, Rytlock and Ryland. Taimi explained that she had been using her network to track the recent earthquakes happening throughout
Tyria
although she was unsure if all of it was related to Primordus or movements of
ley energy
. Vark found Taimi's findings odd, though, as the council had never seen any activity that far below the crust.
Suddenly, Taimi's readings indicated activity beneath Rata Sum. As if on cue, the city was invaded by Primordus's
destroyers
which proved to be stronger than previous destroyer generations. Zudo fought alongside the rest of the Arcane Council, Ryland's
Frost Legion
, Braham, Rytlock, Taimi and the Commander and forced the destroyers into retreat once they had felled the destroyer lieutenant leading the invasion force. As soon as the battle had concluded, Zudo ordered the destroyer corpses to be studied so the asura could catalogue their evolutionary discrepancies and identify any weaknesses.
