Trivia
Elora’s shirt color varies between pastel blue and pastel green.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ember Doomforge
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ember_Doomforge
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Ghosts of Ascalon
, the
personal story
,
Guild Wars 2: Heart of Thorns
,
The Icebrood Saga
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Ember Doomforge
Race
Charr
Level
36, 40, 80
Locations
Postern Ward
(
Lion's Arch
)
Legions' Alcove
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Tranquil Glen
(
Starlit Weald
)
Pilgrim's Rest
(
Eternity's Garden
)
Organization
Ash Legion
Guild Initiative
Vigil
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Ember's appearance as a
Vigil
member.
Interactive map
Ember Doomforge
is an
Ash Legion
charr
who also works for the
Vigil
and the
Guild Initiative
. She is the granddaughter of
General Almorra Soulkeeper
and the niece of
Ajax Anvilburn
. Ember has worked alongside
Dougal Keane
,
Gullik Oddsson
,
Killeen
,
Kranxx
, and
Riona Grady
to
retrieve
the
Claw of the Khan-Ur
from
Ascalon City Ruins
, setting the stage for the
Ebonhawke Treaty
between charr and
humans
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Ember later assists the
United Legions
in the charr civil war in
Drizzlewood Coast
to defeat the rogue
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
and bring him to custody. She ends up working as the charr ambassador to
Cantha
and accompanies the
Tyrian Alliance
to explore the recently discovered island realm of
Castora
.
Biography
Early years
Little is known about Ember Doomforge's parents other than that her dam was both the daughter of
General Almorra Soulkeeper
and a member of the
Ash Legion
.
Ember followed in her dam's footsteps and joined Ash Legion ranks as well. As Almorra's granddaughter, Ember was also the apparent niece of the
Renegade
leader
Ajax Anvilburn
.
After learning that Almorra had left the
Blood Legion
and founded the
Vigil
as a direct response to the threat posed by
Kralkatorrik
and other
Elder Dragons
in 1320
AE
,
Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
of the Ash Legion ordered Ember, Almorra's grandcub, to infiltrate the order under the pretense of remitting Ember from the legion to boost the Vigil's numbers. However, the three female
charr
all knew the real reason Ember was in the Vigil: to report to Malice on the Vigil's activities. Almorra accepted Ember into the order without issue despite knowing Ember's true allegiance; it is suggested that the general eventually learned that Malice was secretly supportive of the faction among the charr who were working for a
truce
with
humanity
so they could free more of their forces to fight the Elder Dragons, a goal which Almorra herself was after.
Ghosts of Ascalon
Forming the group
In 1324
AE
,
Crusader
Ember was ordered by General Soulkeeper to accompany the fellow Crusader
Riona Grady
, the human
Dougal Keane
, the
sylvari
Killeen
, and the
norn
Gullik Oddsson
to the
ghost
-infested ruins of
Ascalon City
. Their
mission
was to retrieve the
Claw of the Khan-Ur
, a lost relic of the
Khan-Ur
which the pro-truce party wanted to hand over to
Queen Jennah
who, in turn, would return it to the charr
High Legions
in a symbolic gesture of friendship to begin negotiations for a ceasefire. Ember was also meant to act as the party's guide while in the charr-controlled
Ascalon
and help them keep a low profile as the party could not let their intentions be known to either local humans or charr lest the anti-truce factions on both sides tried to stop their mission.
Upon meeting her traveling companions in
Vigil Centerhouse
, Ember did not initially get along with her fellow Crusader Riona and the treasure hunter Dougal whose spiteful remarks about charr savagery annoyed her, and she made sure to remind them of the charr view on the
human-charr conflict
and justifying the charr's actions against humanity who, in her view, had stolen the charr's rightful homeland from them. She also made a point to educate the party about charr history involving the
charr rebellion
and
Kalla Scorchrazor
's part in it, as well as the concept of
fahrars
. Ember also helped subdue and calm down the drunk Gullik who had attacked Dougal in grief over Dougal not spreading the legend of Gullik's late cousin
Gyda Oddsdottir
who Dougal had previously been adventuring with.
Ember fiercely protested being placed in chains during their trip to
Ebonhawke
as she viewed Kalla Scorchrazor, who had freed female charr from the chains of servitude, as an inspiration and did not want to tarnish Kalla's legacy by becoming a human captive. However, after a bit of persuasion, Ember eventually understood that there was no other way for the party to get past Ebonhawke because the citizens there, who had been fighting against besieging charr for generations, would become suspicious if a charr was seen walking around without any restraints.
The party's journey nearly ended before it could begin when local guards had been tipped off about their presence. They were forced to recruit another Vigil member, the
asura
engineer
Kranxx
, so he could lead them to an underground passage and help them flee from the pursuers. During the escape, the party were forced to kill several humans and charr in order to maintain the secrecy of their mission, which left some party members shaken. In the chase's aftermath, they began viewing one another with suspicion, wondering if one of them was secretly a spy for one of the anti-truce factions and if this supposed mole had warned the guards in order to make the mission fail.
Adventures in Ascalon
As the party continued the journey to Ascalon City, they were forced to venture into the
Dragonbrand
where Killeen lost her life in a
Branded
assault. Saddened by their sylvari companion's demise, Dougal and Ember slowly began to warm up to one another as they gradually learned more about their respective pasts and conflicting allegiances.
By the time they were closing in on the
Plains of Ashford
and thus the territory of the
Iron Legion
, Ember got to place Dougal and Riona in chains as this time the local charr would grow suspicious if they saw free humans wandering about. Although understanding the humans' frustration after what she had been through, Ember could not help but be delighted by this reversal of fortune. Their journey through Ascalon went smoothly until they witnessed members of the
High Legions
fighting against zealous
Flame Legion
. Realizing that the Flame Legion might overcome the charr forces if left unchecked, the party resolved to aid the High Legions and help the charr secure victory against the Flame shamans.
In the ensuing chaos Riona had left her post and chased after a fleeing Flame Legion member to prevent him from taking intel to Flame reinforcements, but eventually both she and Dougal were found and apprehended. Ember came face to face with the legion forces' commander who was
Tribune
Rytlock Brimstone
of the Blood Legion, a celebrated hero among the charr. Rytlock took the party as his "guests" and interrogated Ember, who had identified herself as a member of the Ash Legion, about such an odd transport of human "prisoners." Ember's lies seemed to convince Rytlock; however, she was fully aware that the tribune had sensed something was amiss in her story but did not want to press the matter further because he hated mysteries and he could always ask Imperator Malice for answers once the two met.
Recognizing Ember's lineage and realizing that whatever mission she was on had to be important, Rytlock ultimately let the party continue their journey although he warned them not to cross paths with his forces again. The rest of the party figured out that Ember's true allegiance actually lay with Malice who had sent her to spy on Almorra. Ember admitted as much; however, she was unable to answer the party's questions about if Malice was part of the secretive truce faction among the charr although Dougal heavily suspected it.
Retrieving the Claw
Upon reaching Ascalon City, the party attempted to locate the Claw of the Khan-Ur based on Dougal's memory of seeing it when he had last braved the dangers of the ruins. They ended up alerting the vigilant
Foefire
ghosts
to their location, and Gullik seemingly lost his life in the resulting brawl. The party were briefly saved by
Savione
, the chief courtier of
King Adelbern
and the only friendly ghost in all of Ascalon due to having died moments before the Sorcerer King had cast the Foefire curse. With Savione's help, the party were able to decipher the clues of the Claw's current whereabouts in Adelbern's trap-filled vault and proceeded to secure it. Riona convinced Ember to accompany Kranxx as a decoy to lure the ghosts away so Riona and Dougal could sneak into the king's treasure vault.
Although the distraction worked for a time, Adelbern eventually spotted all the intruders and summoned his ghost army to kill them, promising to forcibly recruit the party as his undead soldiers and then lead an invasion of the surface world so he would finally have his revenge against charr invaders and any who sympathized with charr. Savione sacrificed himself to distract Adelbern and buy the party more time, but even that was not enough to quell the Sorcerer King's mad rage. For a time Dougal's party believed that Ember had perished as well when the ghosts closed in on them.
Before the Sorcerer King could defeat the party with his magic, however, Kranxx arrived in the nick of time. The asura battled Adelbern and the ghost army with a massive
golem
made from the bones of Ascalon's long-dead soldiers and infused with the magic of the late asuran inventor
Blimm
's ruby known as the
Golem's Eye
. Although Kranxx perished in the clash, he successfully managed to evaporate Adelbern for a time with the sheer mass of the golem and the city's architecture, granting the rest of the party valuable few moments to locate the Claw and flee from the ruins before the ghosts would reform.
Once Dougal had retrieved the Claw from the depths of the vault and handed it over to Riona while expecting to be pulled up, however, the human Crusader showed her true colours by refusing to help him. Riona revealed herself as the traitor who had alerted the guards to the party's presence in Ebonhawke and justified her actions by the crimes which the charr had committed against humanity for centuries and which had cost the lives of her loved ones. Riona had not slain the Flame Legion scout she had been chasing after but had made a secret deal with him: instead of delivering the Claw to Almorra, she would hand it over to the Flame Legion to embolden their forces and ensure that the truce negotiations would never begin. While the charr tore themselves apart in a brutal civil war that would ensue from the Flame Legion's rise, the humans could step in, destroy any charr who remained in the aftermath of the civil war, and reclaim Ascalon at long last.
However, Ember had survived her battle with the ghosts and had managed to track down the pair, overhearing Riona's confession. She managed to surprise the treacherous Crusader and attack her, giving Dougal enough time to find the way up and prevent Riona's escape. Ember had been weakened by her prior battle with the ghosts, however, allowing Riona to fatally slice her with the Claw. Dougal managed to surprise Riona and battled her, ultimately killing the Crusader, who had been his former friend, while snatching the Claw from her as she fell to her death into Adelbern's vault. Dougal pulled out a rejuvenating potion, which Kranxx had given him before his demise, and used it on Ember and himself, allowing both of them to slowly heal from their grave wounds.
As Ember regained her strength, she worried out loud if Riona's words to Dougal had managed to persuade him to keep the Claw from the Vigil. However, she was relieved to hear that Dougal intended to honor his deal with Almorra and ensure that the truce negotiations could begin as he had come to see value in saving charr lives as well. Ember responded that she, too, would honor Almorra's wishes despite being unsure about most humans' motivations as she did not want to face her granddam empty-handed. At that moment Dougal finally recognized the familial connection between Ember and Almorra.
The pair's conversation was cut off by Gullik who staggered into view, revealing that he had survived the ghost attack as well thanks in part to Kranxx's intervention. Upon informing the norn about Kranxx and Riona's demise, the pair acknowledged that while the Claw of the Khan-Ur was not as impressive a weapon as Adelbern's sword
Magdaer
, it would play an important role to ensure a ceasefire for charr and humans and in turn free their forces to face a greater foe that threatened all of
Tyria
: the Elder Dragons.
After taking some of the treasures of Ascalon City with them as compensation for their troubles, the trio returned to Almorra with the Claw. The retrieval of the Khan-Ur's weapon set up the events that would eventually lead to the signing of the Ebonhawke Treaty.
Personal story
Upon her return from Ascalon City, Ember began working with the Vigil at
Summit Peak
to keep the truce negotiations on track.
In 1325
AE
, Ember met with
Warmaster Forgal Kernsson
and a new
Vigil Recruit
under his care who had hurried to Summit Peak with dire news. The pair had learned about an
impending assault
, led by the charr
Renegades
' latest leader
Ajax Anvilburn
, on the summit which aimed to kill the charr and human ambassadors there and spark a war between humans and charr again.
Ember was already aware of Forgal and the Recruit's reputation because her dam had informed her about their adventures, and she happily welcomed the pair's aid. Working together, the Vigil and the ambassadors ultimately managed to repel the Renegades and kill Ajax, ensuring that the negotiations would continue uninterrupted.
Heart of Thorns
Following the
destruction
of the
Pact
fleet in the
Heart of Maguuma
in 1328
AE
, Ember joined Dougal Keane in the newly founded
Guild Initiative
and was stationed in
Lion's Arch
. She helped several new
guilds
establish a foothold via
guild halls
throughout the Heart of Maguuma,
Central Tyria
and beyond. She agreed with Dougal's intentions to turn the guilds into assets that would protect Tyria from various dangers, ranging from local threats to the Elder Dragons, while the Pact was recovering from its losses.
The Icebrood Saga
Upon learning of Almorra's
demise
at the hands of the rogue Blood Legion imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
in 1333
AE
, Ember wanted to avenge her granddam's death by killing Bangar. By the time the Vigil had turned its focus from Elder Dragons to taking down Bangar and his insurgent
Dominion
who were attempting to
awaken
the slumbering Elder Dragon
Jormag
, Malice informed Ember about the operation which the
United Legions
were conducting in
Drizzlewood Coast
to take the fight to Bangar before he could succeed in his goals.
Ember asked Malice to join the United Legions' war effort and helped the imperator scout a suitable, secluded
forward base
for the legions behind enemy lines from which they could strike at Bangar's
Frost Citadel
. Once a party consisting of Rytlock Brimstone,
Crecia Stoneglow
, the norn
Braham Eirsson
, and the
Pact Commander
had successfully
cleared the way
to the secluded area for the legions' reinforcements, Malice and Ember greeted them upon their arrival. Ember introduced herself as a
tribune
of the Ash Legion and explained some of her backstory and reasons for joining the operation. She also informed the party about what other Vigil leaders such as
Jhavi Jorasdottir
and
Laranthir of the Wild
were doing, and expressed her desire to take down Bangar.
After the Commander and the United Legions had weakened local Dominion forces in the region, Ember attended a
strategy meeting
where
Iron Legion
imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
and Braham were debating the best course of action that the legions should take to defeat Bangar. Braham insisted on needing more time to ritually open an enchanted door leading into the
Sanctum of the Wild
where Braham hope to invoke the power of the
Spirits of the Wild
against Bangar and use the sanctum as a route for a backdoor invasion straight into Bangar's stronghold. Smodur, meanwhile, believed that the legions could not wait until Braham had managed to get in contact with the silent Spirits and that the legions and their allies should instead assault the Frost Citadel's main entrance while using their combined strength to defeat any resistance and crush the resolve of Bangar's army.
Before the officers could decide on which plan to follow, Bangar's right hand
Ryland Steelcatcher
invaded the legions' base via portals, assassinated Smodur, and brought the Dominion and Jormag's
icebrood
with him to either force the legion leadership to surrender or kill them if they resisted. Although the allied forces were able to repel the attack, Ryland himself had successfully fled, leaving the victory hollow. In the grim aftermath, the leaders discussed who would succeed Smodur as Iron Imperator, and Malice mentioned that Tribune
Mia Kindleshot
would be the most likely candidate based on her intel. Ember pointed out that they did not know Mia's current whereabouts, but she promised that they would find Mia.
However, the leaders realized that they were running out of time and could not wait for Mia or other Iron tribunes to arrive to lead the Iron Legion, so Crecia and Malice convinced
Efram Greetsglory
, the leader of an allied
Flame Legion
splinter group, to temporarily lead Iron forces on the battlefield. According to the female charr, Iron Legion soldiers had grown to respect Efram after seeing him fight by their side during the war against the Dominion. The plan was to make Crecia, Rytlock and the Commander aid Braham in his rituals and open a backdoor while the rest of the United Legions would assault the Frost Citadel.
Ember joined Efram, Malice and
Kasmeer Meade
in coordinating the
assault
on Dominion strongholds in the region. They eventually fought their way to the gates of the Frost Citadel and breached the icy keep, defeating several high-ranking Dominion tribunes and icebrood along the way while shutting down the Dominion's machines which were using Jormag's blood to
corrupt
charr into empowered
Frost Legion
soldiers.
Upon escaping from the collapsing Frost Citadel, the United Legions found themselves facing a
Claw of Jormag
which attacked them. Working together and using scrap to build reinforced cannons, the charr and their allies managed to repel the
dragon champion
's attacks and pushed it towards an icy gorge. After a fierce
battle
, the legions successfully defeated the Claw of Jormag. However, they were aware that their respite would be brief as more Claws of Jormag would eventually replace the defeated one while the Dominion and the Frost Legion would bring in reinforcements and continue fighting. Ever ferocious, Ember proclaimed that the legions would simply defeat upcoming enemy reinforcements just like they had defeated their previous foes.
End of Dragons
An unexpected invitation from
Empress Ihn
of
Cantha
reaching
Kryta
to establish diplomatic relations spurred Queen Jennah and several other nations' leaders to send a group of ambassadors and other interested people to visit the long-isolated empire in 1335
AE
. Ember accompanied the group as the charr ambassador, and the travelers witnessed Kasmeer Meade cloak their
airship
with
mesmer
magic so they could fly past the vigilant
Risen
Dead Ship
armada blocking access to the southern seas near
Orr
. Upon arriving on
Seitung Province
, Ember was among the delegates welcoming the Commander and
Gorrik
who had been released from Canthan prison after Kasmeer had explained that they were no threats to the empire.
Ember later
participated
in the joint assault on the maddened Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
and the
Void
horde at the
Harvest Temple
. The delegates' visit would end up improving Canthan and Central Tyrian relations, setting the stage for further collaborations between the various nations after the demise of Soo-Won, the last of the original generation of Elder Dragons.
Visions of Eternity
Act 1
Ember and several other charr from the High Legions accompanied the Pact Commander and their companions from the
Tyrian Alliance
to the distant island realm of
Castora
after they had learned about the
Inquest
planning to do something in the newly discovered region in 1338
AE
. While the Commander's party traveled aboard
Sayida the Sly
's ship
The Dark Leviathan
, Ember traveled aboard
Canach
's recently purchased ship, the
Martingale
, and experienced various dangers such as hostile marine life, fire, and a magical fog, during the
weeks-long voyage
. Just as the two ships were about to reach Castoran shores, an unseen creature suddenly attacked the
Martingale
and destroyed it. The Commander managed to save most crew members who would continue the rest of the voyage aboard
The Dark Leviathan
until they made landfall on
Shipwreck Strand
.
Act 2
As the Tyrian Alliance pushed deeper inland into the depths of
Starlit Weald
, they split up into multiple groups setting up camps alongside the river while scouting ahead and facing aggressive native wildlife, enchanted weapons, and Inquest along the way. The Commander
ran into
Ember while checking up on the alliance members to learn what they had discovered during their scouting. Recalling the Commander's efficiency, Ember asked them to plant Ash Legion listening devices to the perimeter of the
Command Complex
, the Inquest's base of operations in the region, so the alliance could listen in on the Inquest and anyone else who might be moving around out there.
The Only Way
The Tyrian Alliance pushed deeper inland into the depths of
Eternity's Garden
in pursuit of the Inquest in 1339
AE
. Upon establishing the alliance's base of operations at
Pilgrim's Rest
, Ember tasked Ash Legion members
Canria Ragesplitter
and
Rytandor Blightcaller
to scout the nearby area for Inquest activity and other useful intel they might find. While hanging out in the camp, Ember chatted with charr from other legions and joked with an Iron Legion member if it was wise to bring a tank to the nearby lake when it would be difficult to get across. She also commented on the strangely thick magic north of their location as well as the lack of Dougal Keane on the mission.
Ember occasionally went to raid the Inquest lab at
Artificer's Islet
for weapons caches to hinder the Inquest's progress.

Historical dialogue
At
Warrior's Crown
I'd rather be cleaving a dragon minion to pieces, but I need to see this through to the end. Since I'm partially responsible for this gathering, I'm obligated to sit through it.
How are you responsible for this?
I was part of the expedition who ventured into the depth of
Ascalon ruins
to retrieve the Claw of Khan-Ur. Without it, the
humans
wouldn't have gotten the charr to the table.
Impressive.

peace
with the humans?
The brass says peace will free us up to fight the
Flame Legion
, but I have seen and killed things that put us and the humans at risk of extinction. We've got dragons to fight—together.
I see.
Hopefully it won't take too long.

Gallery
Ember's historical appearance at
Warrior's Crown
.
Ember's historical appearance as a
Vigil
member.
Notes
Ember had a different appearance during the
personal story
mission
Minister's Defense
, and she initially appeared in her
Guild Initiative
clothes in
Drizzlewood Coast
. Both bugs were addressed in the
August 11th, 2020
update.

in

. Since
the Icebrood Saga

.

Jungle Explorer Outfit
.

,

,

,

, and wields

and

.

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
and
Living World Season 2
.
Emissary Vorpp
Race
Asura
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Locations
The Queen's Address
(
The Crown Pavilion
)
Clockwork Invasion
(
Gendarran Fields
,
The Crown Pavilion
)
Emissary Vorpp
is a well respected member of
Rata Sum
, and the
Arcane Council
's point person according to
Marjory Delaqua
.
Taimi
often brings him up in relation to
Zojja
as the two taking care of her, though what relation he has to Zojja directly remains unclear.
Biography
Living World Season 1
Clockwork Chaos
Emissary Vorpp was among the
emissaries
sent from
Rata Sum
for the
Queen's Jubilee
celebration in 1326
AE
, where he showed fascination for the
Watchknights
' design. When
Scarlet Briar
crashed
the closing ceremony
, the
Seraph
,
Pact Commander
, and
Rox
requested his assistance in reverse engineering the portals that Scarlet was using to send forces out from within the barricaded
Crown Pavilion
. During this time, he explained Scarlet's history with the
Asuran
Colleges
and remarked disdain for her character and achievements and dismissing her claims of seeing the
Eternal Alchemy
, believing that no one could understand everything, especially since it's beyond asura geniuses.
Battle for Lion's Arch (as Vorpp)
To further investigate Scarlet Briar's plans, Marjory Delaqua pulled in several favors, including Vorpp. Together with the Pact Commander and
Kasmeer Meade
, the four investigated Scarlet's past actions from the
Thaumanova Reactor
explosion up to her
Twisted Marionette
and the discoveries in her
secret lair
. Through this, the group deduced that Scarlet's next attack would be on
Lion's Arch
itself.
After Scarlet launched her invasion on Lion's Arch, Vorpp returned as his role as Emissary, this time assisting the refugees of Lion's Arch in
Bouldermouth Vale
's
evacuation camp
.
Living World Season 2
Seeds of Truth (as Vorpp)
While the Pact Commander was investigating
Caithe
's path, they learned that Vorpp was the head asura that experimented upon the
Secondborn
sylvari. During his krewe's experimentation many of the captured Secondborns died. Fought Caithe and
Faolain
, but ultimately lost thus leading to the release of the Secondborns.
Lion's Arch Rebuilt (as Vorpp)
After the reconstruction of Lion's Arch, Vorpp left the
Bouldermouth Vale Refugee Camp
, though has not been seen since.

======================================================================
PAGE: Empress Ihn
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Empress_Ihn
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
,
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Empress Ihn
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Outreach
(
Seitung Province
)
Prologue: The Tyrian Alliance
(
Divinity's Reach
,
Lion's Arch
,
Black Citadel
,
The Grove
,
Hoelbrak
,
Rata Sum
)
Strangers among Lands
(
Postern Ward
,
Harvest Den
,
Harvest Shore
,
Festering Basin
)
Organization
Tyrian Alliance
“
Please afford me the respect my throne merits. I do know sarcasm when I hear it.
—
Empress Ihn
Her Imperial Majesty
Ihn
,
Empress
regnant of the
Empire of the Dragon
, is a direct descendant of the
Canthan imperial family
founded by the first Canthan emperor
Kaineng Tah
. She is also the half-sister of
Yu Joon
and aunt of
Yu Sung
. Ihn cares a lot about the Canthan people and wants the best for them.
After the forming of the
Tyrian Alliance
, she sanctions
ritualists
to spread their teachings beyond Cantha in the spirit of global collaboration.
Biography
Early years
Ihn was born to the
Imperial Family
of the Empire of the Dragon as the daughter of
Emperor Taejeong
.
Following her father's passing, she became the second
empress
regnant of
Cantha
after her great-grandmother
Empress Haebaragi
.
Ihn would soften some of her father's policies, choosing a more openminded and diplomatic approach to solving conflicts as she understood that change was inevitable to ensure Cantha's progress and welfare in the future.
Ihn also learned that the innovator and head of
Xunlai Jade
,
Yu Joon
, was her half-sister from the emperor's extramarital affair with the potter and jade trader
Yu Dal-Rae
.
Despite some tension between the half-sisters as Joon's influence and power began growing due to her company's amassed wealth, Ihn and Joon appeared to get along thanks to the innovations Joon had provided to Canthans with her jade tech.
However, Ihn would sometimes remind Joon to show her proper courtly respect as empress.
In 1330
CC
(1320
AE
), the empress certified Joon's Xunlai Jade acquiring exclusive
dragonjade
mining rights at
the Jade Sea
as well as assets and land rights from the Silver Dawn Mining Group and that the rights would be held for a period of no less than ninety-nine years.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Just like her father and grandfather before her, Ihn learned the secret that the imperial court advisor
Navan
was actually the legendary
saltspray dragon
Kuunavang
in disguise.
When the construction of Xunlai Jade's
Yong Reactor
was complete and the inspection was scheduled, Joon revealed its true purpose to Ihn and the highest ranking ministers in the Canthan government: the reactor was being retrofitted to house the
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
, who had once saved Cantha from the
Great Tsunami
, to siphon
magic
off of her into Joon's dragonjade tech with the Elder Dragon's consent. This knowledge was quickly compartmentalized and classified by the
Ministry of Security
, in coordination with the
Ministry of Intelligence
and select members of the government and the Imperial Family.
End of Dragons
Act 1
Ihn on her throne with
Navan
about to
receive
the
Krytan
delegation.
In 1845
CC
(1335
AE
),
pirates
calling themselves the
Aetherblades
suddenly
appeared
over
Seitung Province
to spread chaos with their air raids, followed by the arrival of the Elder Dragon
Aurene
and two
Tyrian
trespassers—the
asura
Gorrik
and the
Pact Commander
—who had washed ashore amidst Aetherblade
airship
wreckage. While the pair were detained for questioning in
Seitung Prison
, the Imperial Court began considering how to respond to the incident. Realizing the opportunity as a pivotal time for the empire, Joon argued in favor of opening Cantha's borders to foreign visitors, eventually persuading Ihn and the court to her side.
Ihn sent a message to
Kryta
informing the northern kingdom about Cantha reopening its borders and inviting Kryta to reestablish diplomatic relations. The empress also used the opportunity to inquire the Krytan throne about foreign airships appearing in Canthan skies as well as the arrival of a dragon and two foreigners on Seitung's shores.
Queen Jennah
sent a delegation headed by Ambassador
Kasmeer Meade
to enter negotiations with the Imperial Court and assure them that Kryta had nothing to do with the Aetherblade invasion. The delegation flew an airship to Cantha, getting past the Unchained
Risen
armada patrolling the
Sea of Sorrows
by cloaking themselves with Kasmeer's
mesmer
spell.
Kasmeer was eventually invited to
meet
Ihn in the
Royal Court
and was accompanied by the asuran researcher
Taimi
and the Commander. The trio witnessed a heated exchange between Joon and
Minister Li Ha-Eung
about Xunlai Jade's latest jade mechs and if they were safe to use in front of the imperial throne. Ihn took her half-sister's side in the argument, reminding Li that Joon had been pivotal for Cantha's progress and had earned the empress's trust. However, she also made sure to remind Joon of her place in the hierarchy.
Moving her attention to the delegates, Ihn began with courtly etiquette wishing Queen Jennah well although she was also quick to point out the problem the Aetherblades posed to Cantha while wondering if Kryta was involved in the incident in any way. Minister Li interrupted the exchange, trying to sway the empress to see his point of view that Tyrians could not be trusted and had ulterior motives for visiting Cantha. Ihn took Joon's suggestion to have Joon and Li continue their debate in an adjacent chamber in private so the empress's conversation with the delegates could proceed without further interruptions.
Kasmeer formally introduced one of the former prisoners of Seitung in her company as the Commander, making Ihn inquire more about the title until the imperial advisor Navan informed the empress of the Commander's relation to the
Pact
which Ihn had heard about. Ihn accepted Kasmeer's proposal that the Tyrian delegation would repay the Imperial Court's mercy by handling the Aetherblade threat for them with the Ministry of Security's help, and told the delegates to speak to Minister Li about the details of this collaboration. Having said her piece, Ihn concluded the meeting and retired to her chamber with Navan.
Act 2
When Joon's protege
Mai Trin
had surrendered to the Commander's party and was revealed to have been the original leader of the Aetherblades before being ousted in a mutiny by her First Mate
Ankka
, Ihn's advisor Navan expressed worry if Mai Trin could be trusted given her past criminal associations. Kasmeer assured the advisor that the party would keep an eye on Mai Trin while the pirate was aiding them in the Aetherblade investigation, and countered the question by asking Navan if Joon could be trusted. Although Navan admitted that Joon's relationship with the Aetherblades was a concerning matter that Joon would have to answer for, Empress Ihn was certain that her half-sister was not a traitor to the throne.
Act 3
Upon learning of Soo-Won's sudden maddened rampage causing the
destruction
of
Yong Reactor
and that the Commander had been sighted at the reactor just before the incident, Navan and Ihn expressed concern over the Commander's actions while Minister Li and Joon advocated for arresting the Commander's party and deporting all foreigners from Canthan soil for acts of terrorism. After hearing Kasmeer steadfastly vouching for the Commander's innocence, Navan proposed a discreet meeting at Nika's Blade restaurant in New Kaineng to discuss the matter face-to-face on Ihn's behalf as the empress did not want anyone to behave rashly before assessing the diplomatically sensitive situation first and if the Commander could be trusted.
Act 5
After the Commander had proven their innocence and ended the threat posed by the
Void
via the
death
of Soo-Won, Cantha's borders reopened. To strengthen Cantha's diplomatic relations with other nations, Empress Ihn began planning her first trip abroad to visit Queen Jennah in
Divinity's Reach
in Kryta. According to
Mi-Rae
's holonews report, Ihn would be accompanied by Krytan Ambassador Kasmeer Meade and Acting Minister of Security
Cho Min
who had temporarily taken the position of the deposed and imprisoned Minister Li who had been found guilty of high treason. After learning how Mai Trin had sacrificed her life to save New Kaineng from a Void outbreak in the Aetherblade attack in the Yong Reactor, Ihn bestowed her the posthumous title Hero of Kaineng for her exceptional display of bravery.
What Lies Within
Despite Mi-Rae's earlier announcement suggesting otherwise, Cho Min ultimately did not accompany Ihn and Kasmeer on the overseas diplomatic trip. During an
outing
with Detective Rama and their friends in the
Red Duck Tea House
in New Kaineng months after the death of Soo-Won, Min mentioned that the empress was visiting Divinity's Reach and had apparently departed from Cantha sometime within a few weeks before the tea house date.
Lunar New Year 2024
After a successful diplomatic visit to Queen Jennah, Empress Ihn sent Navan to Divinity's Reach as the official Canthan ambassador in 1336
AE
so Navan could observe Kryta's celebration of the new year in hopes of revitalizing the two nations' shared history. While participating in the
Lunar New Year
festival, Navan met the Commander, and the two talked about Cantha's current political climate as well as the differences between Kryta's Lunar New Year and Cantha's
Dragon Festival
. Navan explained that the Imperial Court had been busy as there had been a rush of diplomatic and trade matters for Ihn and the court to negotiate with Cantha's borders reopening.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Empress Ihn at the
Tyrian Alliance
meeting.
Sometime within the past four years prior to the
Tyrian Alliance
's founding, the
cat
criminal mastermind
Ms. Jeeves
had supposedly left a hairball on Empress Ihn's throne as a sign of her prowess as a cat burglar.
In 1337
AE
after multiple hostile incursions from
the Mists
had taken place across the world and caused chaos in their wake, Queen Jennah sent invitations to multiple nations and organizations to attend a meeting in
Lion's Arch
with the intention of forming a Tyrian Alliance so the members of this alliance could combat future threats together. Although Ihn had been advised to send a liaison in her stead, she wanted to attend the
meeting
personally to show the face of the empire. She was accompanied by the
tengu
Ayumi
, now acting as the leader of the
Kestrels
who represented Canthans living outside of New Kaineng. Upon being reunited with the Pact Commander, Ihn remarked how Jennah had been instrumental in Cantha's integration into the wider world and had managed to attract an impressive number of delegates to the gathering. According to Ayumi, the empress had made it her priority to unite the empire despite the thick line between developed Cantha and the rural communities and lingering anxiety surrounding jade tech.
As the representatives of the major powers each had a floor to inform their fellow delegates about the issues in their respective nations,
Councillor Ludo
of
Rata Sum
admitted that Cantha's advancements on asuran designs had ignited the asura's competitive spirit to outdo Xunlai's tech. She motioned to eliminate any restrictions on admittance into the
asuran colleges
and a focus on global information exchange, while Ihn approved of the motion and encouraged friendly competitive spirit between nations for the betterment of all. During her speech Ihn admitted that she wanted to better integrate Cantha into international politics as well as use the opportunity to enlist archivists to update Cantha's out-of-date records on historical events abroad.
When Jennah revealed that the unknown assailants from the Mists had been
demons
called the
Kryptis
, the delegates demanded to know how the queen had discerned this information that had eluded the rest of them. The escalating arguments stopped abruptly when a being whom Jennah's advisor,
Countess Anise
, identified as the
Seer
Isgarren
of the
Astral Ward
made a grand entrance followed by his
norn
assistant
Frode
. The pair quickly summarized the purpose of the Astral Ward as the secret protectors of the world of Tyria and that the Kryptis threat had already been dealt with thanks to the assistance of the Commander whom they referred to as the
Wayfinder
. The reason for the ward finally revealing themselves in public was due to them suffering major losses in the war against the Kryptis and because they needed help from the other factions. According to Isgarren, the Kryptis were just one of many threats now turning their attention to Tyria as the end of the old dragon cycle had exposed the world and its riches to many interested and hostile beings dwelling in the Mists.
Once the meeting had calmed down enough, the Astral Ward representatives suggested sending members of the Tyrian Alliance, including the Commander,
Caithe
, and
Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
of the
Ash Legion
to recruit the isolationist
Lowland Kodan
of
Janthir
to bolster the alliance's numbers and better prepare for the dangerous days ahead. Empress Ihn and Councillor Ludo used the break to have a conversation about future collaboration between their nations and agreed to mutual consultation.
Some time later, the alliance council was summoned to
another meeting
in Lion's Arch when the Commander's party had returned from Janthir with the Lowland Kodan's leader
Stoic Alder
in tow. The party and Alder explained how
titans
, an old foe from the Mists who had once spread chaos across Tyria, had appeared in Janthir to terrorize the lowlanders and spread corruption even though titans had supposedly been destroyed centuries ago. When the delegates tried to figure out the reason for the titans' sudden return, the
necromancer
Livia
, who was sworn to the throne of Kryta, crashed the meeting. She called for utilization of the
Scepter of Orr
, an ancient Seer weapon designed to control or manipulate creatures from the Mists, which she had once wielded and which the Astral Ward had locked in their vaults since. No one had a straight answer about whether or not the scepter might actually work, but a few of the council were willing to risk it to calm the looming threat as quickly as possible. However, the room could not come to an agreement on what to do, so the Commander called for a vote. The majority, including Ihn, ultimately voted on not using the scepter due to its potential risks. As a result, Anise ordered Caithe, Malice, and the Commander to return to Janthir with Stoic Alder to do more research and determine the scope of the titan threat before deciding on whether to send reinforcements to Janthir to aid the lowlanders and the Astral Ward stationed there.
Absolution
In the months following the Tyrian Alliance's founding, Ihn continued corresponding with Councillor Ludo to strengthen their partnership and sharing of knowledge. Ihn's niece,
Yu Sung
, drew a simple illustration of the empress and the councillor which she gifted to Ludo who viewed the gesture as an auspicious omen of Ihn and Ludo's lucrative partnership.
By 1338
AE
, the empress had learned of the Tyrian Alliance expedition's
breakthrough
into the ruins of the fallen
mursaat
city of
Bava Nisos
which contained the source of the titan invasion. Upon learning of the many mursaat
ghosts
haunting Bava Nisos and its
outskirts
, she sent the
ritualist
Kyung
to help the restless spirits pass on to the Mists and thus make the alliance's campaign easier in the region.
As Ludo traveled to Bava Nisos to aid the alliance's push deeper into the ruined city, she managed to convince Ihn to lend several
siege turtles
for the assault. The turtles' heavy firepower came in handy to
break through
the
magical
barriers protecting the membrane which
Saevus Saxum
, the massive titan who had merged with Bava Nisos to turn it into his extended body, had grown around himself to shield his exposed organs.
Some time later, Ihn was present in another Tyrian Alliance meeting where Countess Anise and the Pact Commander had returned to inform the delegates about the details of their
successful mission
to end the titan threat in the ruins of Bava Nisos. The empress attended a subsequent victory celebration held in Lion's Arch, congratulating the Commander on a job well done. During the ensuing conversation, Ihn revealed how Kuunavang and Soo-Won had believed in the Commander's quality to have a positive impact on the world, and that she knew better than to question the two dragons' wisdom in this matter. The empress and Ayumi had also been in talks with the
Dominion of Winds
's tengu such as the royal messenger
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
about the Dominion possibly opening up to the rest of the world one day. Kalidris expressed hope that the talks between the two nations' leaders would bear fruit and that the Dominion's elders would agree to open the gates within their lifetimes.
Visions of Eternity
As collaboration between the members of the Tyrian Alliance deepened, Canthan, Elonian, and Krytan delegates traveled to each other's nations to teach their respective cultures to one another in 1338
AE
. Empress Ihn
contributed
to the initiative by sending Kyung's sister
Sang-Mi
to Divinity's Reach to teach locals the ways of ritualists. She also potentially sent a personal letter to the Pact Commander to seek Sang-Mi out for further discussions about the craft that could be of use to
necromancers
.

Trivia
Ihn is
voiced
by
Tina Huang
.
In development, Ihn was named Empress Yin.
Ihn, alternatively spelled
In
and
Yin
(
인
), has various meanings in
Korean Hanja
.
印
means "mark" or "
seal
".
仁
means "benevolence" which may be a virtue required for a ruler.
Ihn does not wear shoes, just like
Queen Jennah
, which has drawn the scholarly attention of
Steward Gixx
.
Ihn is referred to as The Most Radiant Empress Ihn in legal documentation.
In the time of
Guild Wars Factions
, the title of
"empress"
was bestowed only through marriage and had little real power in the patriarchal Canthan society.
The only other Canthan empress whose name was originally known until the release of
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
was
Tahmu
.
See also
Canthan imperial family tree
References

CATEGORIES: Story characters

======================================================================

Engineer Brizzi
Race
Asura
Profession
Amalgam
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Beneath the Canopy
(
Starlit Weald
)
Organization
Inquest
Engineer Brizzi
is an

Euryale
Race
Charr
Profession

Image(s)
Euryale welding a staff.
Euryale
is a
charr
that is originally part of a charr
player character
's
warband
. She takes part in the
battle
against
Duke Barradin
's ghostly forces in the
Village of Smokestead
. If the player does not choose her as their sparring partner in their
biography
, she dies during the battle. If she is the player's sparring partner, she survives and helps them along the rest of their
personal story
and be in the player's
home instance
.
She is an
elementalist
, and is described as loyal and honest, although she's also stubborn, bad-tempered and protective.

Trivia
On several occasions Euryale displays some self-consciousness with regards to her use of magic. When discussing Soure Doomsday she states that spellcasters that want to be Flame Legion make other magic users look bad, and later in the story she refers to herself as a spellcaster everybody's afraid of, as a negative point against herself.
The name Euryale means "Far-Roaming." It was also the name of one of the Gorgons in Greek mythology.

======================================================================
PAGE: Everlee
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Everlee
======================================================================

Everlee
was a
human
mercenary working under
Logan Thackeray
in
Ascalon
to protect human merchant caravans that supplied
Ebonhawke
. In 1319
AE
, he was in the group that fought with
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
's forces, taken over by
Rytlock Brimstone
, both of whom were then ambushed by
ogres
. He died in the conflict, and her body was burned in a makeshift pyre by Logan afterwards.

======================================================================
PAGE: Evon Gnashblade
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Evon_Gnashblade
======================================================================

This article is about the owner of the Black Lion Trading Company.  For a lookalike, see
"Evon" Gnashblade
. For his birthday
special event
, see
Evon Gnashblade's "Birthday" Celebration
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
,
Living World Season 2
,
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
,
Living World Season 4
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Captain Evon Gnashblade
Race
Charr
Locations
Eastern Ward
(
Lion's Arch
)
Trader's Forum
(
Memory of Old Lion's Arch
)
Organization
Ash Legion
Black Lion Trading Company
Image(s)
Evon's original appearance.
“
The same principles apply to both trade and combat! A stout heart and sufficient supplies will always carry the day! I love the smell of commerce in the morning!
—
Captain Evon Gnashblade
Captain Evon Gnashblade
is the owner and proprietor of the
Black Lion Trading Company
in
Lion's Arch
. Ever ambitious and cunning, he uses his
Ash Legion
training with the intention to dominate the marketing battlefield and deliver the finest and most unique items to his customers.
Biography
Early years
As a soldier of the
High Legions
, Evon Gnashblade first came to
Lion's Arch
at the age of 20 as part of an
Ash Legion
operation to extort the people of the city, under the banner of the
Black Lion Trading Company
. He was by all accounts a dependable soldier who never disobeyed orders—until that day. When he raised concerns with his
legionnaire
,
Aria Gnashfang
, believing that attempting to intimidate the
pirates
of Lion's Arch would not be fruitful, the disagreement
led to a fight
during which Aria attempted to kill Evon. However, Evon was faster and killed the legionnaire instead, becoming the new head of the operation. The next day, the Black Lion Trading Company was established in earnest as a legitimate business.
Under Evon's leadership, the company focused on dominating the marketing battlefield and delivering the finest and most unique items to its customers. Due to Evon's personal preferences,
gems
became the desired currency for the company's most valued items.
As the company grew, Evon hired the
sylvari
Sareb
as his
aide-de-camp
, and gradually extended his reach throughout
Central Tyria
. Sareb has suggested that Evon left his legion to found the company as an independent merchant, but it is unknown if Evon truly cut ties with the Ash Legion for good.
Living World Season 1
Evon's campaign poster during
Cutthroat Politics
.
During elections to appoint a new
Captain's Council
member after the
assassination
of
Captain Theo Ashford
by
Captain Mai Trin
in 1326
AE
, Evon purchased a vessel which made him eligible as a candidate. He attempted to secure a trade agreement with the
Master of Peace
and the
Zephyr Sanctum
to gain a seat in the council, and went on a campaign to discredit his rival candidate
Ellen Kiel
while simultaneously receiving as much criticism from Ellen's supporters in return.
Despite his efforts, Evon narrowly lost out to Ellen who was elected instead. He advocated for Lion's Arch investing in better defenses in case
Scarlet Briar
, who had been menacing
Tyria
throughout the year, decided to attack the city, but his warnings fell on deaf ears.
When
Scarlet's Alliance
invaded Lion's Arch
, Evon had adventurers help him
escort
dolyaks
carrying valuable goods through an
asura gate
. To protect his investments, he shut down the gate from the other side, trapping citizens in the warzone. After the destruction of
Lion's Arch
and its subsequent occupation by Scarlet's forces, Evon settled in a refugee camp in the northern hills of
Gendarran Fields
where the
Vigil
was protecting and providing for survivors as well as planning a counterattack.
Under the orders of Captain Kiel, Evon was
conscripted
into the
Lionguard
under General Emergency Statute 14 and forced, along with all other Black Lion Trading Company merchants, to act as a deputy quartermaster for the Lionguard under penalty of death. He was then
approached
by a
golem
named
Heal-o-Tron
who requested and begged for a combat chassis and an energy amplifier in order to aid in the assault against Scarlet. After some thought Evon helped install the combat chassis on the golem, thus allowing it to participate in the
retaking
of Lion's Arch alongside other adventurers. However, Evon had taken note of the golem's ID number and discreetly researched it, tracing it back to the Black Lion's rival, the
Consortium
, and the incidents involving the settlers at
Southsun Cove
. These findings made Evon realize the origins of the newly named Hero-Tron and the means to use the golem to his advantage.
Living World Season 2
After Scarlet Briar had been
slain
and Lion's Arch had been reclaimed, Evon returned to the ruined city along with other survivors. He provided the concrete for the city's new look as it was rebuilt with new architecture and better defenses. He
blackmailed
Hero-Tron to act as his spy in the Captain's Council by threatening to expose the golem's history with the Consortium if it did not obey his commands. Using his amassed funds, Evon decided to put all of Tyria's experts in armor repair on salary, allowing Tyrians to repair their armor at no cost from that point onward.
During the rebuilding of Lion's Arch, Evon was reportedly seen in two locations at once, which made some locals like
Wild-Eye Miller
spread conspiracy theories about Evon having a twin brother named Tarvan with whom he communicated telepathically to control the city. However, unbeknownst to most, the culprit was actually a charr
lookalike
who had taken to posing as Evon, likely in order to get free drinks in the city.
Evon used the profits he had made from his temporary job as a Lionguard quartermaster to build a better vault for his goods and turned it into his new headquarters. When he was not making investments, he took frequent walks around the rebuilt Lion's Arch and observed local power players such as the Consortium and the
Order of Whispers
gaining ground in the city. Frustrated by losing his seat in the Captain's Council to Ellen and not having a statue built in his image for all the work he had done for Lion's Arch, Evon used his remaining campaign posters from the candidate trials to unceremoniously decorate the trash can in his office. Believing that the Lionguard would not be able to protect his investments well enough, he considered hiring mercenaries to watch over his shipments.
Path of Fire
As Central Tyria made contact with the
Free City of Amnoon
following a sudden influx of
Elonian
refugees in Lion's Arch in 1330
AE
, Evon sent representatives of the Black Lion Trading Company to Amnoon. Along with their rival Consortium representatives, they began negotiating with the business tycoon
Zalambur
for access to the Elonian customer network. Zalambur considered their terms in exchange for them providing him with Tyrian goods that he could not find locally.
To boost his image, Evon even had an
action figure
made in his image during this time.
Living World Season 4
The Captain's Council of Lion's Arch and
Queen Jennah
of
Kryta
collaborated in bringing back the
Festival of the Four Winds
to provide supplies and funds for the displaced
Zephyrites
who had once aided Lion's Arch in its time of need. Learning of the council's plans through his golem spy, Evon pulled the supplies together and accompanied Ellen Kiel to the Labyrinthine Cliffs where they welcomed visitors and also talked with one another. He refused to take part in the festivities himself, instead offering sound economic advice to Ellen about how to handle the trade renegotiations with the Zephyrites to make the percentage better for Lion's Arch.
During their conversations Ellen revealed that she had not forced Evon to aid the Lionguard out of spite during Scarlet's invasion of Lion's Arch but to keep the Black Lion Trading Company in business after the Captain's Council had considered breaking up the company because of Evon's actions with the asura gate. Surprised, Evon questioned whether Ellen saving his company had been an altruistic act or for more selfish reasons, but he was eventually satisfied by her answering that the financial well-being of Lion's Arch was tied to how well Evon's company was faring. Evon let Ellen know in return that he knew about her affair with Zalambur. Although he refused to let her know how he had learned of the affair, he voiced his concerns over whether the relationship would affect their business arrangements, only to hear Ellen reassure him that everything would proceed as usual.
Janthir Wilds
Sometime around 1338
AE
, Evon's vaults were broken into by a
mixed posse of animals
led by the feline criminal mastermind
Ms. Jeeves
who intended to steal some of the charr tycoon's valuables.
Chief Inspector Lutze
discovered the cat's involvement in the heist, but Captain Magnus did not believe her story due to it sounding unbelievable.

Notes
Evon's default location is behind his desk in
The Vaults
, but he frequently leaves to take a walk around
Lion's Arch
.
During his walk through the city, he'll briefly stop at five different locations, in the following order:
Order of Whispers Headquarters
Crow's Nest Tavern (Captain's Council)
Trading Post
in
Trader's Forum
Lion's Court
Bank
in Trader's Forum.
Trophies
associated with Evon are used for various
legendary weapon
collections
:
Dance for Evon Gnashblade
for
The Moot III: The Energizer
.
Evon's Dreams of Economic Dominance
for
The Dreamer III: The Lover
.
Prank Evon Gnashblade
for
Quip III: Chaos Gun
.
Evon was the only vendor of
Recipe: Maintenance Oil Station
.
Bug
:
If players are crafting
The Dreamer
, but have not completed the entire quest chain and obtained the
Mini Red Spark
, Evon Gnashblade will not prompt the relevant dialogue for the legendary collection.
Trivia
Evon is
voiced
by
Sam Riegel
.
During the rebuilding of Lion's Arch, Evon appeared in two locations at the same time with one Evon wearing his new outfit while the other one wore his old clothes. The discrepancy was noted by players on the Guild Wars 2 forums, and developers later explained the existence of two Evons by including an
"Evon" Gnashblade
lookalike in the rebuilt Lion's Arch and having
Wild-Eye Miller
suggest a conspiracy theory revolving around the pair.
Evon's current look features the following armor pieces:
Ascalonian Performer Mantle
Heritage Greatcoat
Ascalonian Performer Pants
Cabalist Boots
The quote by Evon, "I love the smell of commerce in the morning," is a possible reference to the quote, "I love the smell of napalm in the morning," from the movie
Apocalypse Now
.
He disappeared when
Knife Tail Gang
entered
The Vaults
. However, one of the developers said he had lost his airship back to
Lion's Arch
during
Festival of the Four Winds
. He returned in the
August 27, 2019
update.
With the release of the
Largos Fin Backpack Glider Combo
, a blog post written by Evon himself claims that he has no
largos
clientele.
His
birthday celebration
occurred during the
Black Friday
sale.
He wanders around Arborstone in Cantha with two NPCs. He is unnamed, not selectable and not able to be interacted with in this location, but his appearance is an exact match. He wanders from the portal to Lion's Arch to around the area with the bank, trading post and exchange specialist NPCs, and returns to the portal.
See also
Short Story: Evon Gnashblade Disembarks
Associated items

During Gates of Maguuma
I'm tasked with recovering and crating up stray magical artifacts from the wreckage of the Tower of Nightmares.
Where are you bringing the crates?
For now, we're taking them to Fort Salma, where the
Seraph
can keep them under guard. Eventually, we'll take them back to the Priory.
I see. I'll leave you to it.
Why bother recovering these artifacts?
We're trying to take any potentially dangerous magical items away from what's left of the Toxic Alliance, though we also want to study these artifacts.
I'll leave you to your work then.

If norn:

Else:
Of course. Ask, and I shall answer.
What qualities are associated with Wolf?
Wolf is dedicated to brotherhood and loyalty. He is the bond that cannot be broken. He keeps us together in the face of impossible odds and reminds us that we need never fight alone.
I have other questions about Wolf.
I have questions about you as well.
(Same as "I'm more interested in you." below)
I've learned all I need to. Good-bye.
Is Wolf a male or female spirit?
Wolf is a masculine spirit, and so we call him our brother, our son, our father, or ever our uncle when we ask for his blessing.
I have other questions about Wolf.
Thank you. I must be going now.
What kind of offering do you give to Wolf?
Wolf welcomes any prey taken during a hunt, but especially the prey that has proven challenging enough to require it to be slain by a pack.
I have other questions about Wolf.
Speaking of the hunt, I am late for one of my own.
I'm more interested in you.
I'm a servant of Wolf, and I stand before him, eager to prove that I am worthy of the calling he has given me.
How did you become the speaker for Wolf?
When I reached manhood, I heard Wolf's howl strongly in my heart, so I went to the hall to fully learn his ways. In time, Wolf chose me to be his speaker.
Amazing.
Yes, one cannot be anything but awed when a Spirit calls to them.

A mighty achievement. Good luck.

, a beloved and wise Wolf speaker who died far before his time. He was still teaching me the responsibilities of the calling when it happened. I have much to live up to.
That's a sad story. I have to be going, though.
May you be an example to others.

Fel Vaerdenmer
Race
Human
Profession
Elementalist
Level
80
Rank
Veteran
Locations
Caer Aval
(
Fort Trinity
)
Arborstone
(
Cantha
)
Organization
Pact
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
I can sail a bantam in a full hurricane. I can sail anything! Stick a toothpick on a turtle and I'll sail it to Cantha before dawn!
—
Fel Vaerdenmer
Fel Vaerdenmer
is a seafarer and the father of
Benn Tenstrikes
. When he saw his son join the fight against the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, he and his wife
Merri Sunbower
decided to join the
Pact
too.
Biography
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Fel and his
wife
provide assistance to the
Commander
in completing the
Order of Whispers
' plan to capture the Risen navy to perform a covert strike against Zhaitan's forces.
They later reappear at the
victory celebration
at
Fort Trinity
.
Interim years
After losing contact with their son Benn following the Pact's costly war against the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
in the
Heart of Maguuma
in 1328
AE
, Fel and Merri got their hands on their own
airship
, mostly salvaged from parts, so they could search for their son. They named her the
Sun Chaser
after Merri's family since Fel had named their previous ship after his family. However, Benn turned up safe and returned home some time after the campaign in Maguuma had concluded, much to his parents' relief.
End of Dragons
Fel and Merri traveled to
Arborstone
after it had been renovated and upgraded into an international commercial and travel hub following
Cantha
reopening its borders in 1335
AE
. Much to their delight, they ran into the Pact Commander there and exchanged news of what had happened to them since they had last interacted with the Commander.
Once Fel and Merri had read every book in the library, soaked in the pools, and strolled in the market, they began chatting with local officials about any possible changes to Canthan airspace so they could bring their airship to the continent.
Locations
Cantha
Arborstone
(at the inn after completing
Globalization
)

Straits of Devastation
Fort Trinity
Caer Aval
(only after completing the personal story)

projectiles

to allies and curing

Ferghen the Tracker

Ferghen the Tracker
is an aging
norn
hunter and tracker who also acts as a merchant from time to time.
Biography
Ferghen grew up to become one of the best trackers among the
norn
and befriended a young
Eir Stegalkin
in the past. As he got older, he got lost in the forest and let his
dolyaks
wander off, which earned him some ridicule from his peers who were aware that he also liked to make up stories.
In 1325
, Ferghen noticed that the
minotaurs
in
Wayfarer Foothills
were acting strangely, and saw the
Minotaur Spirit
in corporeal form up in a tree. He asked for the help of Eir and the
Slayer of Issormir
to protect his caravan on its way to the
Heart of the Wolf
from the minotaurs before asking them to track down the Spirit itself.
He later joined the
Pact
as one of their expert hunters and trackers in the war against the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
. On a mission scouting for a
Searing Cauldron
found in
Orr
, Ferghen fell victim to the
Risen
, becoming one himself. As a Risen, he hunted down and killed his entire team one by one, leaving
Kekt
the last to be killed. Ferghen was eventually taken down by the Pact Commander, but unlike other Risen he was able to come back after being killed once and had to be put down a second time.
Story involvement
Personal story

Wild Spirits
Chapter 8: Victory or Death

======================================================================
PAGE: Ferroc Torchtail
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ferroc_Torchtail
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Edge of Destiny
.
Ferroc Torchtail
was a
charr
from the
Iron Legion
. Originally serving under
Korrak Blacksnout
, he was one of the few charr who survived the avalanche set off by
Logan Thackeray
and, unlike most of the rest, did not join in with
Rytlock Brimstone
in pursuing the
human
mercenaries. Though this had saved his life, as those who joined Rytlock were all killed by the humans or ambushed by
ogres
, he became a
gladium
and was treated as a coward for surviving.
A year later in 3020
AE
, he was sent up north to
Grothmar Valley
to join a unit investigating the mountain in the middle of
a lake
which had began acting up. His superior officers wanted to investigate why there were earthquakes. Ferroc, seeing the earthquakes first hand, believed the mountain was in fact a volcano. When giant, strange creatures began to climb down the mountain, he and his new unit were ordered to charge and kill the creatures; Ferroc, driven by instinct to survive, marched slower than the rest until fear took over and made him flee.
It was then that he witnessed the mountain rise up, and reveal itself to be the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. As the Elder Dragon took flight, he  was crushed beneath the pressure of wind and began to bleed to death. His life was "saved" by Kralkatorrik corrupting him into a
branded
and he had new a will, one that led him to march south and follow his new master.

Fiana
Race
Sylvari
Location
The Newly Awakened
(
Omphalos Chamber
,
Upper Commons
)
Fiana
was a
Secondborn
and a
gardener
amongst the
sylvari
. She fell in battle at the roots of the
Revered Terebinth
while defending her companions, local hunters; this changed the soil in the process. Now the tree bears
Magical Fruit
year round, and gives a sense familiar to the
Dream of Dreams
to the sylvari, which attracts the
Nightmare Court
's presence.

Episode 7:
Seeds of Truth

======================================================================
PAGE: Fielding
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Fielding
======================================================================

Fielding
was a
human
mercenary working under
Logan Thackeray
in
Ascalon
to protect human merchant caravans that supplied
Ebonhawke
. In 1319
AE
, he was in the group that fought with
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
's forces, taken over by
Rytlock Brimstone
, both of whom were then ambushed by
ogres
. He died in the conflict, and his body was burned in a makeshift pyre by Logan afterwards.

This article is about the world boss.  For the event schedule, see
Destroy the fire elemental created from chaotic energy fusing with the C.L.E.A.N. 5000's energy core
.
Fire Elemental
Race
Elemental
Level
15, 60, 70
Rank
Epic
Locations
Thaumanova Reactor
(
Metrica Province
)
Scorchlands
(
Iron Marches
)
Image(s)
Portrait.
Fire Elementals
, sometimes called
Greater Fire Elementals
to separate them from
normal fire elementals
,
appear as a stationary whirlwind of fire. In the center of this whirlwind is an eye-like orb, similarly made of fire. They scorch the land where they appear, create deadly environments, and summon hordes of embers to defend themselves. One appears as a
world boss
in
Metrica Province
's
Thaumanova Reactor
, and another is summoned by
Champion Drakin Cinderspire
at
the Blasted Hearth
, within the
Scorchlands
.

Combat abilities
Open world: Metrica Province
Behavior
The area at the base of the Fire Elemental will apply constant burning and damage, making melee attacks incredibly dangerous. This area is marked by a circle showing the area of effect.
Intermittently summons
Embers
.
Skills
Fire Storm
— Creates fiery tornados that move around the arena.
(Prior to
July 19th 2022 game update
it inflicted
Knockback
and
Burning
.)
Flame Wall
Meteorite
— A meteor falls from the sky, inflicting heavy damage and creating a fiery shockwave that knocks anything caught in it to the ground.
Lava Font
— Make lava erupt from the target area.
(Removed with the
July 19th 2022 game update
)
Open world: Iron Marches
Behavior
The Fire Elemental here is not attacked directly, but killed by destroying its minions (Embers, Fire Imps, and Greater Embers).

Isgarren
, the other
wizards
, and the Astral Ward, but it's our honor to work alongside you,
Commander

Can you tell me anything more about these rifts?

Kryptis
; a way for them to launch incursions into
Tyria
. The Rift Hunters are tasked with tracking them down, eliminating whatever comes out of them, and sealing them up.
Which parts of Tyria have seen rifts?

What else do you know about the
Heart of the Obscure
?
Not much more than what I told you.
Waiting Sorrow
, the wizard who created it, took many of its secrets with her, but we know it's an artifact of immense power. Use it wisely.

How does the Heart know where to guide me?
I'm not an expert in the arcane arts, but my understanding is that it tunes in to the same astral fluctuations that denote strong emotional essence; it sniffs out the same thing that attracts Kryptis.

What else can the Heart do?
I know that it can tap into the same arcane energy that a lot of the wizards use to safeguard Skywatch and Amnytas. It can open doors and chests and interact with things usually reserved for wizards.

======================================================================
PAGE: First Mate Kiango
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/First_Mate_Kiango
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
First Mate Kiango
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Corsair Flotilla
(
Domain of Istan
)
Dragonfall
(
Crystal Desert
)
Symphony's Haven
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Organization
Corsairs
The Sly Crew
“
Stand down—this one here's under parlay. You touch him/her, you'll feel the steel of the whole Sly crew.
—
First Mate Kiango
First Mate Kiango
is a
corsair
from
the Sly Crew
found on
The Dark Leviathan
. She is the right-hand woman of Captain
Sayida the Sly
.
Biography
Early years
As a
corsair
, Kiango served loyally in Captain
Sayida the Sly
's
crew
in the
Domain of Istan
and eventually became her first mate. During
Palawa Joko
's reign, she helped the rest of the crew capture enemies of state and bring them to the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
in exchange for bounty money.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
In 1330
AE
, the Sly crew managed to ambush and capture
Spearmarshal Zaeim
, who had become a notorious opponent of the
Mordant Crescent
and the
Joko Loyalists
with his revival of the
Order of the Sunspears
, and delivered him to
Warden Amala
as per the corsairs' agreement with the Mordant Crescent.
Not long after handing in Zaeim, Kiango met with the
Pact Commander
who had
arrived
at the Sly ship,
The Dark Leviathan
, to parlay with Sayida for Zaeim's freedom. Although some crew members intended to capture the Commander, Kiango warned the corsairs not to fight as long as the Commander was under the effect of parlay, which granted immunity for anyone invoking it for the duration of the talks, or she would have rule-breakers face Sly steel.
After Sayida had revealed the new whereabouts of Zaeim and been convinced to work with the Commander to free the
spearmarshal
, the Sly crew got to work to deliver strong grog to the
Awakened
at the great hall to get them intoxicated and allow the Commander a chance to find Zaeim. While Sayida and the Commander embarked on the daring mission, Kiango stayed behind on the Sly ship.
A Bug in the System
After Sayida had added an
airship
to her fleet with the bounty money she had received from delivering the Commander and Zaeim to the Mordant Crescent, Kiango continued serving as the captain's first mate.
Sayida and her crew assisted the Commander in
assaulting
the
Inquest
megacomplex of
Rata Primus
with the airship.
Long Live the Lich
Months later, Sayida sent her fleet to intercept Joko's
Scarab Plague
ships launched from
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
during the
Battle of Gandara
in 1331
AE
.
All or Nothing
In 1332
AE
, the Sly crew joined forces with the Commander's other allies in
Thunderhead Peaks
to take down the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. Kiango's motivation for joining the fight was to ensure that the Elder Dragon would not destroy the world and thus deny the corsairs from getting their hands on future loot and treasure.
One of the corsair airships was heavily damaged by Kralkatorrik's
Brandstorm
, leaving behind valuable detonators which were ultimately salvaged by the Commander.
After observing the animosities between the corsairs and the
Pact
for a time, Kiango entered
the Auditorium
of
Thunderhead Keep
along with other Sly crew members to listen to the Commander's speech to the troops before the
confrontation
with Kralkatorrik.
War Eternal
A few weeks later, Kiango traveled with the rest of the crew and their allies to the newly created landmass of
Dragonfall
caused by Kralkatorrik's fall from the Mists. The first mate manned a cannon on the airship piloted by Sayida to circle the location of the Dragonfall while listening to Taimi's excited banter as the asura used the cannons to attack the Branded below.
End of Dragons
Kiango continued accompanying Captain Sayida on her voyages with their airship as Sayida struck a business partnership with the Pact Commander's
sylvari
ally
Canach
. The airship ended up in
Cantha
in 1335
AE
, and the crew were reunited with the Commander who was chasing after
Ankka
, the new captain of the
Aetherblades
whose actions were causing havoc across the continent. Sayida, Canach, and the rest of the crew assisted the Commander and their allies in
setting up a trap
for Ankka above the
Xunlai Jade Junkyard
, fighting against both Ankka's Aetherblade crew as well as the Risen versions of the crew after Ankka had reanimated them with
Void
magic
. The Commander's party was ultimately able to defeat Ankka for good, allowing the Sly crew to take a breather.
Visions of Eternity
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.

Swahili

Take a good look. Any jeweler would be lucky to have access to my stock. Crafting baubles such as these is no hard task for someone of my intellect.

======================================================================
PAGE: Flame Centurion Kagaar
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Flame_Centurion_Kagaar
======================================================================

Flame Centurion Kagaar
Race
Charr
Level
14
Rank
Veteran
Locations
A Spy for a Spy
(
Black Citadel
,
Facula Castrum
)
The Flame Advances
(
Ruins of Rin
,
Junker's Scrapyard
)
Organization
Flame Legion
“
Just a nudge to start Iron and Blood fighting, then we step in and pick the bones clean.
—
Flame Centurion Kagaar
Flame Centurion Kagaar
is the
centurion
serving under
Trybulus Griefblade
in the
Flame Legion
. Prone to rage and dismissive of those serving under him, he will stop at nothing to see the operation to weaken the
Blood Legion
and the
Iron Legion
from within succeed.
Biography
After gaining valuable intel on
Black Citadel
defenses from the
Ash Legion
defector
Trybulus Griefblade
, the
Flame Legion
began an operation to use spies to weaken the alliance of the
High Legions
for the coming assault. In 1235
AE
, Centurion Kagaar was assigned to serve under Trybulus Griefblade as his second-in-command and gave direct orders to the Flame Legion spies who were sent to assassinate
Centurion Volante Tornpaw
of the
Blood Legion
while disguised as
Iron Legion
and sabotage the Iron Legion's newest siege weapon while posing as Blood Legion with the intent of setting the two High Legions against each other and weakening them for the Flame Legion to assault the Black Citadel itself.
While preparing for the coming assault, Kagaar briefly conversed with the
Ash Legionnaire
who had infiltrated the war camp under Flame disguise in search of intel. The centurion refused to let the Legionnaire know the specifics of the plan due to the Legionnaire having been late for the briefing. Kagaar eventually left to handle matters elsewhere, allowing the Legionnaire to use the opportunity to free the imprisoned Blood Legion soldier
Yahuk Fellstrike
from captivity and escape with him.
Although the assassination and sabotage attempts were thwarted at both Blood and Iron camps, the Flame forces managed to capture Ash
Tribune Torga Desertgrave
's
aide-de-camp
Fengar Cruelmane
and torture information out of him about the tribune before killing him. They then used the information to kidnap Desertgrave herself and take her to be interrogated at the
Ruins of Rin
where Flame Legion troops were mustering while leaving a handful of assassins behind in the Black Citadel to get rid of the Ash Legion agents whom Trybulus Griefblade knew would be coming to Tribune Desertgrave's aid. While in the Ruins of Rin, Kagaar was entrusted with an arcane trinket which was the only way to get past the
magically
warded entrance into the hideout.
After defeating the assassins in the Black Citadel, a strike force consisting of the Ash Legionnaire, the Legionnaire's sparring partner, Tribune
Rytlock Brimstone
, and
Sicaea the Shrouded
sneaked and fought their way past Flame Legion patrols until they confronted Kagaar himself for the key. After a fierce battle, the Flame centurion was felled, and the strike force used his trinket to gain access to the hideout at the ruins where they saved Tribune Desertgrave from Trybulus Griefblade.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Getting the Band Back Together
A Spy for a Spy
The Flame Advances
Combat abilities
Effects

- Periodically cleanses conditions.

- Arrows gain an area of effect and do additional damage
Skills

- Basic projectile attack.

- Fan-shaped multiple projectile attack.
Dual Shot
- Double projectile attack.
Stolen skills
Blinding Tuft
Bone Crack
Throw Gunk
Tooth Stab
Trivia
Kagaar means 'precipice' in
Hindi
.
