======================================================================
PAGE: Knut Whitebear
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Knut_Whitebear
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Edge of Destiny
, the
personal story
,
Living World Season 1
,
Living World Season 2
,
Living World Season 3
,
Living World Season 4
, and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Knut Whitebear
Race
Norn
Level
1, 20, 80
Rank
Champion
Location
Knut Whitebear's Loft
(
The Great Lodge
)
“
Knut's the papa bear around here. Nothing happens without his grunt of approval.
—
Lizbet Fleister
Knut Whitebear
is an elder of the
norn
, husband of
Gaerta Whitebear
, and father of
Skarti Knutsson
and
Sigfast
.
The Great Lodge
is his steading, and as the Master of the Lodge he is a defender of
Hoelbrak
, the settlement that has been erected around the lodge. The Great Lodge was founded by Knut's grandfather
Asgeir Dragonrender
, the hero who had severed the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
fang
and led the norn south during the
norn exodus
.
Some might call Knut a leader of the norn, although in truth the concept of 'leadership' in norn society is poorly defined.
Biography
Early years
A grand
moot
was held in
Hoelbrak
to celebrate the birth of Knut Whitebear, grandson of the
norn
hero
Asgeir Dragonrender
.
An unknown
human
, who was present during the moot, wrote down the words of a
norn
skaald
in a letter that would eventually end up in the archives of the
Durmand Priory
. The letter explained how the skaald had told a tale of a person who had been chosen to follow
Bear
's path but who had ultimately found his true totem animal in
Wolf
instead after rescuing and feeding starving wolf pups which had been lying near the corpse of their mother.
As Knut grew up, he eventually inherited
the Great Lodge
which his grandfather had built, and became known as the Master of the Lodge. He married the enchantress
Gaerta Whitebear
who bore him two sons:
Skarti Knutsson
and
Sigfast
. Knut raised his sons and acted as an arbitrator in disputes within Hoelbrak while Gaerta, driven by wanderlust, often left the lodge on lengthy hunts to build her legend in the wilderness.
The couple's years together, and Gaerta's hunts away from home, did not diminish Knut's passion for his wife even when other norn women desired him for his qualities as a strong hunter.
Knut viewed his sons fondly, and denizens of Hoelbrak would remark that the brothers were the light in Knut's eye.
Priory scholars eventually discovered an old fireside song which contained a
prophecy
seemingly predicting Knut Whitebear's death while including the ominous phrase "...and the muttering voices will call for his blood." The scholar
Finn
, however, argued that his fellow scholars were interpreting the prophecy too literally and that it did not necessarily predict Whitebear's demise.
Tales tell how Knut proved himself to be a wise arbitrator of disputes among hot-headed norn while trying to keep Hoelbrak peaceful and organized. He named
Ayslin Hovde
the chief tradesman of Hoelbrak; Ayslin would ensure that prices were reasonable, that wages were appealing, and that the artisans and workers in the various trades had all the materials they required.
At one time Knut had to make a choice between keeping peace in Hoelbrak using violence or wisdom when he broke up a bar fight caused by a quarrel between two brothers who were divided over a woman. He knew the brothers were good and just eager to impress the woman, so he called them over and told them that the first one who could knock him out was worthy of the woman. The brothers were unsure, but stopped fighting each other and circled Knut. Whitebear never laid a hand on them despite proving himself a superior combatant, however. Instead, Knut dodged the brothers' blows, but praised them for working as a team as they attacked him. He let the brothers come at him until they fell over exhausted. When the brothers got their strength back, Knut took them aside and told them that the bonds of family were stronger than a passing fancy. The brothers took the lesson to heart and never quarreled again.
Knut agreed with the norn elders on waiting for the norn of prophecy who would be destined to crack the
Fang of the Serpent
, the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's tooth which Knut's grandfather Asgeir had rended, before the norn marched north to confront the Ice Dragon. Some younger norn disagreed with Whitebear, however, believing that only the elders could stay behind while the rest of the norn either fought as individuals or pooled their strength together to confront Jormag.
Despite his wisdom, Whitebear was not above acting on impulse from time to time. When a norn criticized Hoelbrak's defenses for being too weak, Knut kicked him out, right into a blizzard.
Sons of Svanir
, a norn cult revering Jormag, saw Knut as past his prime. In their view, Knut should have died long ago and the Svanir should be the ones to kill him to prove their worth to Jormag.
Although Whitebear was aware of the cult's troublemaking ways, he tolerated the Sons of Svanir in Hoelbrak as long as they did not cause any trouble, and only had the
Wolfborn
punish troublemaking individuals, not the cult as a whole. Occasionally he would show mercy on the younger Svanir and let them back into Hoelbrak so they could atone for their actions.
Edge of Destiny
The
Dragonspawn
, a
champion
of Jormag, terrorized the countryside around Hoelbrak. Every time the norn sought out the Dragonspawn's lair to slay the champion, the men returned as
icebrood
while the women did not return at all, and the Dragonspawn cast devastating blizzards around the Shiverpeaks in retaliation for any intrusion into its lair.
In 1319
AE
, a particularly sizable icebrood horde, consisting of corrupted norn, attacked Hoelbrak. Knut himself participated in the defense of the settlement and fought alongside
Eir Stegalkin
, the dire wolf
Garm
, and other norn heroes.
Following the battle and seeing many of her friends being killed by the icebrood, Eir resolved to find a way to defeat the Dragonspawn for good. She eventually returned to Hoelbrak with the
asura
Snaff
and
Zojja
who had offered their
golems
to help Eir and Garm in the hunt for the Dragonspawn. The party boasted about their upcoming victory in Hoelbrak before they left to confront the Dragonspawn, and Knut learned of their bold attempt as news spread.
Eir and her companions failed to slay the Dragonspawn who had turned out to be more cunning than they had expected, however, and the dragon champion unleashed a devastating blizzard on Hoelbrak and the surrounding countryside in revenge. Knut confronted the weary heroes as they returned to Hoelbrak and told them about the many lives that had been lost in the storm. Whitebear banished Eir, Garm, Snaff and Zojja from Hoelbrak, telling them not to return until they had found "real warriors" to fight the Dragonspawn rather than relying on mere golems.
Eir and her companions returned to the Shiverpeaks two weeks later while accompanied by their latest recruits: the
charr
Rytlock Brimstone
, the
human
Logan Thackeray
, and the
sylvari
Caithe
. Knut confronted them at the gate and was told that the party had formed a
guild
called
Destiny's Edge
. He learned how Eir, Garm, Snaff and Zojja had defeated the previously invincible gladiatorial trio in
Lion's Arch
and recruited them to help with the hunt for the Dragonspawn. After listening to Eir's story, Knut decided to give her another chance to prove herself while warning her not to fail again or the Dragonspawn's wrath would fall on all the norn.
Destiny's Edge prevailed against the Dragonspawn and returned to Hoelbrak in triumph. Out of all the the people in the settlement, Knut was the most appreciative as he greeted the heroes. He placed an ermine cloak on Eir's shoulders and announced that Destiny's Edge would always be welcome in Hoelbrak.
When Eir pointed out that Whitebear should never have doubted her and that she intended to accomplish even greater things, Knut took it as a challenge. He led Eir to the Fang of the Serpent and asked her if she would challenge Jormag's tooth and prove if she was the norn destined to break it and lead the norn against the Ice Dragon. Eir hesitated, however, and focused Knut and everyone else's attention on the upcoming feast instead.
However, Knut's words weighed on Eir, and she decided to challenge the fang later on in a public event. Knut eventually learned of Eir's attempt and that, despite her best efforts, she had failed to crack the fang and had left Hoelbrak to battle other Elder Dragons' champions to grow in strength and experience.
Personal story
In 1325
AE
, Knut captured the great
wurm
Issormir
in
Lostvyrm Cave
and brought it to
Hangrammr Climb
where he intended the contesters of the
Great Hunt
to battle it to prove their worth.
Knut and his sons Skarti and Sigfast
attended
the Great Hunt alongside Eir. As Master of the Lodge, Whitebear approved of the trophies the hunters had brought to him and
Ido the Tanner
, and stated that the hunters were both skilled enough to defeat their prey and wise enough to revere their spirits. They witnessed one of the participating norn contestants defeat Issormir and earn the title
Slayer of Issormir
as a reward for the feat.
Following the Great Hunt, Knut hosted the
Big Brawl
with help from his old charr friend
Balteus Bloodletter
and Eir. However, unlike previous years' brawls, he invited contestants from various races to participate in the event to decide who got to challenge the reigning champion
Mikkel Toivosson
. The Slayer speculated that Knut may have allowed other races as contestants in the traditionally norn-only competition in order to test the norn's strength against these races.
Knut attended the
preliminary free-for-all
to decide which two norn out of all the participants would be allowed to progress through the following rounds. After the Slayer had bested the combatants, Knut told the Slayer to either defeat the Son of Svanir named
Nollarr
, who had been causing trouble around the Shiverpeaks, or help the merchant
Hekka Ravenswealth
hunt down aggressive
murellows
.
After the Slayer had triumphed over the challenge and other contestants whom Balteus had gathered, the Slayer returned to
the Championship Fight
which Knut was attending. The Master of the Lodge was excited to find out which of the contestants won the competition as both the Slayer and Mikkel were full of passion and purpose. After witnessing the Slayer prevail in the final brawl, Knut announced that seeing the Slayer come back from last year's defeat and rise even higher than before was a lesson that every norn should take to heart.
Around this time, the Gear
warband
led by
Ballista Geargrind
arrived
in Hoelbrak, demanding to recruit the Slayer who had lost Ballista's valuable tank during a drunken joyride with
Mangonel Gearstrip
. Only Ballista's respect for Knut, who had decided to get involved in the dispute upon learning about the warband's accusations, kept the arguments from escalating into violence. Knut ordered Eir to handle the matter peacefully, and Eir in turn helped convince the Slayer to work for the Gear warband until the Slayer's debt had been paid. Instead of getting angry at the Slayer for nearly jeopardizing Hoelbrak, however, Whitebear decided to use the commotion to his advantage by making three skaalds work on "The Ballad of the Wayward War Machine", inspired by the Slayer's drunken mishaps, for the next moot.
Later that year, news spread to Hoelbrak about
dredge
attacking the
Durmand Priory
facility of
Scholar's Cleft
and slaying a
Vigil
patrol, leaving behind bodies with strange wounds. Priory, Vigil and
Order of Whispers
representatives were arguing about what approach to take and asked for someone unaffiliated with the three
orders
to provide them with an outsider's view. Knut knew the Wolfborn had to remain neutral in the matter in order to not favor one of the three orders over the others, so he
sent
Skarti to ask Eir to represent the norn side to the orders. Eir refused, however, as she believed her time as a hero had ended after her failed attempt to defeat the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, and she instead suggested the Slayer to act as the norn representative in her stead. The Slayer helped the orders defeat the dredge, which Knut would eventually learn.
In the same year, Knut began training the Wolfborn with extra intensity as he slowly came to realize that neither he nor Hoelbrak would be safe from the Elder Dragons.
He also ordered Skarti and Sigfast to stay in Hoelbrak and postpone their hunts so they could keep the lodges safe.
Rumors began spreading about friction forming between Knut and Sigfast, while Skarti was torn between his father and brother on whose side to support. Although the topic of the dispute was said to be secretive, Sigfast suggested that he had taken issue with the way the norn were not being proactive enough in reacting to the growing threat of Jormag.
The Priory reached out to Knut as they needed to ask his permission to start excavations at
Lake Mourn
in order to recover old, painted pottery with images of Jormag. The scholars hoped that the paintings would give them a clue to the Ice Dragon's weak spot.
Queen Jennah
sent
Lady Sarah Marduke
as
Kryta
's ambassador to meet with Knut and discuss the future relationship of humans and norn.
Living World Season 1
In 1326
AE
, the
Molten Alliance
assaulted settlements in the Shiverpeaks and
Ascalon
and forced several charr and norn settlers to seek refuge in Hoelbrak and the
Black Citadel
. In response, Knut sent scouts north to assess the situation, and those who managed to return told stories of burned homesteads and a sizable number of corpses. Worried that Hoelbrak might be targeted next, Whitebear had the norn bolster the area's defenses.
During this time Knut sent a letter to his old friend,
Tribune
Bhuer Goreblade
of the
Iron Legion
, asking Bhuer if
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
knew anything about the attacks and what the imperator's stance was regarding how to handle the threat posed by the invaders. Whitebear admitted that he and Smodur had had their differences but hoped that they could cast their petty disagreements and "ancient" rivalries aside to join forces against the strengthened enemy.
While taking in refugees and finding places for them to stay in along with Eir, Knut
met with
the young norn
Braham Eirsson
who asked him to send some Wolfborn to
Cragstead
which the Molten Alliance had been targeting with attacks. Whitebear could not spare any of the Wolfborn as they had their hands full with the refugees, and Eir tried to sway Braham to stay in the safety of Hoelbrak.
Angered by what he perceived as Eir and Knut's cowardice, Braham announced that he would return to his home in Cragstead to help the people there even if he had to do so alone. After Braham had stormed off, Knut asked Eir about him, and Eir revealed that Braham was her son. Upon learning the truth, Whitebear calmly commented that Braham had Eir's stubbornness. While talking with the
Pact Commander
who had overheard the conversation, Knut asked the Commander to accompany Braham to Cragstead as a personal favor; the Master of the Lodge believed that the pair had better odds working together against the Molten Alliance than working alone.
After saving who they could, Braham and the Commander returned to Hoelbrak where they informed the norn about shutting down Molten Alliance operations in the region via raiding the
Molten Facility
. Braham
confronted
his mother and Knut once more and accused them of inaction which had cost lives of many norn in Cragstead. Knut admired Braham's spirit and praised the youth's resourcefulness although Eir worried that Braham was being too reckless for his own good.
Living World Season 2
In 1327
AE
, the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
was spreading its influence over
Tyria
. Braham and the Commander reached out to Eir so she could help convince Knut to attend a summit of racial leaders in
the Grove
and discuss the growing Elder Dragon threat. Eir, however, pointed out that Whitebear was worried about Jormag and the Sons of Svanir and would not leave Hoelbrak until the Svanir's influence over the Shiverpeaks had been dealt a major blow. After the Commander and Braham had helped Eir weaken the Svanir in
Frostgorge Sound
, Eir promised to talk to Whitebear on their behalf.
Knut heeded Eir's words and attended
the World Summit
hosted by the
Pale Tree
later that year. While in the Grove, he raised concerns over convincing several norn to join the
Pact
's campaign against Mordremoth and leaving homesteads vulnerable to Jormag's icebrood and the Sons of Svanir. However, the Commander was able to convince Whitebear that Mordremoth was a more immediate threat and that many norn would welcome the challenge to hunt another Elder Dragon with the Pact even though Knut was not certain how many norn would actually join the fight despite the chance to grow their legend.
The summit came under attack by the
Mordrem
, however, which targeted the leaders and damaged the Pale Tree. Knut fought against the Mordrem, viewing them as a worthy challenge, before Braham escorted him to a safer area in the Grove. By the time the Commander had driven off the
Shadow of the Dragon
which had been leading the attack, Knut declared that he would tell the tale of their victory over the Mordrem to his sons. He believed that the tale would inspire more norn to join the Pact for their upcoming invasion of the
Heart of Maguuma
where Mordremoth was suspected to dwell in.
Living World Season 3
In 1329
AE
, a year after Mordremoth's
demise
, Knut continued addressing several norn who demanded sylvari to be banned from entering Hoelbrak once their origin as Mordremoth's liberated minions had become common knowledge. Despite his fellow norn's insistence, Knut allowed sylvari to access the lodges under the Wolfborn's protection as there had been no evidence of sylvari turning against other races after the Jungle Dragon's death.
Knut attended a
vigil
which Braham had arranged to honor the memory of Eir Stegalkin who had
lost her life
during the Pact's campaign in the Heart of Maguuma. Whitebear sent a letter to the Pact Commander, inviting them to attend the vigil as well. During a discussion that followed, Knut speculated that the reason Braham had neglected to show up for the memorial was that he was still coming to terms with Eir's passing and trying to find reasons that justified her death.
Knut gently allowed the Commander to honor Eir in the way they saw fit, whether it was via talking with Eir's friends and celebrating her triumphs or paying their respects privately. During the vigil, Whitebear was among the first to notice the Commander's charr ally
Rox
arrive with Garm, and he informed the Commander about it. As the crowd gathered around the weary pair, Rox revealed that she had spent a long time searching for Eir's lost dire wolf in the jungle. Knut immediately sent for a healer to tend to Garm's wounds, and he watched the Commander do their part to ease the dire wolf's pain as well. After Garm had been tended to, the Commander gave a speech to everyone present and asked them to honor Eir's legend with a toast. Knut was the first person to respond although he had to clear his throat due to being overcome with emotion.
As the celebration continued, Knut received a letter meant for the Commander and approached them to deliver it personally. The letter was from the Commander's asuran ally
Taimi
who urged the Commander to visit her in
Rata Novus
as she wanted to show the Commander a breakthrough in her Elder Dragon research. The Commander departed after informing the norn present about the details of the campaign in Maguuma and the circumstances surrounding Eir's last stand.
Living World Season 4
When the
Awakened
forces of the
lich
Palawa Joko
invaded
Hoelbrak
and
Wayfarer Foothills
in 1330
AE
, the Wolfborn—including Skarti and Sigfast—defended the norn lands and drove the Awakened back. After the first attack, Knut witnessed norn fighters roaming in Hoelbrak and throwing insults at Joko while they hoped for a good fight.
The Icebrood Saga
Following the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
's
demise
in 1332
AE
, Knut decided to revive the
Dragon Bash
festival of
Lion's Arch
to celebrate the fall of Elder Dragons and hosted it as a grand
moot
in Hoelbrak. Knut contracted the skaald
Bodmodr Svartkelson
to write a welcome speech for the festival although he ultimately ended up changing most of the speech and paid Bodmodr with
Zhaitaffy
much to the skaald's annoyance. Knut had an asura record a
hologram
of himself giving the speech. He was unaware of how to present himself and which parts to cut out as he was not used to dealing with holograms. By the time the visitors poured in and finished listening to the hologram, Knut himself showed up to personally take them on a tour of Hoelbrak, anwering questions and enjoying the attention he was getting from the visitors.

Related achievements
Quip III: Chaos Gun
:
Prank Knut
— Prank
Knut Whitebear
in
Hoelbrak
by throwing a
Confetti Pouch
at him.

as your own.

(1
)
Trivia
Knut is
voiced
by
Jon St. John
.
"Knut"
is a name derived from
knútr
, which is Old Norse for "knot."
Knut is also the name of a
famous polar bear
that used to reside in Berlin, Germany. It is unknown if the character's name is related.
The
Pact
airships
,
Whitebear's Pride I
and
Whitebear's Pride II
, were named after him.

======================================================================
PAGE: Korag
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Korag
======================================================================

Korag
Race
Jotun
Level
14
Rank
Veteran
Locations
Echoes of Ages Past
(
Stonewright's Steading
,
The Osenfold Shear
)
The Last of the Giant-Kings
(
The Osenfold Shear
)
“
Jormag will give us the strength to restore our glory. We will rule the mountains again!
—
Korag
Korag
is a
jotun
clan leader who has been converted by the
Sons of Svanir
into worshiping the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
. He seeks to gain power from Jormag in order to reclaim his heritage of the ancient
giant-kings
.
Biography
Korag ruled a jotun clan, being one of the few jotun left who was a direct descendant of the ancient giant-kings.
In 1325
AE
, the
Sons of Svanir
managed to convert Korag and his tribe into worshiping the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
.
In exchange for receiving Jormag's power, Korag would reclaim the
Shiverpeak Mountains
for the jotun and rule them.
A party consisting of
Eir Stegalkin
, the
Slayer of Issormir
and the dire wolf
Garm
eventually
discovered
what the Sons of Svanir were up. While Eir and Garm took one of the Svanir survivors,
Herjarr
, to Hoelbrak for questioning, the Slayer headed for
the Osenfold Shear
to challenge Korag to a duel before the would-be giant-king could convert more jotun to his cause.
However, upon experiencing the
magic
of the
Worship Stone
and
learning
of the history of the jotun from the memory of an ancient giant-king called
Elder Thruln
, the Slayer took pity on Korag and offered him a chance to reject the Ice Dragon's temptations so he could save what was left of his people. Korag dismissed the idea and announced his decision to visit the Sons of Svanir to demand Jormag's blessing while urging the Slayer to find more power in preparation for their future confrontation.
Armed with either the blessing of
Wolf
or a strong weapon forged by
Beigarth the Smith
, the Slayer
returned
to the Osenfold Shear in the company of Eir and Garm to meet with Korag again. Having been
empowered
by Jormag, Korag mockingly greeted the Slayer's party and engaged them in battle once the Slayer expressed pity on how the giant-king had sold his soul and his people for the promise of power and a lifetime of slavery under the Ice Dragon.
After a fierce battle, the party successfully slew Korag. The rest of the jotun tribe surrounded the victorious trio at first but let them pass once the jotun
Bergris
announced that the tribe would become powerful again someday but not through Jormag's influence.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Forging a Legend
Echoes of Ages Past
The Last of the Giant-Kings
Combat abilities
Abilities
Korag
Skills
Slash
- Melee attack.
Kick
- Melee attack.
Leaping Smash
- Leaps to enemy and attacks.
Stolen skills
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing skills
Notes
Korag wields a
Shiverpeak Maul
with one hand, and a
Corrupted Harbinger
in his off-hand.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Korrak Blacksnout
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Korrak_Blacksnout
======================================================================

“
Can't wait to sink my claws into some fat human merchants.
— Korrak Blacksnout
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
was an officer of the
Iron Legion
. He had a grizzled face with a lion-like snout, scars that crisscrossed his dewlaps, and his horns were broken from years of hard campaigning.
In 1319
AE
, he was put in charge of leading a contingent of 300 soldiers through the
Blazeridge Gap
to hunt down
human
merchant caravans supplying
Ebonhawke
. Among his soldiers was
Rytlock Brimstone
, after he had been stripped of his rank and forced to perform a long term tour campaign among the other legions by
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
. During the journey, Rytlock warned Korrak of an incoming trap he was marching his troops into. Korrak ignored the warning, however, and called Rytlock a coward, forcing Rytlock to march in the front of the column. Korrak and his command corps joined in the front, which inadvertently saved their lives when the trap was sprung and several of the warbands were buried under an avalanche of rocks.
When Rytlock repeated that he warned Korrak, Korrak burst out in rage and swung his axe-rifle at Rytlock, who dodged out of the way, unsheathed
Sohothin
, and cut off Korrak's head. With their leader dead, Rytlock took charge of the surviving
charr
solders to chase after the human mercenaries led by
Logan Thackeray
.

asura

's
krewe

Kormir
and a raptor egg that hatches. Kozzak names the baby raptor, which he finds adorable,
Shrieksy
and keeps it as a pet which accompanies him in all later interactions.
Later he is acting as scribe for his krewe leader's achievements during the
Cathedral of Silence
mission. While overall helpful, being in the Temple of Grenth makes him extremely uneasy and he frightens easily.

Raven
who made him so clever.
And you want Erli to what?
Interesting. Thanks for the chat.
If he can't bring in a good catch, he doesn't deserve to win.
Mog may hunt big, but when he gets back, he yells for someone else to do the gutting and cooking. Not like Erli! He's the best cook around, and he always makes enough to share.
Ah, what do you know. See you later, kid.
Sounds dramatic. Good luck with that.
Have you heard about the time Erli saved my life? I was out hunting moas. I'd attacked a nice, plump one. And I'd have had it, too, but five of its friends showed up. I'm sure you know how that feels.
Maybe. What happened next?
So, I'm surrounded by big, beaky moas. I wasn't scared, but I had some healthy respect stuck in my throat. I look up and I see an oakheat running toward me yelling like a panther with its tail on fire.
An oakheart? Those are pretty dangerous.
Yeah, and the moas knew it. They took one look and ran like chickens in the other direction. Then I heard Erli call my name. See, Erli had picked up a leafy branch on the way, and we thought HE was an oakheart.
So there was no oakheart? I thought this was about Erli saving your life.
He did! From the moas! He knew if he tried to take all five of them head on I might get hurt, so he came up with a plan to scare them away. And it worked. Those birds are probably still running now. Ha!
Thanks for the story, kid.
Not really, and I don't want to know.
Quotes
Tomorrow I go on my first hunt!
Hi there!

PAGE: Lady Camilla
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Lady_Camilla
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Lady Camilla
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Allied Encampment
(
Domain of Kourna
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
I was your herald, back when you were first exploring Tyria with Destiny's Edge. You must have gotten all my letters?
—
Lady Camilla Einshorn
Lady Camilla Einshorn
is the
Pact Commander
's personal
herald
who has been documenting their rise as well as their legend and exploits and informing them of events taking place in
dungeons
. Her identity is revealed once she appears for the first time in person in the
Domain of Kourna
. Talking to her for the first time unlocks
Rude Awakening
,
The Tyrian Service Medal
and
The Gandaran Service Medal
.
Biography
Personal story
Lady Camilla Einshorn was a collector of tales of great daring and heroism and learned of the deeds of the hero, who would become known as the
Pact Commander
, from stories that had been reported to her in 1325
AE
. She spoke with the Commander's mentor from
Destiny's Edge
who suggested that she contact the hero to add their stories to her collection.
In her first letter, Camilla did not outright name herself, preferring to go by "Your Herald" while explaining some of her background and asking for a favor from the Commander. She was curious about the reasons why Destiny's Edge had fallen apart since 1320
AE
and hoped that the Commander could help her uncover what had happened while making sure the Commander was kept up to date with what Destiny's Edge members were doing in
Tyria
.
Throughout the year, the Commander used the provided information to help Destiny's Edge fight their way through various dangerous
dungeons
while Camilla often contacted Destiny's Edge members to investigate matters in various locations or call some members to help others as was the case with summoning
Logan Thackeray
and
Rytlock Brimstone
to assist
Zojja
in the
Crucible of Eternity
. After defeating initial threats in each dungeon and seeing Destiny's Edge members move on to other challenges, the Commander received more letters from Camilla who urged the Commander to continue exploring the dungeons while bringing attention to lingering issues such as rising threats and surviving enemies of whom she had been informed.
Living World Season 4
Lady Camilla continued chronicling the Pact Commander's actions in the following years and eventually decided to meet them face to face after traveling to the
Domain of Kourna
alongside other allies in the war against
Palawa Joko
in 1331
AE
. Upon settling in
Zelbahn Garrison
which the Commander had cleared of hostile
Awakened
forces, Camilla sent a letter to the Commander and asked for a meeting. Upon encountering her idol, she could hardly contain her excitement before finally introducing herself as the mysterious Herald from the Commander's past.
With the proper introductions done, Camilla proposed to create a banner wrought from the very best material which the Commander could carry into battle as a personal item to inspire both valor and terror. In exchange for making the banner and a suitable ornament for it, she asked the Commander to
hunt for rare materials
in Kourna and to
spread their fame
by participating in heroic battles throughout Tyria. With the deeds done and crafting finished by both Camilla and the Commander, she presented her idol with her ultimate gift: the
Ascended Banner of the Dauntless Commander
.
In 1332
AE
, Camilla joined the allied forces who traveled from
Central Tyria
and
Elona
to aid the Commander in the war against the Elder Dragon Kralkatorrik. After arriving at
the Auditorium
of
Thunderhead Keep
, she witnessed the Commander's inspirational speech on the eve of battle and participated in the
fight against Kralkatorrik
when the Elder Dragon was lured into the keep. Although the allied forces were able to wound Kralkatorrik, the Crystal Dragon successfully killed the Commander's
dragon
companion
Aurene
before departing from the keep and left the Commander and allies mourning Aurene's passing.
The Icebrood Saga
In 1333
AE
, Camilla followed the
Order of the Crystal Bloom
to the
Eye of the North
to eventually join the Commander and Aurene there, helping with arranging additional services for the halls.
When the Commander's allies from various factions were gathering at the Eye of the North to offer their assistance in the war against the rival Elder Dragons
Jormag
and
Primordus
, Camilla suggested that the Commander help weaponsmiths and various soldiers by boosting their morale and that the Commander should lead by example and travel to zones suffering from dragon minion incursions.

Dialogue
Domain of Kourna
Oh my! It's really you. You're even more majestic in the flesh.
(on first interaction, unlocks
Rude Awakening
,
The Tyrian Service Medal
and
The Gandaran Service Medal
)
Pardon me, do we know each other?
Yes! Well, no. Not exactly. I'm your herald. I've been chronicling your heroic exploits. You've received my correspondence before.
I'm honored to have such a dedicated fan. What are you doing here?
I've come with a proposal. I wish to create a monument to your greatness. Nothing so trivial as a portrait or statue—rather, an item you can carry into battle to inspire both valor AND terror.
Intriguing. What sort of item?
A banner, wrought from the very best materials—which are very hard to find. Fortunately, you're here! Bring the me
[
sic
]
items I've listed, and I will fashion you the most exquisite banner you've ever seen.
I should have figured there was a catch. Okay. I'm on it.
(after obtaining
Banner Ornament
)
Say, can we add this ornament to the banner?
THAT ornament? I mean, it's... well, it's a rather unremarkable adornment, isn't it? Hardly befitting your legend.
Oh. Okay.
Now now, hold on, let me apply a little something here... Ah, there. I've imbued it with magic that should resonate with heroic deeds.
And now we add it to the banner?
What? No, no! It isn't worthy of you yet—it needs to bear witness to your greatness. Travel about Gandara and Tyria. Perform mighty deeds. Slay fearsome beasts. Steep it into your heroism.
I'll see what I can do.
(on subsequent interactions)
What have you got for me?
I have to go.
Eye of the North
Visions of the Past: Steel and Fire
<Character name>! I heard you and Aurene were here, so I followed the Crystal Bloom to join you. These halls are legendary. And a good place to make your own.
Make my own?
Just say the word, and I'll arrange for more services here to support you. You could do a lot with this place.
What kind of services are we talking about here?
I'll let you know.
Let's talk later.
Champions
—
Chapter 1: Truce
<Character name>, everyone's been gathering here to help you and Aurene against the dragons. I'll do what I can—let me know if you need an update!
It looks like new allies are gathering here.
Everyone is pulling together. The newcomers would appreciate your help boosting morale and supplies—talk with their quartermaster to find out what they need!
Thanks, I'm on my way.
I see more weaponsmiths have arrived.
They're developing weapons that harness Aurene's blessing. Should come in handy against the Elder Dragons. See if you can help them. I bet they'd appreciate it.
Got it.
How's everyone holding up?
To tell the truth, people are scared. It would help to see you leading the charge. We're routing the asura gate directly to hot zones.
I'm on my way.
Let's look at services to recruit for the Eye of the North.
Let's talk later.
Dungeon Rush
Commander, it's been too long. My little archive on you has gotten...well, not so little. Are you eager for exploration? The dungeons beckon your mastery. Drafty halls filled with ghosts. Scalding—
Show me the
achievements
.
(Only shown after completing
Deputized for Dungeons
)
Any special items I can barter a tale for?
/
Dungeon-delving, eh? In this economy?
Indeed. A good tumble through the darkest manors and caves in our known world. To an archivist like myself? A dream to observe. Our darkest secrets cling to those places. Histories, buried.
/
Sounds exciting enough. I'm always down for a good tumble.
Thought you might. The world is often distracted by large events. Exploring the histories beneath our feet—the creatures and foes that linger in shadows. That's where heroism is born.
/
Sounds like I have some crawlspaces to explore.
(Awards
Deputized for Dungeons
)
As always, your enthusiasm...beams? That a writerly world? I'll get delving.
So you've been keeping busy then, Camilla?
If this character has completed part of
Secrets of the Obscure
.
Indeed. As have you, dearest. All of Kryta's been in a bit of a tizzy since that tower vanished, but you... Little more hands-on with that incident, were we?
I... No idea what you're talking about.
I heard that you'd gone to
Garenhoff
to investigate! Fought one of the beasts in the country. Is my information false...?
Garenhoff! Right! THAT Garenhoff. Hold that thought—I need to...go.
If not
I've been quite occupied following a series of disturbances in the country. Dire times all around, it seems. I'm eager to pivot my mind to more heroic feats, living vicariously through my dungeoneer.
Can't keep the lady waiting, then.
Hold those many thoughts, Camilla. I'll be back.

Lady Valette Wi
Race
Human
Profession
Mesmer
Level
80
Locations
Confessor's Stronghold
(

)
Confessor's End
(
Caudecus's Manor
)
"Where's Balthazar?"
(

,

,
White Mantle Hideout
)
Organization
White Mantle
Shining Blade
Image(s)
Valette serving Anise.
Valette's new look.
“
I just want to balance this ledger. Then I'll gladly accept whatever fate you see fit.
—
Lady Valette Wi
Lady Valette Wi
is the daughter of
Lady Wi
and
Minister Wi
, and the second cousin of
Demmi Beetlestone
. She is a well known violinist in
Rurikton
and has even played for
Queen Jennah
several times.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
Valette is eventually revealed as a member of the
White Mantle
who has been persuaded by
Confessor
Caudecus Beetlestone
to fight against Jennah's royalists in the new
Krytan
civil war. She aids Caudecus multiple times during the insurrection but changes sides during the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
when she notices how the unhinged Caudecus kills his daughter Demmi in cold blood. Thanks to Valette's help, the
Pact Commander
's strike team is able to enter Caudecus's sanctuary and
kill him
, dealing a devastating blow to the White Mantle.
Countess Anise
ultimately chooses to spare Valette's life for her assistance instead of executing her for high treason, and she has Valette carry out her sentence as an agent bound to Anise in the
Shining Blade
, granting her the name
Valette the Treasonous
.
Biography
Early years
Valette Wi was born to a noble family in
Rurikton
as the daughter of
Minister Wi
and
Lady Wi
. She is the second cousin of
Demmi Beetlestone
via Demmi's mother
Lady Beetlestone
who was Lady Wi's first cousin. After Lady Beetlestone's untimely death, Lady Wi stepped in as godmother to Demmi as she disliked Demmi's father,
Legate Minister
Caudecus Beetlestone
.
Valette trained as a
mesmer
and as a violinist, becoming so renowned as a musician that she got to play to
Queen Jennah
herself on multiple occasions while tour guides would mention the trivia to tourists.
After Demmi had suddenly fled from the Beetlestone manor in 1325
AE
and gone undercover with the assistance of the
Order of Whispers
and Lady Wi who was secretly a Whispers agent, Caudecus set his sights on Lady Wi's daughter Valette. To earn Valette's favor, he gifted her with a violin, prompting Valette to promise to perform to him while she lamented Demmi's absence and her desire to reunite with her second cousin in the future.
After one of Valette's performances, Caudecus called her to a private audience where he discussed the severity of many threats to Kryta, ranging from
bandits
to
centaurs
and even a supposed
charr
plot against the kingdom. Valette admitted that she had grown frustrated with her father's loyalty to
Queen Jennah
who had seemingly not responded to the threats sufficiently enough and how her mother, Lady Wi, claimed to know more than she was willing to share with Valette, and how both of them had been treating her like a child. She decided to accept Caudecus's offer to strengthen Kryta, hoping that her parents would open up their eyes to the reality the kingdom was facing. She was grateful for Caudecus treating her like an adult and attended a later, secret meeting with other like-minded people. This would lead to her first contact with the
White Mantle
, and she would eventually adopt their radical views and plot against the crown.
Living World Season 3
Out of the Shadows
Caudecus suddenly fled from the Royal Palace in 1329
AE
, prompting the
Shining Blade
officer
Canach
, the
Pact Commander
, and members of the
Dragon's Watch
guild
to track him down and bring him back by any means necessary.
When found in
Bloodstone Fen
experimenting on
Bloodstone
shards, Caudecus
revealed himself
not as a kidnapped victim of the
White Mantle
but as the current
Confessor
of the cult who planned to take over the Krytan throne from Queen Jennah by force. Caudecus attempted to have the Commander's party killed with the assistance of a
subjugated jade construct
but failed; even so, he had Valette use her mesmer magic to immobilize the Commander's party after the battle to have his reinforcements attack them.
However, the sudden arrival of a
being
introducing himself as
Lazarus
, the last
mursaat
whom a White Mantle faction led by High Inquisitor
Xera
had been attempting to
revive
behind Caudecus's back, led to the White Mantle splintering into two factions when Caudecus refused to submit to Lazarus, who in turn branded Caudecus as a heretic.
Lazarus slew several White Mantle in response to their defiance, which distracted Valette and allowed the Commander's party to break free from her spell. Canach used the provided opening to attack Caudecus, but the Confessor managed to escape in the nick of time when Valette teleported him and some surviving White Mantle to safety.
The Head of the Snake
Bloodstone
-crazed
Caudecus Beetlestone
with Valette and
Demmi Beetlestone
.
After escaping, Caudecus gathered his resources, sent
letters
to the White Mantle in hopes of preventing them from joining Lazarus's schism while proclaiming the mursaat a "false god", tried to sway Krytans to his side with
posted messages
openly criticizing Queen Jennah's reign, and was rumored to have headed north for the
Janthir
.  It is unknown if Valette accompanied him during the journey or if she was sent elsewhere at the time.
Caudecus eventually reemerged in Kryta by leading a
siege
on Divinity's Reach from
Lake Doric
in 1330
AE
with a combined force of White Mantle and Bloodstone-crazed
Harathi
centaurs
. The Confessor ordered one of his underlings,
Minister Estelle
, to assassinate Jennah during an
assault
on
the Upper City
, although the attempt was thwarted by a timely intervention from the Pact Commander and
Logan Thackeray
. The siege was quickly turned back by the
Seraph
, bolstered by
Legionnaire
Nylia Steelpaw
's
warband
, due to the White Mantle's weakened state by defectors.
Witnessing his plans crashing around him, the Confessor was ultimately forced to take shelter as he sought out to unleash a "contingency plan" in his desperation. As he did so, Canach and a group of Shining Blade infiltrated the manor, but were all killed or captured. Following the loss of contact with Canach's squad, the Pact Commander, Demmi Beetlestone, and Countess Anise sneaked into the manor from the
Hidden Falls Dock
connecting to Lake Doric to bring down the traitorous minister for good.
When Caudecus was finally
found
in the
Grand Salon
in his manor alongside Valette, he had begun to go
crazed
from consuming large amounts of empowering Bloodstone. During the fight, the Confessor admitted to having his wife murdered and fatally shot Demmi with the misguided belief that they could become a family again in
the Mists
, which prompted Valette to switch sides as she realized how unhinged Caudecus had become. Before he could be caught, the Confessor retreated to a lower dungeon decorated with his own face across the walls.
By the time the Commander's party reached him with Valette's help, Caudecus had grown to become an unrecognizable version of himself, partially becoming a bloodstone abomination. Here, in the depths of his manor, Caudecus, the ambitious puppetmaster who had engineered so many plots against Kryta and the crown over the years, was finally killed.
Following the battle, Anise freed Canach from servitude thanks to his assistance in bringing Caudecus down. She intended to execute Valette next for high treason but was ultimately persuaded to spare the young noblewoman and offer her a chance to serve her sentence in the Shining Blade instead as the countess was one hand short following Canach's release.
One Path Ends
A few months later, the noblewoman was seen wearing more modest attire and had become known as Valette the Treasonous, a mocking reference to the lofty titles many White Mantle members had once had. Serving under Anise, Valette met with the Commander again while the latter was pursuing
Balthazar
, the returned
God
of War, who had been masquerading as Lazarus earlier to divide the White Mantle. Valette provided the Commander information about the history of the
Eye of Janthir
, a magical construct tied to the mursaat and the White Mantle, which was seeking out artifacts housing the separated aspects of the still slumbering, real Lazarus, and which the Commander was hoping would lead the strike team to Balthazar as well.
Later years
Valette continued serving Anise and the Shining Blade faithfully over the years to atone for her misdeeds, and she gradually earned the countess's respect. At some point, Anise wanted to ensure that the events involving the White Mantle insurrection and other threats to Kryta from that time would fully be put to rest. To achieve that end, the countess instructed Valette to
gather any and all recollections and detailed accounts
from persons of importance surrounding the crisis so the Shining Blade could use that information to make sure that Kryta would never again be brought to the brink. While carrying out her duty, Valette briefly met with the Pact Commander who decided to lend a hand in gathering the recollections. Valette confessed that Anise was treating her fairly despite the past crimes although the young Lady Wi also expressed regret that she could not rewrite her misdeeds from those years.
Janthir Wilds

Lady Wisteria Whiskington
is from the
Shire of Beetletun
. She won Divinity's Reach Fanciest Cat competition, beating
Chauncey von Snuffles III
. She is the prized cat of
Minister Caudecus
and can be adopted by The
Pact Commander
if she is offered with several bloodstone food items.

near the

Trivia
In the home instance, Lady Wisteria Whiskington will breathe fire in response to the
Feline Holoscepter
, and will become hostile if attacked with the
Feline Water Pistol
. The Water Pistol is the only tool that can break her breakbar, putting her to sleep for the rest of the time spent in the instance.

. For the champion rank, see

.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Lagula
Race
Ogre
Rank
Champion
Locations
Ogre Camp
(
Tangled Depths
)
Dragon's Domain
(
Dragon's Stand
)
Organization
Pact
Image(s)
Appearance during the
Untamed Wilds
.
“
I won't forget what you did for us. When you make your stand against the dragons, call me. I will repay that debt.
—
Lagula
Chieftain Lagula
is an
ogre
of
Agrak Kraal
who becomes chieftain of her tribe after discovering the fate of her predecessor. She assists the
Pact
in the war against the
Elder Dragons
.
Biography
Personal story
While
Chieftain Uldek
was in charge of
Agrak Kraal
, Lagula was considered viable for chieftainship, but had always refrained in fear of splitting the
kraal
apart. However, after Uldek was corrupted by the
Branded
in 1325
AE
, Lagula took leadership to defend against his attack, evacuating Agrak Kraal when the massing Branded army approached.
She ultimately defeated the corrupted Uldek with the help of the
Pact Commander
but was unable to save Agrak Kraal from being destroyed. Wishing to avenge their kraal and previous chieftain, Lagula and her tribe vowed to join the
Orders
in fighting the
Elder Dragons
and began looking for a new place for their people to live in.
Lagula later assisted in the
Pact
's assault on
Orr
and helped the Commander's party bring down one of the
Eyes of Zhaitan
. When the Commander and Marshal
Trahearne
were about to be swarmed by
Risen
while trying to purify
the Artesian Waters
later on in the campaign, the ogre chieftain was among the reinforcements who arrived to support the pair and ultimately witnessed the successful culmination of Trahearne's
Wyld Hunt
. Lagula was among the allies in
Fort Trinity
who celebrated the victory over the Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
after the Pact Commander and
Destiny's Edge
had returned from
Arah
.
Heart of Thorns
After Zhaitan's death, Lagula and her tribe left the Pact due to the Pact being unable to help them get new pets and a new home. They headed westward aboard an airship in 1328
AE
in hopes of finding a new home and new pets, having heard that the
Heart of Maguuma
was home to many pets. However, the airship crashed in
Tangled Depths
and they were left with many dead, including all of their pets from
Ascalon
.
Having discovered the
chak
, Lagula and her people investigated them and tried to tame the chak as well as local bats. Realizing that chak could not be tamed, the ogres rejoined the Pact's forces south into
Dragon's Stand
to confront the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
. They used the knowledge they had gained in the jungle to gather an army of jungle pets to assist them in the assault while also riling the chak against
Mordrem
as a distraction.
The three-pronged assault was successful, and the Pact and their jungle allies fought their way into
Dragon's Domain
. Lagula joined other commanders in the clash against Mordremoth and saw the Elder Dragon fall after a fierce battle. She exchanged excited words with the Pact Commander about the battle, claiming that it had been a great hunt that would be long remembered.

This article is about the NPC.  For the
attribute combination
"Laranthir's", see
Vigilant
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
,
Living World
, and
expansions
.
Laranthir of the Wild
Race
Sylvari
Rank
Champion
Locations
Vigil Headquarters
(
Vigil Keep
)
Verdant Brink
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Dragon's Stand
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
The Jade Whirl
(
Dragon's End
)
Organization
Vigil
Pact
Pale Reavers
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Click to enlarge.
“
I am a Grand Warmaster, Almorra's second-in-command. Should she fall, I will lead the order...but I pray to the Tree I am never needed in that role.
—
Laranthir of the Wild
Grand Warmaster Laranthir of the Wild
,
secondborn
of the
sylvari
, is a prominent member of the
Pact
. He is also one of the leaders of the
Vigil
alongside
General Almorra Soulkeeper
,
Warmaster Efut
,
Lord Rodrigo of Cormoch
, and
Jhavi Jorasdottir
. As
Grand Warmaster
, he is the Vigil's second-in-command, tasked with taking leadership of the order in the event of General Soulkeeper's death.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Following General Soulkeeper's
death
and the end of the
Icebrood War
, Laranthir does not succeed her as expected. He retains his rank as Grand Warmaster while Jhavi Jorasdottir becomes the new General of the Vigil.
Biography
Early years
Laranthir awakened in
the Grove
as a
secondborn
sylvari
around 1304
AE
,
circa two years after the
firstborn
.
Following the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
's
rise
in 1320
AE
, Laranthir met with the
charr
Almorra Soulkeeper
who had become a
gladium
after being forced to
slay
her
warband
whom the Elder Dragon had
Branded
during his flight south. Almorra had fallen into vengeful depression at the realization how impossible it was for a single person to take on an Elder Dragon. Laranthir and a few other unlikely allies helped her return to her senses, and it was Laranthir in particular who convinced Almorra to turn her mission of vengeance into a mission of justice. Almorra made a vow to her fallen warband to destroy Kralkatorrik as well as the Crystal Dragon's minions.
Realizing that only through unity could the
Tyrian
races hope to defeat the Elder Dragons, and wanting to save her from herself, Almorra founded a military organization which she named the
Vigil
, coining the order's motto "Some must fight, so that all may be free."
As the General of the Vigil, Almorra made the co-founder Laranthir a
Grand Warmaster
, her second-in-command, and dictated that he would succeed her as general if she fell in battle.
Laranthir had often sought love but had not found it. Until that day, he swore to serve the Vigil as if it were his beloved.
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Crystal Corruption
Death from Below
Order Neophyte
The Battle of Claw Island
Victory or Death
Living World Season 1
In 1326
AE
, Laranthir and Almorra learned about the
Molten Alliance
assaulting both
charr
and
norn
. The Vigil tasked able adventurers, including the Pact Commander, to raid
Molten Facilities
to hamper enemy operations and free captives.
When
Durmand Priory
scholars reported that
Scarlet Briar
's
Breachmaker
might have disturbed an
Elder Dragon
via redirecting a
ley line
in the aftermath of the
Battle for Lion's Arch
, Laranthir petitioned
Trahearne
to send an
airship
to
Lion's Arch
to investigate. Later, he personally congratulated the
Pact Commander
for
defeating
Scarlet while explaining that the Pact could not initially get involved with stopping Scarlet as the organization's purpose was to fight the Elder Dragons and not protect cities from deviants.
Living World Season 2
The Pact eventually learned that Scarlet's tampering with the ley lines beneath Lion's Arch had led to the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
's awakening and the emergence of its
Mordrem
minions which began appearing around the world in areas with magical hotspots or powerful artifacts. Laranthir joined Trahearne at
the World Summit
in
the Grove
in 1327
AE
, though he remained out of the meeting itself while leaders of five nations discussed the threat Mordremoth posed to the world. Laranthir and people below the
Omphalos Chamber
were forced to fight a sudden Mordrem invasion while Mordremoth's
champion
, the
Shadow of the Dragon
, attacked the Pale Tree. The Pact Commander was successful in driving the Shadow of the Dragon away although the Pale Tree was weakened from the attack and fell unconscious.
Following the defense of the Grove which had invigorated many sylvari to avenge their Mother Tree, Laranthir helped Trahearne prepare the Pact to focus on Mordremoth from the staging ground of
Camp Resolve
in
the Silverwastes
. Although the Pact had to speed up its schedule to strike at Mordremoth as soon as possible before the Elder Dragon could grow in power, they had gained enough funding, supplies and reinforcements from the five nations to be able to launch an
airship
fleet to the
Heart of Maguuma
where Mordremoth was believed to reside in. Laranthir accompanied Trahearne and members of
Destiny's Edge
to the jungle, certain that they could defeat Mordremoth like they had done with Zhaitan.
Heart of Thorns
Act 1
When the Pact Fleet was destroyed by a joint attack from Mordremoth and sylvari who had succumbed to the Elder Dragon's call in 1328
AE
, Laranthir sent a request for aid to the Pact Commander, who then assigned him as the new leader of the
Pale Reavers
in
Carys
's absence. Despite facing distrust from some members of the Pact who believed that all sylvari had become a liability after some had turned into the
Mordrem Guard
to serve the Elder Dragon, Laranthir was able to
rescue
a few surviving Pale Reavers to boost his forces. He and
Chief Aviator Skybreaker
joined forces to bring down Mordremoth's minions and
cleared a path
for air support.
Act 4
Once the Pact had reorganized itself in
Verdant Brink
, Laranthir led it as well as the jungle allies they had gained along the way, such as the
Exalted
, in the assault on Mordremoth in the heart of
Dragon's Domain
. He witnessed the Elder Dragon's demise after a
fierce battle
and was relieved that Mordremoth's buzzing voice was finally gone from his head. However, the victory came at a great cost to the Pact: not only had they lost many soldiers in the war, but Trahearne had
sacrificed
himself to end Mordremoth for good, leaving the Pact without a marshal to lead it.
Path of Fire
After dying in the rogue
god
Balthazar
's
ambush
in 1330
AE
, the Pact Commander's soul appeared in the
Domain of the Lost
where all spirits which experience sudden, violent deaths end up in. While recovering their name and remembering
their purpose
to be properly
judged
, the Commander was subjected to fragmented visions of past deeds, including an encounter with Laranthir from the Maguuma campaign.
The Icebrood Saga
Some time after the allied Tyrian and
Elonian
forces had
slain
Kralkatorrik on
Dragonfall
in 1332
AE
, Laranthir submitted a request for a temporary leave of absence so he might return to the Grove. Almorra granted the request, expecting Laranthir to return to his duties at his discretion. In the Grand Warmaster's absence,
Jhavi Jorasdottir
assumed the role of second-in-command of the Vigil.
Laranthir learned about Almorra's
death
at the hands of
Blood Legion
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
and his subordinate
Ryland Steelcatcher
by 1333
AE
as well as the Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
which the rogue charr's actions had caused. However, against Jhavi's expectations, Laranthir did not immediately resume his duties as Grand Warmaster, leaving Jhavi to continue leading the Vigil in his absence as Tyrian forces had to battle against both Jormag's
icebrood
and
Primordus
's
destroyers
in the
Icebrood War
which would eventually lead to the
simultaneous deaths
of the rival Elder Dragons.
End of Dragons
In 1335
AE
, Laranthir boarded one of the Pact airships and accompanied the allied Tyrian and
Elonian
forces led by Pact Marshal
Logan Thackeray
to
Cantha
which had fallen under attack of the
Void
led by the Void-corrupted
Soo-Won
, the Elder Dragon of Water and Life. They were assisted by Aurene when the fleet fell under attack by the Void horde off the Canthan coast,
and they eventually reached the
Harvest Temple
where Soo-Won was gathering the Void horde to obliterate the Canthan defenders and spread the Void worldwide. Laranthir sniped Void minions atop a Pact airship, providing assistance to the ground forces and the Pact Commander to
weaken Soo-Won
so the Void could be extracted from her. Following the Elder Dragon's defeat, Laranthir stated how happy he was to be reunited with old faces and see how people of different backgrounds had put aside differences to fight together.
Interim years
In the months following the death of Soo-Won, the Pact was unable to find a new purpose as all the Elder Dragons had been defeated. Logan decided to go on a sabbatical, and the three orders went off and did their own work for a while. In 1336
AE
, all corners of Tyria and beyond suddenly experienced invasions of
demons
emerging from
the Mists
. None of the three orders could figure out the origin and identity of the invaders or their ultimate goals beyond slaughter. Although the demons were driven back, they had left the peoples of Tyria rattled. The attack drew the orders together, reinvigorating the Pact.
During this time Laranthir oversaw the Pact operation to
return to the Heart of Maguuma
to aid their local allies there by stabilizing the area from remaining threats. He also hoped to recover Pact records and assets from the battles against Mordremoth, asking the Pact Commander to aid in their recovery. He revealed that Jhavi had been working hard to rebuild the Vigil and the Pact after the war against the Elder Dragons, and he stated his fervent belief that there was still a need for their organization as guardians.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Against expectations, Laranthir did not succeed the late Almorra but retained his rank as Grand Warsmarshal while acting as an advisor to the newly promoted general Jhavi Jorasdottir instead. In 1337
AE
, the pair accompanied
Steward Gixx
of the Durmand Priory and
Riel Darkwater
of the Order of Whispers to Lion's Arch to participate in the
second meeting
of the newly established
Tyrian Alliance
as representatives of the Pact while Logan was still on a sabbatical.
Laranthir was happy to be reunited with the Pact Commander in the meeting, informing them of recent events concerning the Pact and voicing his support for the orders to join the Tyrian Alliance. He also admitted that he wanted to keep an eye on the
Astral Ward
who had recently revealed themselves to Tyrians and had provided the factions of the alliance with information about the defeated demon invaders' origin as the
Kryptis
.
The meeting's participants listened to the Commander's report which revealed that
titans
, dreadful foes which had ben thought to have been defeated by
heroes
from centuries ago, had been sighted spreading corruption across
Janthir
. The newer participants observed as the founders of the Tyrian Alliance cast a vote on whether to use the
Scepter of Orr
, an ancient
Seer
artifact, to try to control any titans that remained to prevent further destruction. The faction led by
Queen Jennah
opposing the use of the scepter won the vote and elected to send a team led by the Commander to investigate Janthir further to learn more about the titan presence in Janthir.

Dialogue
Welcome to Tribune Brimstone's office. I'm Laria Sharpeye, his aide-de-camp. Is there something I can help you with?
(If joined an order)
Where is Tribune Brimstone?
The tribune has taken a temporary leave from the
Black Citadel
. I believe he has gone south to
Lion's Arch
. Are there any other questions I can answer for you?
Tell me, what does a tribune's aide-de-camp do?
(Same as below)
No, that's all I needed to know. Good-bye.
Tell me, what does a tribune's aide-de-camp do?
I can't speak for the entire Black Citadel, but I mostly handle logistics and organization. Tribune Brimstone is very busy in the field, so I manage the flow of information to and from his office.
Important work. I wonder if the tribune is grooming you for a leadership position.
Leadership? I'd enjoy that. But at some point in the future...the distant future. I have so much to learn, but there's no better teacher and role model than Tribune Brimstone.
I feel the same way. Good luck, and good-bye.
What does your
warband
think of your job?
They mostly think office work suits me. I'm too..."soft-hearted and shy" is the nicest way to put it. They usually use other words. The plain fact is, I'm better at planning than I am at fighting.
I'd say Rytlock knows your worth far better than they do. Good-bye.
What's it like working for Tribune Brimstone?
Challenging...exacting...but I enjoy it. He's even given me clearance to issue some orders in his name. I think it amuses him to see senior officers take direction from someone they outrank.
Fascinating. Good-bye for now.
I see. And from the way you're staring, I see you have a question for me.
(same as below)
Thanks for the information. Good-bye.
(if a ranger)
From the way you're staring, I'd say you have a question for me.
Sorry, I didn't mean to stare. I was just admiring your
companion
. I'm a
ranger
myself, and I was curious. Do you mind if I ask you a few questions?
(if a charr ranger)
I'd be happy to. Ask away.
What animal do you think makes the best companion? Some swear by
devourers
, but they've always seemed a bit cold to me. I need a pet whose mood I can read. What do you think?
Ranger and pet need to work together. Try out a devourer and see if it suits you.

Asuran rangers aren't common, but they do tame animals too.

Rata Sum

human

The bond with your companion is all that matters. Let others worry about their own choices.

CATEGORIES: Story characters

======================================================================

Larra
Race
Asura
Location
Unspecified
Organization
College of Dynamics
“
I didn't graduate from some Statics clown college. I've got a plan! You want something done right, go with Dynamics.
— Larra
Larra
is an
asura
who can be encountered in
sylvari
personal story
missions if the player chose Act with Wisdom.
Tegwen
describes her as "loud-mouthed, ruthless, and vulgar. Your standard asuran scientist."
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Larra was captured while checking her
skritt
traps in a storm along with
Tegwen
. The player rescues them both from a
krait
tower with the help of
Carys
. Larra decides to tag along, while inventing colorful nicknames for her party. With her help the party is able to find and trade the skritt for the
artifact
Tegwen is looking for. Optionally, the player can also ask Larra to recover the lost Tegwen once the sylvari finally finds the artifact.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 2: The Teachings of Ventari
Beneath the Waves
Mirror, Mirror
Shattered Light
Combat abilities
Skills
Harpoon Shot
- Underwater melee attack.
Harpoon Shot
- Underwater melee attack.
Shrapnel Grenade
- Throw grenades that explode in a hail of shrapnel, causing
bleeding
.
Trivia
Larra's nicknames
Sylvari = "houseplant" "frond-faces" "salad"
Trahearne
= "walking fern"
Tegwen = "wilting shrub"
Carys = "fluff wit" "celery stalk" "yammering salad"
Player Character = "soft-hearted artichoke"

is a resident of

, a town in

Location
Kryta
Kessex Hills
Wizard's Fief
Garenhoff
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 3: Rescue Researcher
Pry the Eye Open
Dialogue
Welcome to Garenhoff, home of
Isgarren
's
castle
. If you have any questions, I'd be happy to answer them.
Who's Isgarren?
Not much is known about Isgarren. He keeps to his castle, and is rarely ever seen or heard. What is known, is that he has a kind gracious heart, and he cares for and loves the people of this town.
Thank you for your time.
Tell me about the castle.
All that's known about the castle is that it's Isgarren's home. That I know of, no one save Isgarren has ever entered the castle. Even so, it draws tourists from all over
Tyria
.
Thank you for your time.
Why are the
elementals
here?
The elementals are creations of Isgarren. They defend the town from threats, and work as servants for the town's people.
Thank you for your time.
I'm busy right now.
After completing
Interlude: Forward
Welcome to Garenhoff, the former home of
Isgarren
's
castle
. If you have questions, I'll do my best to answer them, but right now, we're all a bit...lost.
You said this was the "former home" of Isgarren's castle?
Yes. Until very recently, the southern sky was occupied by the grand floating castle of Isgarren, the wizard who's protected and watched over this town for generations.
That's all I need to know, thanks.
Tell me more about Isgarren.
Isgarren is a grand wizard, but beyond that most know very little. The
mayor
claimed that he made regular visits to the castle, but after the tower disappeared we learned the truth. And now we feel lost.
That's all I need to know, thanks.
Why are you feeling lost?
Everything we thought we knew turned out to be wrong. The castle was the anchor around which this town revolved, but it turns out, we were not important to Isgarren. He abandoned us.
That's all I need to know, thanks.
Sorry, I've got nothing else to say.

Urvan welding a shield during Fury of the Dead.
“
You want my job, you miserable little puke? Earn it. Get down there and fight. Show me what you've got, and maybe I'll show you how a real legionnaire fights!
— Legionnaire Urvan Steelbane
Legionnaire Urvan Steelbane
is a legionnaire of the
Blood Legion
. He appears during the charr tutorial mission,
Fury of the Dead
guarding the gates to the crypt, willing to let
Rytlock Brimstone
die in the crypt by trying to give the player new orders if the gates are shut. If the player joined the Blood Legion, Steelbane is also the legionnaire of the player's warband and appears in
Chain of Command
and
Time for a Promotion
. The Blood Legion player will need to defeat him in order to take command of the warband and then decide Urvan's fate, to live or die.
If left alive, true to his word, he is not seen or heard from again.

Heart of Thorns story

Dialogue
In Queensdale
How can I help you, citizen?
How are the Seraph holding up?
Between bandits, centaurs, and standard crime, we're stretched pretty thin. Is there anything else?
I'd like to ask you something else
I'll leave you to your duties.
How bad is the bandit situation?
We're holding, but I don't have the troops or the resources to launch a strong counterattack. For now, all we can do is hold our ground.
How can bandits stand up to you? You're the law!
There are debates going on within the Seraph. It's my belief that the bandits have a backer in Divinity's Reach.
Who do you suspect?
They're getting help from someone with a lot of influence. They're organized, well-equipped, and informed. They know things they shouldn't know.
You're doing the best you can with what you got- which is the mark of a leader.
Thank you. I wish I could do more. With the centaurs on one side and the bandits on the other, it's all we can do just to keep the peace.
Can I ask you something else?
You're a good example to your troops. Stay the course. Good day.
Maybe you're just getting beat and don't want to admit it.
Maybe. Do you need anything else? I have duties here, and I'm sure you must be busy.
Can I ask you something else?
Don't be so touchy. I'll take care of any bandits I see for you.
That's not good. Good-bye.
Good luck.
Good-bye.

In Auric Basin, speaking to him again
Glad to see you, Commander. We've established a safe foothold here, but we're still finding survivors.
No time to waste. How can I help?
We're searching the canopy for survivors and hoping to recover supplies that were tossed or fell overboard.
Impressive encampment. How's morale?
Credit Ceera for starting the camp. I just help coordinate relief efforts and defense. Trust me, your arrival here has the troops' spirits on the rise.
One more question.
Good job, Lieutenant.
I'd hoped for more survivors.
There were—most descended the canopy to the east to check out a golden structure. The skritt detachment stayed south with the airship wreck.
Good to know. Anything to be careful of?
Hostile Itzel control most of the canopy here, and all types of saurians are roaming the jungle floor. Even worse, we can hear the Mordrem mustering on a regular basis.
One more question.
Good job, Lieutenant.
What have you learned about this region?
There are huge, aggressive saurians in the area. Our Nuhoch friends speak of shining beings in the jungle who call themselves Exalted, and something called a Vinetooth that even the Exalted fear.
What do you know about these Exalted?
It's said they come from a golden city deeper in the jungle. Never seen it or them, myself.
One more question.
Good job, Lieutenant.
Good job, Lieutenant.

It's a soldier's life for me.

May Balthazar strengthen you.
These are dangerous times.

PAGE: Liliana Black

======================================================================

Liliana Black
Race
Human
Location
Raven Lodge
(
Hoelbrak
)
Organization
Durmand Priory
Image(s)
Interactive map
Liliana Black
is a member of the
Durmand Priory
found at the
Raven Lodge
that specializes in decryption and solving ancient mysteries.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Hoelbrak
Raven Lodge
Story involvement
Personal story
Code Breaker
Dialogue
Liliana Black, at your service. Codes cracked, ciphers solved, mysteries demystified. My work for the Durmand Priory doesn't leave much time for chit-chat, but what's on your mind?
I've heard of the Priory. Tell me more.
We're one of the
orders
of
Tyria
, dedicated to fighting the
Elder Dragons
before they destroy us all. We employ scholars, explorers, and adventurers of the highest quality.
How does any of that help against dragons?
Think like a hunter: the more you know about your prey, the better your chances. Besides, there may already be an elder dragon-killing spell out there, but we won't find it if we're not looking.
That makes sense. I'll let you get back to work now.
Fascinating. I must be going now.
Nothing. I just wondered what you were doing. Carry on.
Notes
Anomaly
:
Even if you have completed initiation to the Durmand Priory, this "introduction dialogue" to the Priory remains the same.
Trivia
Her name might be a reference to Liliana Vess, a necromancer heavily associated with the black color, as well as a major character of the
Magic: The Gathering
universe by
Wizards of the Coast
.
