Trolls possess a unique brutish intelligence. But all else about them seems vicious, heartless, and territorial. I cannot say much beyond that.
Can I ask you something else?
Thanks for the information. Good-bye.
What is a "Mender"?
We Menders are specialists in the healing of the sylvari form. Our bodies, born as they are from our mother
Pale Tree
, are unlike anything else that walks upon
Tyria
...with only a few exceptions.
Can I ask you something else?
Thanks for the information. Farewell.
Never mind, I'll talk to you later.
Nothing right now, thanks.
If completed
Awakening
You did a brave thing for that knight, sapling. Such kindness!
May I ask you a question?
Certainly! What would you like to know?
What else do you know about trolls?
They possess a certain brutish cleverness, but they are also vicious and territorial.
Interesting. May I ask something else?
I'll keep that in mind.
Why do you have these moas?
These are my pets, Ori and Anje
[
sic
]
! They're lovely, and they protect me here in the forest. It can be so dangerous.
That doesn't sound good. Can I ask another question?
They're lovely, indeed. Thank you.
What's a mender?
We are healers and shapers. We cure sylvari of ailments and wounds, and try to keep the land healthy.
Ah, how helpful. May I trouble you with one more question?
I'm glad to know that. Thank you.
Sorry to bother you.
You've been very kind as well, Mender. Thank you for your help.
Combat abilities
Skills
Shoot
- Shoot your foe from long range. The farther the arrow flies, the more damage it does.
Point-Blank Shot
-
Push
back your foe with a point-blank shot. The closer they are, the farther it pushes them back.

Mender Serimon

Interactive map
“
Welcome, sapling. My name is Mender Serimon. You are safe here.
— Mender Serimon
Mender Serimon
is a
mender
devoted to helping newly awakened
sylvari
acclimatize to
Tyria
; his is the first face sylvari
player characters
see upon their awakening. Serimon is one of the oldest menders amongst the sylvari, having taken up the role even as his fellow
secondborns
, such as
Ceara
, were still emerging from their pods.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Caledon Forest
Village of Astorea

Fighting the Nightmare

Valiant

, in Astorea.

Thank you, Mender.
Where was I while I dreamt? Before I awoke?

Pale Tree
.

Thank you, Mender.

Wyld Hunt
. What does that mean?
A Wyld Hunt is an important purpose given to a very few sylvari by the
Dream of Dreams
.
Are all newly-awakened sylvari called to a Wyld Hunt?
Not at all! It is rare to awaken with one, much less such a prominent hunt as yours. You're quite exceptional.
May I ask you another question?
Thank you, Mender.
May I ask another question?

dragon
. What do they mean?

biography
will appear here)

firstborn
, who have experience interpreting these images.
May I ask you another question?
Thank you, Mender.
Thank you, but I'll be on my way now, Mender.

Hello, stranger. If you'll excuse me, I have newly-awakened sylvari to attend.
Of course. Please, continue.

Merri Sunbower
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Caer Aval
(
Fort Trinity
)
Arborstone
(
Cantha
)
Organization
Pact
Image(s)
Interactive map
Merri Sunbower
is a seafarer and the mother of
Benn Tenstrikes
. When she saw her son join the fight against the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, she and her husband
Fel Vaerdenmer
decided to join the
Pact
too.
Biography
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Merri and her
husband
provide assistance to the
Commander
in completing the
Order of Whispers
' plan to capture the Risen navy to perform a covert strike against Zhaitan's forces.
They later reappear at the
victory celebration
at
Fort Trinity
.
Interim years
After losing contact with their son Benn following the Pact's costly war against the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
in the
Heart of Maguuma
in 1328
AE
, Fel and Merri got their hands on their own
airship
, mostly salvaged from parts, so they could search their son with her. They named her
Sun Chaser
after Merri's family since Fel had named their previous ship after his family. However, Benn turned up safe and returned home some time after the campaign in Maguuma had concluded, much to his parents' relief.
End of Dragons
Fel and Merri traveled to
Arborstone
after it had been renovated and upgraded into an international commercial and travel hub following
Cantha
reopening its borders in 1335
AE
. Much to their delight, they ran into the Pact Commander there and exchanged news of what had happened to them since they had last interacted with the Commander.
Once Fel and Merri had read every book in the library, soaked in the pools, and strolled in the market, they began chatting with local officials about any possible changes to Canthan airspace so they could bring their airship to the continent.

Globalization

Notes
Bug
:
In some news reports Mi-Rae appears as a hologram named MBS Anchor instead, while her proper name appears in the recorded chat box dialogue.
Mi-Rae's dialogue updates based on player progress in
End of Dragons story
.
Mi-Rae's hologram appears with randomized dialogue when approaching the various hologram stands throughout
Cantha
. To trigger dialogue again from the same stand, the
player
has to move a significant distance away before approaching the stand again.
Trivia
Mi-Rae is
voiced
by
Tina Huang
.
Mi-Rae 未來 means "future" in
Korean
.
MBC and KBS and SBS are the largest television networks in Korea. KBS is the national broadcaster of South Korea.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mia Kindleshot
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Mia_Kindleshot
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
,
Living World Season 1
,
Living World Season 4
,
The Icebrood Saga
,
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Mia Kindleshot
Race
Charr
Locations
Caudecus's Manor (story)
(
Kryta
)
The Grotto
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Organization
Iron Legion
Image(s)
Appearance while wielding a
Dragonsblood Rifle
.
“
Wherever Iron goes, we leave the broken wreckage of our enemies behind.
— Mia Kindleshot
Mia Kindleshot
is a
tribune
of the
Iron Legion
who has been one of the driving forces behind the
Ebonhawke Treaty
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Following the
death
of
Smodur the Unflinching
, Mia succeeds him as the new Iron Legion
Imperator
.
After the forming of the
Tyrian Alliance
, she collaborates with
Flame Legion
Imperator
Efram Greetsglory
to create the
luminary
elite specialization
for
guardians
.
Biography
Early years
While in the
Iron Legion
, Mia Kindleshot eventually became one of the legion's
tribunes
. She was considered to be one of the most respected Iron Legion tribunes alongside
Bhuer Goreblade
and
Fume Brighteye
.
Her views had also been noted to be similar to
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
of the Iron Legion.
Mia was said to have been instrumental in arranging the original ceasefire between
charr
and
humans
, and pushed the powers that be to sign the
Ebonhawke Treaty
.
Although Mia's efforts won over many charr who sided with the pro-treaty faction, several other charr in the Iron Legion, notably Fume Brighteye, made their dislike about the truce clear.
At some point, she saw the inventor
Uzolan
in
Ebonhawke
.
Personal story
Because of Mia's contributions to the ceasefire, she was invited to a
party
at
Minister Caudecus
's
manor
that celebrated the peace negotiations in 1325
AE
. As a representative of the Iron Legion, she acted as the charr ambassador to
Kryta
. While at the celebration, she explained to a guest that while she was present as a representative of her legion, she missed her
warband
, who she mentioned were around somewhere and always within her thoughts, and that she was present to further negotiations with Kryta. She also compared the party's host, Caudecus, to the
Flame Legion
. The same guest later questioned her while interrogating delegates at the party on
Logan Thackeray
's orders.
The party was interrupted by
Separatists
who kidnapped
Queen Jennah
and several ambassadors with help from the inventor Uzolan. Mia was placed in a cage where she was to be kept alive until
Separatist Captain DeLana
had finished her business with the captive queen. Logan Thackeray,
Zojja
, and the
Pact Commander
freed the tribune who told them that the queen had been taken farther up the valley behind the manor.
The group ultimately managed to defeat the Separatists and save the queen, allowing Mia to return to her duties.
Living World Season 1
As tribune, Mia was part of the
defense quorum
convened
between Imperator Smodur the Unflinching and the tribunes within
Ascalon
to deal with the threat of the
Molten Alliance
in 1326
AE
. The tribunes argued how to best face the new threat. While conversing with the Pact Commander, Mia mentioned that she agreed with
Makk the Silent
about concentrating their forces around the Black Citadel in case the enemy tried to attack the city next.
Living World Season 4
Leading Iron Legion forces in the area under Imperator Smodur's orders, Mia, alongside her warband, joined the Pact Commander's allied forces in the assault on the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
at
Thunderhead Peaks
in 1332
AE
. Upon meeting the Commander in
Thunderhead Keep
, she expressed the Iron Legion's interest in killing the Elder Dragon and listened to the Commander's speech before participating in the
battle
. During the battle against the Elder Dragon and the
Branded
, Mia fought alongside Logan Thackeray,
Boticca
, and
Rox
. She survived the battle and went back to
High Legion
territories after the
dragon
Aurene
's apparent demise. The survivors would eventually learn that Aurene had
revived
and
slain
Kralkatorrik to take his place as an Elder Dragon with the Commander's help.
The Icebrood Saga
Jormag Rising
During the
charr civil war
between the rogue Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's
Dominion
and the
United Legions
in 1333
AE
, Mia remained loyal to Imperator Smodur. She attempted to have herself reassigned to
Drizzlewood Coast
where Smodur and the other imperators were located, causing Smodur to reply that she was pushing dangerously close to attempting to order him around. Within the letter Smodur had sent back to her, Mia was rebuffed for this and told that the other imperators believed that killing Bangar would collapse his Dominion forces, while Smodur claimed that he knew that the Dominion would only respect a show of force to break their morale. Smodur wanted Mia to lead the war effort on one of the other fronts because the Iron Legion was stretched thin, and he needed his best officers where they would do the most good rather than leaving key strategic locations vulnerable to the Dominion.
After Smodur's
death
at the hands of Bangar's second-in-command
Ryland Steelcatcher
, Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
of the
Ash Legion
noted that Mia would most likely be the next Iron Imperator as Smodur had trusted her. However, the United Legions were unable to contact Mia in time for the assault on Bangar's base of operations at the
Frost Citadel
, and so she did not succeed Smodur as Iron Imperator immediately.
Champions
Mia ascended as the new Iron Imperator some time after the United Legions had
captured
Bangar and defeated the Dominion's main force at Drizzlewood Coast. During the
destroyer
invasion
of Ebonhawke, the Blood Legion's new Imperator
Crecia Stoneglow
noted to the Pact Commander that Mia was cut from the same cloth as Smodur was, though Mia luckily did not aspire to become
Khan-Ur
according to her. Crecia also noted that Mia was popular among the rank and file and had been promoting loyal charr to tribunes within the Iron Legion.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Crecia Stoneglow was elected as the charr representative of the High Legions for the
Tyrian Alliance
's
inaugural meeting
in
Lion's Arch
in 1337
AE
after the sudden
Kryptis
attacks had made several factions consider joining forces to better combat future threats from
Tyria
and beyond. Crecia noted that tensions were rising in the legions due to the disgraced Bangar's impending trial and calls for another Khan-Ur to rise. According to Crecia the imperators were mostly aligned; however, she only named Flame Legion's new Imperator
Efram Greetsglory
and Ash Legion's Malice Swordshadow while leaving Mia out as if to suggest that Mia had not wholly agreed with the other imperators' stance for unknown reasons.
Absolution
Following the Tyrian Alliance's success in ending the
titan
threat in
Janthir
and the
Foundry of Failed Creations
in 1338
AE
, a
victory celebration
was held in Lion's Arch. Mia was among the many guests attending the festivities, taking the time to listen to Queen Jennah's celebration speech before mingling with her fellow imperators. When approached by the Pact Commander, Mia admitted that it had not been easy to step into Smodur's boots and it had taken this long after the charr civil war for the Iron Legion to feel normal again. However, she also suggested that the legions would have more good news to come without elaborating on the specifics.
Visions of Eternity
As collaboration between the members of the Tyrian Alliance deepened, the four High Legions began even deeper collaboration to combine their respective strengths in 1338
AE
. Mia
contributed
to the initiative by working with Efram Greetsglory to create the
luminary
elite specialization
for
guardians
who would wield Iron Legion torches which would emanate a radiant flame reclaimed by the Flame Legion. She also potentially sent a personal letter to the Pact Commander to seek
Petrus Dawnwelder
out for further discussions about the craft that could be of use to guardians.

======================================================================
PAGE: Minister Arton
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Minister_Arton
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Minister Arton
Race
Human
Locations
Accusation
(
Rurikton
,
The Upper City
,
Delanian Foothills
)
Party Politics
(
The Crown Pavilion
,
Plaza of Balthazar
,
Balthazar Low Road
)
A Meeting of Ministers
(
The Upper City
)
Organization
Ministry
“
A minister would never betray our soldiers. I wish this matter investigated immediately, Your Majesty. The Ministry's honor is at stake.
—
Minister Arton
Minister Arton
is a
minister
loyal to
Queen Jennah
and a close friend of
Legate Minister
Caudecus Beetlestone
. Despite his stance, Arton's reputation has suffered a blow from rumors accusing him of conspiring with the enemies of
Divinity's Reach
against the crown.
Biography
Personal story
Evidence uncovered during investigations in 1325
AE
seemingly indicated that Arton had set up the fall of
Falcon Company
at the hands of
centaurs
to undermine the
detente
between humanity and the
charr
. However, thanks to further investigations by the
Hero of Shaemoor
, Arton was ultimately found innocent and released from custody. Arton had been set up by his supposed friend Caudecus, though there was no proof and the minister remained unaware of this fact.
Living World Season 2
During a party in 1327
AE
, Arton made a brief appearance. He suggested that the
bandits
found throughout
Kryta
were connected to the
White Mantle
cult. He also believed that the cult ran the black market in
Divinity's Reach
, though he gave no insight into what led him to this theory. Arton also vouched for Queen Jennah when questioned by the
Pact Commander
about the queen's involvement with the
sylvari
terrorist
Scarlet Briar
, stating that Jennah had politely refused to allow Scarlet to study with the designer of the
Watchknights
and that the sylvari had become livid as a result.
Living World Season 3
After Caudecus Beetlestone revealed himself as the Confessor of the White Mantle, Arton was put under much scrutiny due to his then-broken friendship with the former
Legate Minister
. Despite this, he continued proclaiming confidence and support in Queen Jennah, who in turn offered her trust.
When Jennah called
a Meeting of Ministers
and the White Mantle
laid siege
to Divinity's Reach from
Lake Doric
in 1330
AE
, Arton was among the first to be slain by turncoat
Ministers
and
Ministry Guards
. Upon Arton's death, Jennah cried out his name in dismay and fought alongside the Pact Commander against the invaders. The White Mantle sympathizers and spies were ultimately driven back, and the siege ended with a crushing blow against the White Mantle forces and the death of Confessor Caudecus.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 2: Shadows of the Past

The Dragon's Reach: Part 1
Party Politics
Living World Season 3

The Head of the Snake

======================================================================
PAGE: Minister Caudecus
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Minister_Caudecus
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Edge of Destiny
, the
personal story
,
Living World Season 3
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Minister Caudecus
Race
Human
Profession
Thief
Locations
Formal Garden
(
Caudecus's Manor
)
Hidden Trail
(
Caudecus's Manor
)
Organization
Ministry
“
Kryta will win through. As we ever have. Just leave everything to me.
—
Minister Caudecus
Legate Minister Lord Caudecus Beetlestone the Wise
is the charismatic and cunning
Legate Minister
of the
Krytan Ministry
and father of
Demmi Beetlestone
. As Legate Minister, he is responsible for overseeing the functions of
the Ministry
. The
Commander
of the
Ministry Guard
reports directly to him.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
Caudecus eventually reveals himself as the
Confessor
of the
White Mantle
who has been plotting to dethrone
Queen Jennah
for years via various schemes involving
bandits
and
centaurs
. He is
killed
during the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
, and is succeeded by
Minister Wi
as Legate Minister and by
Lord Faren
as the Lord of
Beetletun
.
Biography
Early years
Caudecus's official portrait with the Beetle
heraldic badge
of the Beetlestone family. The writing below it reads "Lord Beetlestone" in
New Krytan
.
Caudecus Beetlestone married the cousin of
Lady Wi
and fathered
Demmi Beetlestone
. He served as a general in the
War of Ascalon Independence
until the eventual
truce
between
humans
and
charr
which he opposed ever since.
With his experience and popularity, Caudecus rose to the position of
Legate Minister
in the
Krytan Ministry
and emerged as a serious political rival to
Queen Jennah
, whose egalitarian rule had eroded her support with the nobility and whose failure to hold back the
centaurs
and
bandits
plaguing
Kryta
had caused some of the common folk to begin to doubt the queen's ability to defend her nation.
In addition to his activities in
Divinity's Reach
, Caudecus was credited with the expansion of
Beetletun
, home to both his own
mansion
and a statue of himself. His carnivals allowed him to amass a fortune which in turn allowed him to retain the services of many
Ministry Guards
and
Seraph
. According to Caudecus, the number of Ministry Guards was the reason why the bandits and centaurs did not attack Beetletun and allowed his shire to be relatively prosperous.
Caudecus's wife died several years ago, reportedly killed by bandits, which left their daughter Demmi as Caudecus's only family. Lady Wi stepped in as a godmother to Demmi, being the young Demmi's guide while the Legate Minister was busy handling administrative matters. To keep track of his daughter's movements, Caudecus purchased a tracking enchantment and secretly placed it on the necklace of his departed wife which Demmi was wearing.
Edge of Destiny
In 1319
AE
, Caudecus was present in Queen Jennah's throne room where Jennah named the young gladiator
Logan Thackeray
, who had impressed her, as her champion under the eyes of the nobles and ministers. During the ceremony the queen also made it clear that champions like Logan would protect her from mortal peril, and that all who plotted against the throne should beware the consequences.
When Logan replaced his late brother
Dylan Thackeray
as a
Captain of the Seraph
in the aftermath of the
Ogre Revolt
in 1320
AE
, Caudecus became interested in the new captain and decorated
Beetlestone Manor
with several paintings of Logan, including his bedroom.
Interim years
Some time later, Caudecus met
Lieutenant Serentine
of the Ministry Guard who was asking for his help when the Seraph were taking over the case of solving the murder of
Minister Brios
by bandits. The Legate Minister revealed his dislike for the Seraph's incompetence but suggested that Serentine follow the queen's orders while he would make sure that the Ministry Guard was not at fault should the perpetrators not be found.
Serentine was eventually promoted to Commander of the Ministry Guard, and she served Caudecus loyally since.
Personal story
A statue of Caudecus with hounds at his feet, found in
Beetletun
.
In 1325
AE
, Caudecus became suspected of orchestrating various bandit operations, the capture of the
Falcon Company
and framing
Minister Arton
for their supposed deaths, as well as being behind
Minister Zamon
's dealings with the bandits. However, no solid proof was provided for any of these cases as most of the suspects ended up dead, preventing prosecution. Through his contacts, Caudecus learned of the assassination plot headed by the
White Mantle
Confessor Esthel
and sent an anonymous letter to the enigmatic spymaster
E
who responded with a thinly veiled threat should the lead run cold.
However, the rumors ultimately turned out to be true, and
Shining Blade
operatives
raided
the White Mantle hideout in the
Hunting Lodge
, ending Confessor Esthel's life and the schemes of her White Mantle cell in one blow.
During this time Caudecus took note of the
Hero of Shaemoor
, who had been involved in solving many of the cases which had threatened the queen's regime, and gently offered counsel to the Hero. However, the Hero grew suspicious of the Legate Minister after investigating the cases and Caudecus's suspected albeit unproven involvement in them with Logan Thackeray and
Countess Anise
. Later, these suspicions grew when Demmi Beetlestone defected to the
Order of Whispers
with the help of
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
while claiming to have evidence of shady dealings by her father. Caudecus dispatched
Ministry Commander Landon
to recover Demmi, but Landon perished during an
ambush
orchestrated by Demmi, Tybalt Leftpaw, and a Whispers
Lightbringer
.
Frustrated by his daughter's rebellion and learning that Lady Wi may have been an encouraging influence behind Demmi's actions, Caudecus set his sights on Lady Wi's young and impressionable daughter
Valette Wi
. As a patron of the arts, the Legate Minister began supporting Valette's violin playing with expensive gifts. He had private meetings with her during which Valette became convinced that the queen's administration was lacking and that Caudecus's political faction would be needed to make things right for Krytans.
Caudecus
hosted a party
at his estate, Beetlestone Manor, to celebrate the human-charr détente. There, he promised to unveil a new creation by
Uzolan the Artist
, his personal inventor, who ultimately turned out to be behind a
Separatist
plot by Captain
Monique DeLana
to kill Queen Jennah. Fortunately, the soon-to-be
Pact Commander
,
Zojja
, and Logan Thackeray were able to stop the plot before either the queen or the Legate Minister could come to harm. Following this, at Jennah's insistence, Caudecus was taken back to the
Royal Palace
where he remained, officially for his safety, while his manor was
investigated
for further Separatist and bandit activities.
Living World Season 3
Out of the Shadows
Lazarus
reveals
himself to Caudecus and
Lady Valette Wi
.
Caudecus fled from the Royal Palace in 1329
AE
, prompting the
Shining Blade
officer
Canach
, the Pact Commander, and members of the
Dragon's Watch
guild
to track him down and bring him back by any means necessary.
When found in
Bloodstone Fen
experimenting on
Bloodstone
shards, Caudecus
revealed himself
as the current Confessor of the
White Mantle
and announced his plan to take over the Krytan throne from Queen Jennah by force.
However, the sudden arrival of
Lazarus
, the last
mursaat
whom a faction led by High Inquisitor
Xera
had been attempting to revive behind Caudecus's back, led to the White Mantle splintering into two factions when Caudecus refused to submit to Lazarus, who in turn branded Caudecus as a heretic.
Before he could be killed, Caudecus fled once more with the assistance of his mesmer underling, Lady Valette Wi, to continue his schemes elsewhere.
The Head of the Snake
After escaping, Caudecus gathered his resources, sent
letters
to the White Mantle in hopes of preventing them from joining Lazarus's schism while proclaiming the mursaat a "false god", tried to sway Krytans to his side with
posted messages
openly criticizing Queen Jennah's reign, and was rumored to have headed north for the
Isles of Janthir
.
Caudecus eventually reemerged in Kryta by leading a
siege
on Divinity's Reach from
Lake Doric
in 1330
AE
with a combined force of White Mantle and Bloodstone-crazed
Harathi
centaurs. The Confessor ordered one of his underlings,
Minister Estelle
, to assassinate Jennah during an
assault
on
the Upper City
, although the attempt was thwarted by a timely intervention from the Pact Commander and Logan Thackeray. The siege was quickly turned back by the Seraph, bolstered by
Legionnaire
Nylia Steelpaw
's
warband
, due to the White Mantle's weakened state by defectors.
Witnessing his plans crashing around him, the Confessor was ultimately forced to take shelter as he sought out to unleash a "contingency plan" in his desperation. As he did so, Canach and a group of Shining Blade infiltrated the manor, but were all killed or captured. Following the loss of contact with Canach's squad, the Pact Commander, Demmi Beetlestone, and Countess Anise sneaked into the manor from the
Hidden Falls Dock
connecting to Lake Doric to bring down the traitorous minister for good.
Caudecus with
Lady Valette Wi
and
Demmi Beetlestone
.
Discarded letters
found while navigating through the manor revealed that Caudecus had not only been behind the fall of Falcon Company and Minister Zamon, but he had also ordered
Commander Serentine
to free
captured bandits
, had
Captain Tervelan
butcher
Ulgoth the Mighty
's family to further spur the
Centaur War
, hired Separatists as his carnival workers and spurred the
Ringmaster
and Separatist Captain Monique DeLana's attacks. Caudecus had also orchestrated the death of
Confessor Esthel
, his predecessor, through feeding E with information on the White Mantle in order to rise through the cult's ranks.
When Caudecus was finally
found
in the
Grand Salon
in his manor alongside Valette, he had begun to go
crazed
from consuming large amounts of empowering Bloodstone. During the fight, the Confessor admitted to having his wife murdered and fatally shot Demmi with the misguided belief that they could become a family again in
the Mists
, which prompted Valette to switch sides as she realized how unhinged Caudecus had become. Before he could be caught, the Confessor retreated to a lower dungeon decorated with his own face across the walls.
By the time the Commander's party reached him with Valette's help, Caudecus had grown to become an unrecognizable version of himself, partially becoming a bloodstone abomination. Here, in the depths of his manor, Caudecus, the ambitious puppetmaster who had engineered so many plots against Kryta and the crown over the years, was finally killed.
Legacy
Discoveries among Caudecus's belongings
Further investigation of the manor after Caudecus's death revealed that he had obtained an artifact, which supposedly held the fifth and final
aspect
of Lazarus, from
Justiciar Bauer
who had been working for both Caudecus and Xera. Caudecus's journal claimed that without the last aspect the ritual to revive the fallen mursaat should have failed, which explained Caudecus's surprise at seeing Lazarus appear in Bloodstone Fen. The revelation prompted the Pact Commander to investigate whether the Lazarus who had been encountered earlier was a real mursaat or
an imposter
.
With Caudecus no longer backing them up and with the Shining Blade tracking down what remained of the once formidable organization, the White Mantle began to fall apart.
After the
death
of the last known justiciar
Araya
and the arrest of several White Mantle spies within the
orders
and Kryta, the threat which the White Mantle had been posing to Kryta for centuries came to an end.
Albert
, Caudecus's loyal butler, secured many of his former master's valued possessions from the manor before looters could get to them and sold them at
Lakeside Bazaar
. The deaths of both Caudecus and Demmi ended the Beetlestone line; Queen Jennah appointed
Lord Faren
as the new Lord of Beetletun, while
Minister Wi
succeeded Caudecus as Legate Minister in the Krytan Ministry.
Janthir Wilds
In Janthir Wilds, it is revealed that Caudecus traveled to Janthir during the events of
Heart of Thorns
. The goal of his travels was to follow in the footsteps of
Saul D'Alessio
, hoping to find the fabled city of
Bava Nisos
.

Trivia
Caudecus is
voiced
by
Mark Bramhall
.
Caudecus Beetlestone is named and modeled after
ArenaNet
Environment Art Lead
Dave Beetlestone
.
Caduceus
is the staff carried by Hermes Trismegistus in Greco-Egyptian mythology and Hermes in Greek mythology. The same staff was also borne by heralds in general, for example by Iris, the messenger of Hera.
"Caudecus", a
track
composed by
Maclaine Diemer
, is named after him.
Caudecus's abomination form in
Confessor's End
is caused by the same condition that
Matthias Gabrel
has in
Salvation Pass
except that Caudecus's case is at a much earlier state, thus weaker than Matthias's abomination form.

Minister Duran
Race

I'm heartened by Ambassador Steelcrush's attitude. I really think we can find compromises that help both of our people.
— Minister Duran
Minister Kent Duran
is the
human
Krytan
ambassador sent to negotiate the
treaty
with the
charr
at
Summit Peak
. In 1325
AE
, a new leader of the charr
Renegades
,
Ajax Anvilburn
, planned to kill him and put the blame on the charr
Ambassador Aximus Steelcrusher
to disrupt the ceasefire between humans and charr. This plot was brought to Duran and Steelcrusher's attention by
Eilye Jeyne
,
Warmaster Forgal Kernsson
, and
Forgal's recruit
, and Ajax was swiftly dealt with, putting a halt to the Renegades' plan.

Dialogue
I thought once we got to the peace talks, it'd be easy. But this has been as much a challenge as anything we've faced before!
Why is that?
It's the extremists. The Renegades would rather kill other charr than accept peace with the humans. I hear they're stockpiling munitions for an offensive against the summit itself!
What about the local humans? How are they taking the treaty?
Ebonhawke
wants peace, but some humans are willing to burn the whole treaty down rather than admit the charr have won
Ascalon
! They call themselves
Separatists
. I'd say "lunatics" is a better name.

Hopefully that'll be enough.
I wish you luck then.
Good luck then.
After years of war, I'm surprised you thought any of this would be easy.

This article is about the
Canthan
Minister of Security
.  For the material used in crafting
legendary armor
, see
Legendary Insight
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
In this
Canthan
name, the
family name
is Li.
Minister Li
Race
Human
Profession
Bladesworn
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Kaineng Overlook
(
New Kaineng City
)
Organization
Ministry of Security
“
My understanding is you're something of a "protector of the realm"; doing whatever's necessary to keep your people safe. Well, it is my duty to keep the people of Cantha safe.
—
Minister Li Ha-Eung
Minister Li Ha-Eung
is the head of the
Ministry of Security
. As he tries to keep
Cantha
safe, he is distrustful of outsiders. He is a traditionalist and can come across as very aggressive and stern to others. Li has a mentor-mentee relationship with
Detective Rama
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Li is eventually revealed to be the White Falcon, one of the leaders of the
Purists
, who has been plotting against
Empress Ihn
's regime. After he is
arrested
,
Precinct Captain Min
succeeds him as the acting Minister of Security.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add information from lore books etc.
End of Dragons
Act I
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Act II
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Minister Li was arguing with
Joon
via hologram, threatening to talk to
Empress Ihn
about her actions, when he spotted the Commander and their allies in the
Yong Reactor
. He accused Joon of ignoring national security by allowing foreigners to visit this secret location, but before he could say anything more she hung up on him to greet her guests.
Act IV
Detective Rama was unable to contact Minister Li to bring news of his investigation into
Ankka
and her
Extractor
as evidence to clear the Pact Commander of wrongdoing. After completing tasks for MinSec, Rama and the Commander were able to secure a meeting with Minister Li. While Li was adamant about apprehending the Commander, Rama argued in favour of the Commander in light of the evidence he discovered. Li stood his ground until the Commander placed the broken Extractor onto his desk to prove that Ankka was responsible. Li found the story far-fetched. Rama pressed that the immediate threat was the
Soo-won
loose in the
Jade Sea
. Rama implored Li to trust him by referring to him by his given name, Ha-Eung. Li relented, but warned Rama not to use his given name again.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Minister Li the White Falcon of the Purists.
Li led Rama and the Commander to an elevator where he had arranged transport, where they were joined by MinSec specialists. During the elevator ride, Li and Rama reminisced over the progress made in
New Kaineng
as they watched over the city. Upon reaching the rooftop, Li revealed his deception, ordering his men to subdue the Commander. Rama and the Commander resisted Li and the MinSec specialists.
Precinct Captain Min
communicated with Rama with a
Jade Bot
, revealing that she had connected Li with the
Purists
and attempted to warn Rama. Li held himself on the high ground and dueled against Rama until while the Commander finished his MinSec henchmen. Li criticised Rama's position on Cantha's progress and accused him of diluting their culture.
With Min's assistance, the Commander reached Li's position and subdued him. He was arrested by Min and Rama.
Act V
Li turned out to be surprisingly cooperative while in custody, providing names of some of his co-conspirators to the authorities during interrogation. His only wish was for someone to look after his dog Choby. Cho Min was appointed as the Acting Minister of Security until Li's proper successor would be named.

Skills
Artillery Slash
- Minister Li fires a shell at long-range targets that explode on the first enemy hit.
Dragon Slash—Burst
- Minister Li fires a bolt of energy at targets. This bolt explodes on impact, dealing damage to targets in an area.
Dragon Slash—Rush
- Minister Li launches forward to a target location, damaging and launching enemies in his path.
Dragon Slash—Wave
- Minister Li swings his blade with mighty strength, creating a cone-shaped shock wave that deals significant damage and launches enemies.
Swift Cut
- Slash your foe.
Steel Divide
- Strike your foe again.
Explosive Thrust
- Hit your foe with a final, brutal stab, and fire a shot that causes a secondary explosion.

- Minister Li fires a bolt of energy at targets in sequential order. This bolt explodes on impact, dealing significant damage and applying extreme vulnerability to targets in an area. Targets with extreme vulnerability take significant damage.
Dragon Slash—Burst
(challenge mode only)
- Minister Li fires a bolt of energy at targets in sequential order. This bolt explodes on impact, dealing significant damage and applying extreme vulnerability to targets in an area. Targets with extreme vulnerability are killed by this attack.

- Minister Li launches forward to a target location, damaging and launching enemies in his path.
Dragon Slash—Wave
- Minister Li swings his blade with mighty strength, creating a cone-shaped shock wave that deals significant damage and launches enemies.

PAGE: Minister Wi
CATEGORIES: Story characters

======================================================================

Minister Wi
Race
Human
Locations
A Society Function
(
Rurikton
)
Party Politics
(
The Crown Pavilion
,
Plaza of Balthazar
,
Balthazar Low Road
)
A Meeting of Ministers
(
The Upper City
)
Organization
Ministry
Minister Wi
is the
Krytan Ministry
representative for
Rurikton
and the husband of
Lady Wi
. He is considered to be a man of great learning, calm demeanor, and surprising generosity. Minister Wi is known for his
landmark mansion
and his spectacular galas, which are often for a good cause. His daughter
Valette
is a famous violinist who is revered by many within the city and has even played for
Queen Jennah
several times.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
With the
White Mantle
coming out of the shadows, Minister Wi and his wife were scrutinized by others for Valette joining the White Mantle. Despite this, he remained stalwartly supporting Queen Jennah during the crisis. With
Legate Minister Caudecus
's
death
and the end of the White Mantle's siege from
Lake Doric
, Minister Wi was promoted to
Legate Minister
.
Story involvement
Personal story

A Society Function
Living World Season 2
Episode 4:
The Dragon's Reach: Part 2
Party Politics
Living World Season 3
Episode 4:
The Head of the Snake
A Meeting of Ministers
Trivia
He is of
Canthan
descent.

======================================================================
PAGE: Minister Zamon
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Minister_Zamon
======================================================================

Minister Zamon
Race
Human
Rank
Champion
Location
Divinity's Reach
(
Kryta
)
Organization
Ministry
White Mantle
“
I only... did... as I was told...
— Minister Julius Zamon
Minister Julius Zamon
was the corrupt
Krytan Ministry
representative for the
Ossan Quarter
. In 1325
AE
, it was discovered that Zamon had been sending orders to at least some of the
bandits
in
Shaemoor Fields
, having somehow "obtained" a chalice that was stolen from the
Corone family
. He had also received a letter from his sister,
Lady Madeline
, detailing his connections to a black market, and had been found with blueprints of all the houses that had been recently burgled in the
Salma District
. Additionally, someone had been sending the
Ministry Guard
away from soon-to-be bandit targets, just before they were raided.
Zamon was arrested and called to trial (presided over by
Legate Minister Caudecus
). Rather than refute the accusations, he chose to invoke his right to
trial by combat
to clear the charges against him. How Zamon knew about this ancient law was unknown as he was no historian;
Countess Anise
suspected that Caudecus might have been backing Zamon and had advised the minister about the clause in secret. Zamon died while fighting the
Hero of Shaemoor
in the trial and was therefore declared guilty posthumously. However, Zamon's death also left no loose ends to pursue regarding any other potential bandit ties to the Ministry.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
Through the letters found in
Caudecus's Correspondence
, it is revealed that Zamon had joined the
White Mantle
under Caudecus's command. He threatened to expose Caudecus when he was brought to trial if Caudecus wouldn't get him excused.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Krytan Politics
Welcome Home
Search the Premises
A Society Function
The Trial of Julius Zamon

Effects
Skills

-
Damage
nearby foes with a ring of fire,
burning
foes that pass through it.
Static Field
- Create an electrical field that
stuns
foes crossing it.
Water Blast
- Spray a jet of water at your foe that splashes to
heal
nearby allies.
Stolen skills

Ministry Commander Landon
Race

Rank

Organization

“
Lady Beetlestone, if you think a few apple barrels will stop me, you're going to be disappointed.
— Commander Landon
Ministry Commander Landon
was a recent
Commander of Divinity's Reach
. Landon was the successor to
Commander Serentine
. In 1325
AE
,
Legate Minister Caudecus
put Landon on the trail of his daughter,
Demmi Beetlestone
, who had defected to the
Order of Whispers
. After Demmi was kidnapped by
Captain Jayne
of the
Jackdaw Pirates
and subsequently rescued by
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
and a Whispers Initiate, Landon and his men followed the group through
Lion's Arch
towards
Stormbluff Isle
, where they were caught in an ambush and ultimately killed.

Notes
She will appear in the player's
home instance
after completing The Battle of Claw Island.
Bug
:
In some cases, Mira will fail to appear in the Black Citadel
home instance
, even if she appears in the other cities.
Anomaly
:
In some instances, such as
Rata Sum
and
Hoelbrak
, Mira will be laying down, bald, scarred, and wearing a patch on one eye. In others, like Divinity's Reach and the Grove, she appears as she does in the Claw Island story instances, and is standing.

Monti Scythescrape
Race
Charr
Location
Reunion with the Pact
(
Vandal's Claim
,
Camp Resolve
)
Organization
Blood Legion
“
Come closer, and I'll give you a taste of how charming I can be.
—
Monti Scythescrape
Monti Scythescrape
is a
charr
soldier who has been dealing discreetly with
Rox
. When questioned by the
Pact Commander
in
Camp Resolve
, neither Monti nor Rox are willing to divulge the exact details of how they know one another although Monti does not seem to hold Rox in high regard.
Story involvement
Living World Season 2
Episode 5:
Echoes of the Past
Reunion with the Pact
(as Soldier)
Notes
Although the charr Pact Commander notes that Monti does not appear to be wearing any legion or order gear that would identify him, Monti is in fact wearing tier 3 of
charr cultural medium armor
with a red color scheme, suggesting a potential affiliation with the
Blood Legion
.

Morraloo

are around them. Morraloo says they were blackmailed into carrying the Aetherblades' offerings to the effigy. They are then convinced to return to Lion's Arch.

Morrigu
Race
Sylvari

4, 55

)

Appearance in Twilight Arbor.
Interactive map
Morrigu
is a sylvari whose
Wyld Hunt
is to protect
Caer Verdant
and regularly fight the
Risen
in the area. She is the former lover of a
Nightmare Courtier
,
Leurent
.
When
Renvari
was captured, she,
Brugh
, and
Blanche
were sent off to scout as the
Nightmare Court
sent an attack to free the
Wardens
' prisoner. After
Faolain
was chased out of
Twilight Arbor
, she heads there to defend refugees and track down Laurent,
Vevina
, and
Fyonna
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mort Duserm
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Mort_Duserm
======================================================================

Captain Mort Duserm
was the first guard captain of
Commodore Marriner
's
Lionguard
. A roaring drunkard, Duserm was the only person who'd stepped up to take the position. By 1256
AE
, he had been replaced by
Bronn Svaard
.

Mr. Sparkles
Race
Golem
Rank
Veteran
Location
Snaff Memorial Lab
(
Rata Sum
)
Organization
Destiny's Edge
Image(s)
Interactive map
Mr. Sparkles
is a
golem
created by the
asura
Flummox
and named by his
sylvari
assistant,
Sareb
. When traveling through the
Shiverpeak Mountains
, their caravan got intercepted by
jotun
and the golem detonated while fighting them. Later, because Flummox needed money Sareb sold the golem to
Zojja
, who then heavily customized it and currently owns it. Sareb used her share of money from this trade to help
Evon Gnashblade
found the
Black Lion Trading Company
.
After the destruction the Pact fleet sent to confront Mordremoth, Mr. Sparkles is discovered in Verdant Brink badly damaged from either the crash or in the Mordrem attacks that followed to the point where it cannot even be salvaged for parts.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mysterious Stranger
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Mysterious_Stranger
======================================================================

This article is about the character seen in
Split Second
.  For the character seen in
Tracking the Aspect Masters
, see
Mysterious Stranger (Tracking the Aspect Masters)
.
Mysterious Stranger
(This NPC's appearance may change based on the present characters.)
Race
Asura
Location
Split Second
(
The Wendon Steps
)
“
I can do anything I want. And here, as in my world, the only obstacle to my transcendence is you. To become all that I am capable of becoming, you must die. It's my destiny...and my pleasure.
—
Mysterious Stranger
The
Mysterious Stranger
, also known as the
Grand High Sovereign
, is the ostensibly alternate reality version of the
Snaff Savant
who came out of the portal created by the
Infinity Ball
. This version of the Savant had gone evil and conquered
Tyria
with their creations, the
steam creatures
. Ultimately the Sovereign and their ally,
Grand Vice Admiral Shodd
, were defeated by the Snaff Savant,
Shodd
, and
Zojja
as soon as they appeared in 1325
AE
, ending the threat before it could spread.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 2: Interested Parties
Split Second
Combat abilities
Skills
Nova Blast
Shot
Volley
Stolen skills
Blinding Tuft
Notes
The appearance of the Mysterious Stranger will be exactly like the player character, except the Mysterious Stranger will be wearing the
Adept
asuran racial armor and wields a
Peacemaker Rifle
.
Despite their claims, the Mysterious Stranger and Grand Vice Admiral Shodd do not actually come from an alternate reality but from a potential future that exists within
the Mists
, suggesting that they may have been
fractal
anomalies which manifested because of the Infinity Ball.
References

have disappeared into. Little is known about him or his motives at this time, other than he appears to be human.

Necromancer Rissa
was an
asura
necromancer
that somehow became corrupted by the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, even though she appears to still be among the living. She had taken a
Dragon Crystal
and broke fragments off of it, making amulets out of the fragments which she then spread across
Tyria
.
Howl the Brazen
was given one such amulet that twisted him into a
Risen
upon death.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nenah
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Nenah
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Nenah
Race
Ghost
Level
80
Locations
The Sanctum
(
The Mists
)
Reclaimed Chantry
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
“
I'm where I ought to be. I accept whatever the afterlife has in store for me. You should, too.
—
Nenah
Nenah
is a
ghost
who can be first encountered inside
Judgment
. Originally a
lost spirit
, she reclaimed her name and memories. In life, she was a teacher, and after learning about herself in the
Domain of the Lost
she began helping other Lost Souls, including the
Pact Commander
, to recover their memories and
purpose
before moving on to her final resting place,
The Sanctum
. After settling into the Sanctum, Nenah briefly appears to those kneeling at the Statue of
Kormir
in the
Reclaimed Chantry
.
Biography
Early years
In life, Nenah was a teacher who secretly worshipped the
goddess
Kormir
in the
Domain of Kourna
. In 1330
AE
, she had to flee her home because she refused to teach
Palawa Joko
's propaganda. However, she was killed by the rogue god
Balthazar
's
Forged
army which had recently arrived in
Elona
.
Because of her traumatic death and her life's goal having gone unfulfilled, Nenah ended up in the
Domain of the Lost
as a
lost spirit
who had lost both her memories and
purpose
. She eventually reclaimed her name by winning a game of riddles against the spirit of her former mentor, and her purpose by discovering a hidden diary she had written as a child. However, she was too scared to go stand in front of
the Judge
, a servant of
Grenth
who judged and sent spirits to their appointed afterlives, as she feared what afterlife she would be sent to. She decided to spend her time helping other lost spirits while postponing her own judgement.
Path of Fire
While in the Domain of the Lost, Nenah conversed with Palawa Joko, whom Balthazar had betrayed and imprisoned in a cage during their visit to the domain. Joko tried to convince Nenah to set him free by promising her a second chance at life, only for Nenah to reject his offer as she did not want to serve him as an
Awakened
. Instead of continuing listening to Joko who was not willing to accept his fate, she turned her attention to a recently arrived lost spirit and suggested visiting the Judge who in turn said the lost spirit should recover its weapons, name and purpose with Nenah's aid.
The pair succeeded in the two tasks, and Nenah discovered that the lost spirit was the
Pact Commander
who had died in combat against Balthazar. They returned to the Judge to ask for the Commander to be returned to the land of the living, only to find out that the only potential way to reanimate the Commander's corpse was to claim the life force of a fearsome beast, the
Eater of Souls
, which had arrived in the domain to prey on the spirits residing there.
Nenah witnessed the Commander defeating the Eater of Souls and freeing the spirits it had consumed after a fierce struggle and wished the Commander luck. Realizing there was no point in postponing the inevitable, she returned to the Judge to receive her judgement and final reward. Much to Nenah's surprise, the Judge awarded her bravery in life by granting her access to
the Sanctum
, Kormir's great library in the
Realm of Torment
, where Nenah would spend the rest of her days as a librarian documenting knowledge in her goddess's name.
However, Kormir departing from her realm to join the other gods on their journey left the Sanctum vulnerable to malignant forces from
the Mists
which invaded the area and turned it into the Dark Library.
Many of Kormir's librarians perished in the assault, but Nenah managed to save herself by hiding in Kormir's secret room within the library. She was in disbelief that the gods would just abandon their followers, but resolved to protect what knowledge she could from the
demons
so it would not be lost.
Nenah eventually reunited with the Commander who was revisiting the Sanctum on a
quest
on behalf of
Tahlkora
after Balthazar's demise. She informed the Commander what had happened since their previous encounter as well as where to find books on the
Sunspears
which the Commander was after. She later learned that the Commander had defeated several powerful demonic entities guarding the stolen keys for the chests containing the Sunspear books, and in so doing had restored some semblance of hope to the fallen library.
Living World Season 4
Despite the initial demon invasion, the Dark Library was eventually reclaimed and became known as the Sanctum once more. In 1331
AE
, Nenah was summoned to the
Reclaimed Chantry
in
Jahai Bluffs
after the Pact Commander had shown respect to the
Statue of Kormir
by kneeling in front of it. After the initial confusion caused by their unexpected reunion, the Commander informed the surprised Nenah that Palawa Joko had
died
and Elona was finally free from the lich king's grip.
Upon revisiting the statue, the Commander summoned Nenah's spirit again to warn her about the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
who had used its new abilities to breach the Mists. Nenah let the Commander know that she was already aware of the threat Kralkatorrik posed to the dead and the living alike and how spirits had been seeking refuge from the Elder Dragon in the Sanctum. Nenah speculated that the reason for the gods' departure might be that they had foreseen this outcome. Before Nenah returned to the Sanctum, the Commander hoped that she would watch out for herself.
Locations
Crystal Desert
Jahai Bluffs
Reclaimed Chantry
The Mists
Realm of Torment
The Sanctum
Story involvement
Path of Fire story
Act III
The Departing
Side Stories
:
Open Skies
Open Skies: Sunspear Wisdom
Dialogue
The Sanctum
I didn't think I'd ever see you again,
Commander
. Does this mean...?
Balthazar
has been
dealt with
.
It seems we were both rewarded for our
trials
in the
Domain of the Lost
.
Are you safe here?
Mostly. The things outside haven't found this inner sanctum...yet. And I just can't bear to let all this wisdom be forgotten. I'll stay as long as I can.
Have you seen any books on the
Sunspears
?
(As below)

Have you seen any books on the Sunspears?
Kormir
did preserve some writings, but they're out there, with the
demons
. I haven't managed to obtain any, though I suspect they're still safe. They're locked inside
sturdy chests
.

Why were you in the Domain of the Lost?
I suppose my life's purpose had gone unfulfilled, leaving me lost. A teacher cannot teach lies, and that's all
Joko
left me—so I fled his kingdom and died soon after.
Are you safe here?

I'm sorry, but I can't stay.
Jahai Bluffs
In the
Reclaimed Chantry
, after kneeling at the Statue of Kormir
Commander?! What—? This... I can sense... the old Chantry? But how... One moment I was sorting scroll— the next I felt an intense tug on my spirit, and then... I'm in
Kourna
. I'm...home.
My apologies for the jarring trip. I had no idea that would happen.
No! No apology is necessary. Although I can't smell the air or feel the ground beneath my feet, it's good to be in
Elona
again. I'm curious though... How did you summon me?
I knelt at Kormir's statue and thanked her for her help with Balthazar.
The
magic
of the
gods
remains strong, even in their absence. Very few of the original god statues were left standing after Joko. Wait... something's changed. I sense...
We've
put an end
to Joko. Elona is free.
Oh, Commander. To be free, even as a spirit... You've done so much for the
world
. I... must go now. I hope you'll visit this shrine again. It's good to be home.
Farewell, Nenah.
I should go.
Let's chat another time.
I'll... leave you to that.
In the Reclaimed Chantry, after kneeling at the Statue of Kormir (for the second time)
Commander, you've come back. Was there something else you wished of me?
I need to warn you—
Kralkatorrik
is running rampart in
the Mists
.
Yes, spirits have come to the Sanctum, seeking refuge. They believe the gods' realm to be safe, but we don't truly know. Maybe this is why the gods departed so long ago. They foresaw this.
I hope you'll watch out for yourself, Nenah.
Nothing right now.
Related achievements
A Star to Guide Us
:
Ethereal Exchange
—
I have to stop Kralkatorrik for
Nenah
and all the others in the Mists who are in danger.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nettleblaze
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Nettleblaze
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
Nettleblaze
Race
Spirit
Locations
Arrival and Survival
(
Rata Sum Port Authority
,
Draconis Mons
)
In Pursuit of a God
(
Draconis Mons
)
Organization
Druids
“
You are intruding upon one of nature's purest gardens.
—
Nettleblaze
Nettleblaze
is a
druid
encountered in
Draconis Mons
. He guides the
Pact Commander
in the pursuit of
Balthazar
.
Biography
Early years
Nettleblaze was one of the
druids
, a group of
human
worshipers of the
goddess
Melandru
, who eventually transcended their mortal bodies in a ritual and became guardians of nature. He and his brethren were drawn to a submerged sanctum of nature which eventually became known as the hollowed volcano of
Draconis Mons
as they built one of Melandru's first gardens there.
After finding his way into the sanctum in 1173
AE
, the ambitious
asura
Zinn
and other survivors from
Rata Novus
founded a new colony named
Rata Arcanum
there and captured some of the druids for their terraforming experiments. The remaining druids tried to make the colonists leave, but Zinn ignored their pleas and told them to leave. Angered, the druids returned with more of their kind in 1175
AE
and killed the colonists in retaliation for their crimes against nature.
Living World Season 3
In 1330
AE
, a small army consisting of
White Mantle
and
mercenaries
invaded the island in their hunt for the
Elder Dragon
Primordus
who had moved to the core of the active volcano of
Titan's Throat
. The druids were unable to stop the army's advance and eventually discovered that the invaders were led by the god
Balthazar
whose presence was upsetting the natural balance.
When the
Pact Commander
and an expeditionary
Vigil
force arrived on the hollow volcano in their pursuit of Balthazar, Nettleblaze noticed
Scientist Viraddi
being snatched by vines and used a disembodied voice to guide the Commander to where the vines had taken the unlucky scientist. After the Commander had found Viraddi, Nettleblaze revealed himself as the source of the mysterious voice. The druid first told the Commander's people to leave, but decided to reveal that Balthazar had moved to the apex of the volcano when he learned that the Commander's goal was to make the rogue god leave as well.
Nettleblaze met with the Commander at the apex of Titan's Throat, only to inform them that they were too late: Balthazar had already entered the
Primal Kiln
, and a being of flesh like the Commander would be unable to follow the god into the flames. Wanting all the trespassers to leave and realizing that the Commander was still serious about stopping Balthazar, Nettleblaze told the Commander to free four ancient druid spirits who remained trapped in Zinn's vaults. He promised to let the wind carry his message to the ancients in advance so they would each bless the Commander with enough heat resistance for a safe trip into the volcano after they had been freed.
Nettleblaze eventually learned that the Commander had successfully freed the ancient druids and driven Balthazar from the volcano while the Vigil had dealt with the remaining mercenaries. Upon emerging from the volcano, the Commander was greeted by the disembodied voice of a druid thanking the Commander for their help and uttering solemn words of farewell.
Story involvement
Living World Season 3
Episode 5:
Flashpoint
Arrival and Survival
In Pursuit of a God
References

Trivia
NPCs in the game pronounce her name as
"nigh-am"
. In real life, the name Niamh is pronounced
"neev"
.
Niamh means 'bright' or 'radiant' in
Irish
. She might also be inspired by
Niamh
, one of the Queens of Tir na nÓg, from Irish mythology.
The House of Niamh
is named after her.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nicabar Steelweaver
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Nicabar_Steelweaver
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Nicabar Steelweaver
Race
Charr
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Forging Steel
(
Shiverpeak Mountains
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Dominion
Renegades
Steel Warband
“
Nicabar Steelweaver is the best combat medic around. He makes some nervous with his magic—and his renegade past—but you can't deny the effectiveness of the tonics he brews.
—
Ryland Steelcatcher
in
Forging Steel
Nicabar Steelweaver
is a member of
Ryland Steelcatcher
's
Steel Warband
in the
Blood Legion
. He is a former
Renegade
who has since sided with the
Dominion
along with the rest of the warband.
Biography
Early years
Nicabar Steelweaver was brought up on stories of
charr
heroes and hated
humans
deeply,
leading him to become a
Renegade
when the
Ebonhawke Treaty
was in the process of being signed between the two races. He was quickly disillusioned to the Renegade lifestyle when it turned out to be less heroic than he had hoped for.
Nicabar became trapped in a cave (likely the
Branded Mine
) after being chased into it by a large
Branded Devourer
, and a human lured the devourer away, preventing Nicabar from starving to death. Witnessing the human's selfless action made Nicabar reconsider his stance on seeing all humans as enemies.
Afterwards, Nicabar left the Renegades and began making tonics as he wanted to create something rather than just destroy everything others had worked for.
He entered
the Ooze Pit Trials
pledged to the
Blood Legion
where he was noticed and was mentioned to a
Blood Legion
tribune
to be considered for the
Steel Warband
. A slip of paper suggests that the person who had suggested him to the tribune may have been
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
himself.
. Nicabar later met
Cinder Steeltemper
who brought him into the
warband
.
The Icebrood Saga
Nicabar Steelweaver appeared during the
opening ceremony
of the
All-Legion Rally
of 1332
AE
, fighting a
Branded devourer
along with Cinder Steeltemper and
Ryland Steelcatcher
. After the Elder Dragon
Aurene
flew by and Branded a broodmother which had invaded the rally, Nicabar walked away from the creature.
Nicabar would later appear in a
vision
of the Steel Warband witnessed by the
Pact Commander
. In the vision Nicabar was asking warband recruits to help him create tonics which would help them in the scouting mission. Before the warband set out, he requested
alpine blossoms
,
cave mushrooms
,
grubs
and
handfuls of snow
to gather supplies for the creation of some
Health Tonics
. After crossing the bridge, Nicabar expressed skepticism of Bangar Ruinbringer's ideal of 'one charr', asking what the other charr in the warband felt about it. As the warband approached the gate to a
Stone Summit
ruin, Nicabar suggested passing through the ruins to avoid the approaching blizzard. Afterwards, he began making another batch of tonics, this time out of only alpine blossoms and grubs. While traversing the ruins and fighting against the Stone Summit who were zealously guarding their once mighty fortress, Nicabar requested supplies for another batch of tonics, this time wanting cave mushrooms and handfuls of snow. After encountering and defeating the
Ancient Forgeman
, Nicabar discussed some of his past with a recruit to the warband.
In
Drizzlewood Coast
in 1333
AE
, Nicabar was promoted to the rank of
centurion
and assigned to guard
Wolf's Crossing Bridge
with a
Dominion Bomber
. When his warbandmates
Vishen Steelshot
and
Ranoah Grindsteel
were defeated at
Leadfoot Village
and
Lighthouse Point
, he notified them of the impending loss of the bases and requested they fall back and regroup with him at
Wolf's Crossing
, personally extracting the latter's tank with his bomber. When the
United Legions
eventually attacked the bridge, he assisted his warbandmates from the air, and after they were killed, he directly engaged legion forces before being shot down and killed by charrzooka fire.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Drizzlewood Coast
Wolf's Crossing
(only during related events)
Forging Steel
Story involvement
The Icebrood Saga
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Coming Home
Visions of the Past: Steel and Fire

Trivia

voiced
by
Dave Fennoy
.

, and wears
Archon Shoulders
, a
Magus Coat
,
Archon Gloves
,
Archon Leggings
and
Masquerade Boots
.

Coming Home
story step with the
Visions of the Past: Steel and Fire
update.

Notes
Mechanically, the Thorn Wolf is a pet or minion attached to a
Nightmare Courtier
. Killing its master will result in its immediate death.
Once dead, the Thorn Wolf will not be raised or resummoned.
The Nightmare Courtier will often be accompanied by one or more Nightmare Hounds. These are allied creatures and not minions.

Nightmare Hound
Thorn Wolf

Dialogue
Our
Aspect
crystals can help you navigate this wasteland. Since the crash shattered them, our crystals can only grant you their movement skills for a short while before their energies ebb.
What are Aspect crystals?
Our crystals help us attune to the Aspects of Sun, Wind and Lightning to gain useful movement skills. Even shattered, the crystals can grant you these abilities for a short time.
How do I use the Aspect of Sun?
Run across any red Sun crystal and the Aspect of Sun will flow through you. You'll be able to dash like a beam of light.
I have another question about your Aspects.
Thank you. Take care.
How do I use the Aspect of Wind?
The Aspect of Wind will help lift you up. Run across any blue Wind crystal and you'll be able to jump far higher.
I have another question about your Aspects.
Thank you. Take care.
How do I use the Aspect of Lightning?
Just as lightning leaps from place to place, so can you. Collect any Purple lightning
[
sic
]
crystals you see and you'll gain the power to tether to locations at great distances.
I have another question about your Aspects.
Thank you. Take care.
Thank you. Take care.
Can you tell me more about your people?
Using the power of our Aspect crystals, my people traveled the skies before our flying ships crashed here. We live lives of simplicity and peace, dedicated to the legacy of our prophet
Glint
.
Can you tell me about Glint?
Our beloved
dragon champion
fell
defending
all who live from her former master, the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. Her crystalline remains gave us the
magic
to loft our ships and move like Aspects of the sky.
Can you remind me again what your Aspect crystals do?

Thank you. Take care.
