Orders storyline
At the end of Chapter 3,
player's character
is required to join one of the three
Orders
of Tyria:
Durmand Priory
: The scholars of the Priory believe the dragon can be defeated using ancient knowledge of past victories.
Order of Whispers
: The secret agents of the Whispers order believe the dragons cannot be defeated, but must be returned to sleep.
Vigil
: The Vigil believe a united army of all the races can defeat an elder dragon.
Chapters IV and VI are determined by the chosen
Order
, while Chapter V is influencied by
race
.
Chapter 4: Order Neophyte
Order Mentor
After joining one of the three Orders of Tyria, a
Mentor
will be assigned to instruct the
new recruit
.

Chapter 5: Helping Hands
Through Chapter 5,
player's character
is requested to help one of five available
minor races
and have them to join the battle against
the elder dragon
. Available options is restricted by player's
character's race
.

Orr - Part 3
At this stage, the
Pact Commander
must choose one out three plans to follow: Priory's to find a missing squad, Whispers' to go after Zhaitan's navy, or Vigil's to take tanks straight to the heart of Orr. The choice made will determine the next 3 missions and some of the available NPCs for the following missions.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sykox Steamshroud
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Sykox_Steamshroud
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Sea of Sorrows
.
Commander Sykox Steamshroud
was the
charr
confidante of
Commodore Marriner
. He was described as being more than a head taller than
Cobiah Marriner
, with fur the color of rust, touched here and there with scalloped, leopard-like spots of darker brown and black on his arms and legs. He had a paler muzzle, more of a rusty white, and the lighter shade spread across his chest and down the insides of his arms. Ram's horns spread out on either side of his head, and four slender ears flicked back and forth below them.
Steamshroud was raised in a
fahrar
near the border of
Kryta
, and spent a lot of time studying the people and architectural plans of
Lion's Arch
. The
imperator
of the Iron Legion was planning for his generation to assault and seize the city, after the charr were done conquering
Ascalon
. He was an advocate for sparing
Cobiah Marriner
's life when he was first found stranded at sea and brought aboard the
Havoc
after the
Great Tsunami
, and over the next decade the two became good friends. Engineer Steamshroud was present for the sacking of
Port Stalwart
, the raid on the
Salma's Grace
and subsequent sinking of the
Harbinger
. In 1237
AE
, when the
Pride
left Lion's Arch, Sykox elected not to join her, instead staying in the newly-rebuilt
Lion's Arch
to act as a second for Captain Marriner, the representative for the
Pride
on the
Captain's Council
, despite Sykox's disdain for council meetings.
By 1256
AE
, Steamshroud was ranked commander. Sometime in between, his leg had been injured during one of the
Dead Ship
attacks, and the charr's weight had partially redistributed itself to his belly. By now, his fur was beginning to grey. When the Dead Ships arrived during the
Great Krytan Blockade
, Commander Steamshroud organised the building of an ad-hoc fleet from the ships' hulls that made up the buildings of Lion's Arch, and came to the aid of ships like the
Balthazar's Trident
and the
Nomad II
. Sykox personally led the charge, at the helm of the
Pride
.

Dialogue
Welcome to the alliance's Fort Concordia.
If the player completed

.
How's everyone been holding up since the
Rankor Ruins
attack?
Actually, really well, I think. People seem...I dunno...more confident? Like their work's got more purpose. Even I feel...what's the word...energized! I like that word. It's an asura word—or something

Thanks. so, why did the alliance put this fort here?

You really seem on top of things. I can see why you were put in charge here.
Well, thank you for saying so. About time someone noticed!
I'd better let you get back to your important work.
You don't strike me as the intellectually curious sort of soldier.
No indeed! I was told I would be very useful out here, greeting anyone who wandered through...
And a fine job you're doing here! I'd better leave you to it!
Carry on.
Great. Keep up the good work!
Thanks. hope you don't mind if I just look around.
During the
Defend Concordia from enemy attack
event
Watch my form, here! I'm fairly skilled at this.
Go get 'em, Commander!
Trivia
He is voiced by
Yuri Lowenthal
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarb
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters

======================================================================

Captain Tarb
was a relative newcomer to the
Captain's Council
, as of 1237
AE
, and the first asura to join the Captain's Council. He was bigger and more muscular than most of his people, and carried a heavy war hammer across his back. His first mate was a petite
human
named
Gamina
. He led the ship
Priority Divide
.
By 1256
AE
, Tarb had gone half-senile, according to
Yomm
.

Dialogue
In Labyrinthine Cliffs
Ah,
Commander
! It's good to see you again. I hope you're enjoying the festival as much as I am!
What brings you here, Tasa?
Captain Kiel
asked me and my brother to bring some of our mounts for the
Zephyrites
, to remind them of home. I've also brought our newest addition: a

Dialogue
Before starting
What Was Before
Ah, youngling. Word of your actions in Nyedra have reached me. I would ask something of someone so...capable.
What do you need?
Tecla the Venerable:
Ah, youngling. Spare me the briefest moment, will you? Well, less brief relative to your ephemeral span of existence...
Tecla the Venerable:
We must not shape the future without considering the contours of the past. To that end, I ask for your assistance.
Tecla the Venerable:
There are some few of us who recall a time before
Eparch
's misdeeds altered the course of our culture.
Tecla the Venerable:
But our light dims, and our memories with us. So I ask: Will you seek out these vestiges of a bygone age?
Tecla the Venerable:
Some might seem trivial. Others inscrutable.
Tecla the Venerable:
But what is more valuable: A hoard of riches, or a trinket of the past that helps mold a brighter future?
Sorry, I can't right now.
After starting
What Was Before
Word has spread that the beacons have changed.
Nayos
is listening. Lady
Peitha
will have the audience she seeks.
These relics—where can I find them, again?
Throughout Inner Nayos, youngling. The
Heart
can attune to their ancient energy. Use it to reveal them.
I see. Thank you.
Are there more beacons out there?
(Same as during
Eventide's March
)
Likely. The beacons have been around for a very long time—longer than me, if you can imagine. Only recently has the Midnight King utilized them for oppression.
Maybe we'll deal with those, too, if they're ever a problem.
Glad I could help.
After completing
What Was Before
Tecla the Venerable:
Youngling.
Wayfinder
. Your contribution to the future of my people—ALL peoples—will far outlast our lifetimes.
Tecla the Venerable:
Let us hope these artifacts you've retrieved on this aged Kryptis's whim will have a fraction of your impact.
After having completed
What Was Before
Word has spread that the beacons have changed. Nayos is listening. Lady Peitha will have the audience she seeks.
Are there more beacons out there?

Eventide's March

Likely. The beacons have been around for a very long time—longer than me, if you can imagine. Only recently has the Midnight King utilized them for oppression.
Maybe we'll deal with those, too, if they're ever a problem.
Glad I could help.
Trivia
Tecla the Venerable is most likely named after
Maria Teresa Merlo
, a catholic nun to whom Pope John Paul II conferred the title of "Venerable" after confirming that she had lived a model life of heroic virtue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tenzin Moran
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tenzin_Moran
======================================================================

Colonel Tenzin Moran
(b. ~1218
AE
) was the well-decorated chief mate aboard the
Nomad II
. The son of Captain
Osh Moran
, Tenzin was considered a
Krytan
hero, with lots of medals for valour and bravery. He earned them fighting
centaurs
in
the Shiverpeaks
, and was according to his father one of their best sharpshooters. In 1256
AE
Commander
Sykox Steamshroud
speculated that Tenzin was in the Krytan marines.
Tenzin was tall and strong, broad of shoulder with caramel-coloured eyes and a light moustache. His hair was curly, a dark brown shot through with sun-bronzed red. He had a rifle strapped across his back, and at his side he wore a macelike scepter made of iron and brass. He, too, wore the Krytan uniform—but his baldric had far more small medals and trinkets than
Isaye Marriner
's. Tenzin might not have outranked her, but he had clearly been in the military far longer, and far more notably, than she.
Tenzin distinguished himself in the
Great Krytan Blockade
.
Trivia
Tenzin
(བསྟན་འཛིན) is a Tibetan name meaning "the holder of Buddha Dharma", most notable as the name of the 14th Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso.

======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
The Dragonvoid
Race
Void
Rank
Epic
Location
Harvest Temple
(
Dragon's End
)
“
We are all. We are nothing. We are the thread that binds every eon. We cannot be undone. What began as void shall return to it.
—
The Dragonvoid
The Dragonvoid
is a manifestation of the
Void
after it was extracted from the
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
. It is the amalgamation of all the Elder Dragons that have suffered from Torment, and it takes each of their appearances in turn while leading the Void horde. The Dragonvoid intends to end all life on
Tyria
and return everything to the state of primordial chaos in
the Mists
.
Biography
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing info.
Location
Cantha
Harvest Temple
(
raid encounter
)
Story involvement
End of Dragons story
Act 5
The Only One
Combat abilities
Open world:
The Battle for the Jade Sea
Void Storm
- Void energy strikes an area, inflicting
Burning
and
Vulnerability
.
Story mode and
raid encounter
:
Harvest Temple
Most Dragonvoid phases
(raid encounter only)
Targeted Expulsion
- Weaves dangerous elemental energies to heavily damage nearby foes.
Void Pool
- Target two enemies with void energy that drops below them after a delay. The resulting pools pulse heavy damage and torment.
Jormag phase
Breath of Jormag
- Cover the area with icy patterns that apply
Chilled
.
Frost Meteor
- Launch a meteor of ice, dealing very heavy damage and covering the area with slippery ice.
Grasp of Jormag
- Concentrate ice magic towards the furthest enemy, dealing medium damage and chilling them.
Primordus phase
Jaws of Destruction
- Devour a massive area and deal lethal, unavoidable damage. Leave behind a field of fire that pulses heavy damage and burning.
3x Lava Slam
- Bash the area with your chin and deal lethal damage.
Kralkatorrik phase
Crystal Barrage
- Launch a barrage of four crystals that deal heavy damage and knockback enemies.
Branded Beam
- Concentrate beams of void energy that deal massive damage and leave void pools that pulse heavy damage and torment.
Mordremoth phase
Poison Roar
- Cover the area with poison fields that pulse heavy damage and
Poison
.
3x Shock Wave
- Bash the area your head and unleash a shockwave that deals very heavy damage and knocks back enemies.
Zhaitan phase
Putrid Deluge
- Cover the area with poison clouds that pulse heavy damage and poison.
Slam
- Slam the area with your wing and deal massive damage.
Scream of Zhaitan
- Unleash a massive scream that deals heavy damage,
Fear
, and torment.
Soo-Won phase
Claw Slap
- Swipe orbs of void energy and deal heavy unavoidable damage, knocking down enemies, and applying vulnerability.
Torment of the Void
- Void energy bounces out, knocking enemies down dealing medium damage and applying torment.
Tail Slam
- Slam an orb of void energy with your tail and unleash a watery shockwave and smaller orbs of void. Each deals heavy damage and
knocks back
enemies. The slam and orbs apply vulnerability whereas the shockwave applies torment.
Magic Hail
- Excess void energy coalesce and target a nearby enemy.
Tsunami Slam
- Slam an orb of void energy with your claw and unleash a watery shockwave and smaller orbs of void. Each deals heavy damage and knocks back enemies. The slam and orbs apply vulnerability whereas the shockwave applies torment.
Magic Hail
- Excess void energy coalesce and target a nearby enemy.
Void Beam
- The Dragonvoid shoots beams of void energy that deal heavy damage and leave small, weak void pools.
Void Purge
- Excess void energy escape from the beams, pulsing low damage.
Void Energy
- Elder Dragon magic coalesce into an unstable orb, pulsing medium damage and applying
Slow
to enemies it passes through.

Trivia
The Dragonvoid is
voiced
by each Elder Dragon's original voice actor:
Debra Wilson
for the manifestation of
Jormag
,
Fred Tatasciore
for the manifestation of
Kralkatorrik
,
Nolan North
for the manifestation of
Mordremoth
, and
Kelly Hu
for the manifestation of
Soo-Won
. At times they all speak in unison to represent the amalgamation of the Void.
The Dragonvoid's voice lines are reminiscent of the arguments used by the speaking Elder Dragons from earlier story instances. The manifestation of Jormag offers a chance to go to sleep and a world free of strife,
Primordus
roars and growls, Kralkatorrik cries about anguish and blindness, Mordremoth commands everyone to obey and become part of it, and Soo-Won explains that the end of the world is inevitable.
Zhaitan
is the only Elder Dragon not given either voice lines or growls.
Similarly, the
music tracks
played during the final boss rush have previously been used to represent each Elder Dragon in their respective boss battles: "Jormag's World" for Jormag, "Primordus" for Primordus, "Dragonflight" for Kralkatorrik, "Mordremoth" for Mordremoth, "Here Be Dragons" for Zhaitan, and the new track "The Cycle Ends" for the final Soo-Won manifestation.
It is unclear which time period of the
Elder Dragon
lives the appearances come from. For example, the Dragonvoid manifestation of
Zhaitan
has its right arm and tails missing, basically mimicking the Elder Dragon's body shape from its last moments. On the other hand,
Kralkatorrik
's head has the appearance from his time in the
Crystal Desert
, way before the destruction of the right side of his face and his demise.

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
.
The Judge
Race
Avatar
Level
80
Location
The Departing
(
Judgment
,
Skimshallow Cove
)
“
You will reach your rightful place in time. First, you must recover your name to know who you were and how you lived. Then you must learn your purpose, to understand the choices you made and why you lived as you did. Once you know your name and your purpose, only then can I determine your final destination.
—
The Judge
The Judge
is a loyal servant of
Grenth
tasked with overseeing the
Judgment
, where he directs
lost spirits
to recovering their name and purpose, before sending them to their final destination.
When he met the
Pact Commander
, The Judge gave advice on how they could resurrect from the dead, despite the loss of such
magic
.
Story involvement
Path of Fire story
Act III
The Departing
Notes
He is located directly east of the anvil, in the circle of seven pillars.
Events during
Hall of Chains
, and discussion of there being no more leaders in the
Underworld
besides
Desmina
, implies that The Judge dies after the events of The Departing.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Maw
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/The_Maw
======================================================================

The Maw's remains southeast of
Claw Island
.
The Maw
was a fifty-foot long
leviathan
swimming in the waters of
Malchor's Leap
prior to the
Rising of Orr
. It was a purplish color with sharp teeth, each the size of a man. Long thought to be a sailor's legend, it began fervently attacking ships in 1219
AE
. This led to
King Baede
sending out the
Indomitable
to investigate the cause of the ships' wrecks, resulting in a battle that was interrupted by
Zhaitan
's
awakening
.
It returned later in 1256
AE
as a fierce
Risen
during the
Orrian
armada's assault on the
Great Krytan Blockade
. During the battle, it swarmed the waters consuming any being—living or Risen—that fell into the waters within its reach. It was the final Risen to be defeated, when
Macha
led the
Pride
straight into The Maw's gaping jaws and caused the ship's engine to explode.
See also
Maw and Pride

PAGE: Tiachren
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tiachren
======================================================================

Tiachren
Race
Sylvari
Rank
Veteran
Locations
Awakening
(
Caledon Forest
,
Dreamer's Terrace
)
A Sly Trick
(
Nightshade Garden
)
Deeply Tangled Roots
(
Sandycove Beach
)
Beneath a Cold Moon
(

)
The Heart of Nightmare
(
Ogham Wilds
)
Organization
Wardens
Image(s)
Tiachren during The Heart of Nightmare.
“
Revenge is a bitter fraught, but I will drink it gladly if I might see my beloved Ysvelta again.
— Tiachren in
Awakening
Tiachren
is a
sylvari
and is part of the quest for sylvari players who chose the Shield of the Moon
personal storyline
.
Biography
Tiachren is the beloved of
Ysvelta
, who gave him the
Shield of the Moon
as a wedding gift and show of their love. The two were attacked by the
Nightmare Court
, with them poisoning Tiachren and leaving him for dead while capturing Ysvelta. Upon his recovery, he asks for the player's assistance to save Ysvelta from the Nightmare Court, though Caithe warns it may already be too late.
Tiachren and the player are able to track down Ysvelta, but as Caithe warned, she has already been corrupted by the Nightmare Court and going to lead an attack on the
Village of Astorea
, leaving the player to decide either save the village or Ysvelta. If the player chooses to save the village, Ysvelta will turn Tiachren into a Courtier, whose shield
becomes twisted to reflect his new state
, and both assault the village together. Choosing to save Ysvelta will also result in Ysvelta's death, and a brokenhearted Tiachren, who is then comforted by The
Pale Tree
saying Ysvelta's spirit will live on in the Dream, and that his story will be told, as a reminder love is good even if others try to make it not so.
Story involvement
Personal story

PAGE: Tippett
CATEGORIES: Edge of Destiny characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tippett
======================================================================

Tippett
was a
human
mercenary working under
Logan Thackeray
in
Ascalon
to protect human merchant caravans that supplied
Ebonhawke
. In 1319
AE
, he was in the group that fought with
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
's forces, taken over by
Rytlock Brimstone
, both of whom were then ambushed by
ogres
. He died in the conflict, and his body was burned in a makeshift pyre by Logan afterwards.

What does an Ash tribune do in Iron's citadel?
I'm glad to hear that. Thank you, Tribune.
I'll leave you to your work, Tribune. Sorry.
To Ash charr (before joining an order)
You have your orders,
Legionnaire
. Report back to me when you've carried them out.
Will do, boss.
To Ash charr (after joining an
order
)
Good to see you again. Things have been heating up between us and the
Flame Legion
since I last saw you.
I wanted to say how sorry I am about
Fengar
.
Why? It was my call to send him into harm's way. I won't make that kind of mistake again. And until I find a replacement
aide-de-camp
,
Sicaea
is filling in just fine.
Glad to hear it.
Any lingering effects from
your time
with
Trybulus
?
The pain is fading, and until it does it serves as a reminder of my foul up. But don't worry: I'll pay the Flame Legion back tenfold for every twinge.
Glad to hear it.
How is the campaign against Flame going?
Well enough. Losing Trybulus set them back, and we've also rooted most of the moles they had among the Citadel brass. The war goes on, but we've got it well in hand.
Glad to hear it.
Thank you for letting me pursue the matter with my father.
The
High Legions
need soldiers who are focused on the task at hand. It was worth giving you some time to wrap things up with your father. It eliminated a distraction.
Thank you, Tribune.
Don't stop killing until they're all dead.
Notes
Torga wears
Prowler Shoulders
, a
Priory's Historical Jerkin
,
Drover Gloves
,
Seeker Pants
, and
Vigil's Honor Boots
.
Every story instance of her refers to her by her full title as "Tribune Torga Desertgrave", but she is without the title in the Ash Tribune Quarters instance.
She has
heterochromia
, as her right eye is red, and her left is light blue. Other story characters with heterochromia include
Ankka
and
Halvora Snapdagger
.
Torga appears to lack at least one fang.
Trivia
Torga is
voiced
by
Jocelyn Blue
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tormented Judge
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tormented_Judge
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Tormented Judge
Race
Ghost
Profession
Necromancer
Level
80
Rank
Champion
Location
Salvation's Cost
(
Divinity's Reach
,
Lion's Arch
,
Queensdale
,
Brisban Wildlands
,
Bava Nisos
,
Foundry of Failed Creations
)
Organization
White Mantle
Image(s)
Click to enlarge.
Tormented Judge
was a member of the
White Mantle
. During the White Mantle's exploration of
Janthir
,
Confessor
Caudecus Beetlestone
left Judge,
Tatyanna
, and
Ulrich
to seek a way into the warded
mursaat
city of
Bava Nisos
to use its secrets to aid the White Mantle's war against the
Krytan
regime.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
The White Mantle trio are eventually revealed to have been manipulated by the
titan
Saevus Saxum
, who had merged with Bava Nisos, to open the
Mists Gate
within the city so the titan can feed himself with the released magic. Using the Mists Gate to enter the
Foundry of Failed Creations
, the trio's souls merge with
demonic
entities as they create the titans
Greer
,
Decima
, and
Ura
who reflect the trio's twisted personalities as the titans enter Tyria to spread corruption and destruction. The tormented White Mantle are ultimately
exorcised
from the Mists by the
Pact Commander
using the
Heart of the Obscure
, ending the titan threat.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information from early years and Season 3 events.
Janthir Wilds
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
After the mursaat
wizard
Mabon
's
death
removed the arcane ward he had placed around Bava Nisos to protect it in 1336
AE
, the White Mantle trio were finally able to see past the late wizard's now crumbling illusion to make their way to the northern isle containing the fabled city. After entering
Mistburned Barrens
to explore the outskirts of the great mountain housing the city, the trio were manipulated by the
titan
Saevus Saxum
, who had merged with Bava Nisos over the centuries he had been trapped there, to open the city's
Mists Gate
leading to the
Foundry of Failed Creations
in the
Realm of Torment
.
Upon entering the foundry, the trio eventually became trapped in the realm. Their souls merged with
demonic
entities who warped their minds, allowing the now maddened trio to create the titans
Greer
,
Decima
, and
Ura
who reflected twisted versions of their personalities. The tormented White Mantle would remain in the foundry to keep it active while the titans entered Tyria to spread corruption across Janthir and create their respective titanspawn to bolster their ranks.
In 1338
AE
, a strike team including the
Pact Commander
,
Countess Anise
,
Isgarren
,
Livia
,
Poised Arrow
, and
Waiting Sorrow
entered the foundry
after receiving information from Mabon's ghost on how to end the titan threat. The team soon located the tormented White Mantle trio who sensed the
Scepter of Orr
that Livia was carrying and demanded she give it to them. When the team refused, the White Mantle attacked them one by one, proving to be formidable foes in combat. After each tormented soul had been weakened enough to make them vulnerable, the Commander used the
Heart of the Obscure
in tandem with a touchstone related to the respective spirit's personal keepsake to channel the touchstones into their souls and then exorcise each soul from the Mists. The trio's banishment and Mabon subsequently sealing the Mists Gate by remaining on the other side with the Scepter of Orr ended the titan threat by severing access to the foundry.
Story involvement
Janthir Wilds story
Absolution
Salvation's Cost
Combat abilities
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.

======================================================================
PAGE: Torrin
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Torrin
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Torrin
Race
Norn
Level
80
Location
What's Left Behind
(
Bjora Marches
)
Organization
Destiny's Edge (norn)
Sons of Svanir
Torrin
is one of
Braham Eirsson
's former guildmates from the
norn
guild
called
Destiny's Edge
. After Braham's failure keep to his vow of defeating
Jormag
, Torrin defected to the
Sons of Svanir
cause alongside another guildmate,
Olar
.
Biography
Living World Season 3
In 1329
AE
,
Braham Eirsson
fulfilled the
norn
prophecy
by
cracking
the
Fang of the Serpent
with his mother
Eir Stegalkin
's
longbow
which had been enchanted with the fire
magic
of an ancient
jotun scroll
. Following his success, he made plans to rally the norn who celebrated him and take the fight to the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
. During this time, he formed his own
guild
and named it
Destiny's Edge
to honor Eir's memory. Braham's
charr
friend
Rox
followed him to
Hoelbrak
and managed to convince him to instead take a scouting party north to assess the situation first before mobilizing a larger force.
Braham and his guild—consisting of himself, Rox, the dire wolf
Garm
and the norn
Alva
,
Olar
and Torrin—headed north and fought
Icebrood
and
Sons of Svanir
along the way. The guild eventually tracked down and surrounded Jormag, only to witness the Elder Dragon suddenly roaring in anguish and returning to the ice much to their confusion. Braham suspected that the
Pact Commander
had
something to do with this turn of events
and wrote a letter to their
asuran
friend
Taimi
inquiring about the matter. The norn put Braham under pressure to kill Jormag, and he felt he had to live up to Eir's legend and could no longer back down.
Realizing they were unable to reach Jormag in its current state, the guild returned south. Angry and ashamed of his failure, Braham left the disappointed Alva, Olar and Torrin at
Jora's Keep
in
Bjora Marches
while heading back to Hoelbrak with Rox and Garm to think of a way to reach Jormag and finish the mission.
News of Braham's failure spread, and eventually the norn in the region, including
Vigil
members, began calling him Braham Vowbreaker for abandoning his guild's mission and being unable to deliver on his vow to defeat Jormag.
The Icebrood Saga
After
General Almorra Soulkeeper
visited Jora's Keep to inform the soldiers there about the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
's
demise
in 1332
AE
, the Sons of Svanir became more active and conducted raids throughout Bjora Marches under the orders of the
Fraenir of Jormag
. Those staying in the region began hearing strange whispers in their head that both reminded them of their failings and offered them salvation and peace through accepting the voice's offers.
Olar, Torrin and several other male norn in the keep were influenced by the voices and eventually turned against Alva and the rest of the Vigil by opening the gates to let the Sons of Svanir in and killing Vigil soldiers in their sleep. Despite suffering many losses when the soldiers were alerted to their treachery, the Sons of Svanir and the traitors successfully killed Alva and most of the Vigil at the keep, taking Almorra and
Warmaster
Jhavi Jorasdottir
captive.
Olar and Torrin were instructed to take the captive pair deeper into the wilderness so Jormag could begin influencing their minds and turn them against the Vigil. When Torrin grew annoyed at Almorra's taunts and intended to kill her, Olar had to remind him of their orders to stay his hand. While resting with the prisoners, Olar and Torrin noticed Almorra causing a distraction which allowed Jhavi to flee. Although the general managed to flee as well, Olar and Torrin sent several Sons of Svanir after her to bring her back.
While several converted norn were busy
chasing after
Almorra in
Darkrime Delves
, Olar and Torrin caught up to Jhavi and tied her up. Olar complimented Jhavi on using her wits like
Raven
while telling her that Almorra had killed her last dragon, suggesting that the defiant general had been permanently dealt with. Despite Jhavi's inquiries, the pair refused to reveal more about the events following Almorra's disappearance.
Olar and Torrin took Jhavi to the
Boneskinner Den
in the
Aberrant Forest
and guarded her with their fellow Sons of Svanir while waiting for the whispers to break the warmaster's resolve. They witnessed Braham and the Pact Commander
breaking into the cave
and welcomed them with disparaging remarks. When Braham tried to reason with Olar, the influenced guildmate countered him by stating that killing Elder Dragons was wrong and that the Sons of Svanir had given him and Torrin a new brotherhood and shown them that Jormag was not the enemy. Olar commanded the hiding Sons of Svanir to ambush the rescuers, only to witness the pair defeating the Sons and Torrin, before he was defeated as well.
Story involvement
The Icebrood Saga
Episode 1:
Whisper in the Dark
What's Left Behind
Combat abilities

Chills
Effects

Treasonous Minister
Race
Human
Rank
Veteran
Location
Confessor's End
(
Caudecus's Manor
)
Organization
Ministry
White Mantle
Image(s)
Male minister.
The
Treasonous Ministers
are former members of the
Ministry
who have openly sided with
Minister Caudecus
even after he has been found a traitor. They are now part of his army and can be found in his mansion. There are two ministers in the
Confessor's End
instance. The first is a
dagger
-wielding female in a room full of
White Mantle
. She will fight to the death if confronted. The second is an unarmed male in a room full of
villagers
. If he is not immediately attacked, he will attempt to flee the mansion with the villagers.

PAGE: Trembling Song
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Trembling_Song
======================================================================

Trembling Song
Race
Kodan
Location
Hard Boiled
(
Divinity's Reach
,
Lion's Arch
,
Bloodtide Coast
,
Gendarran Fields
,
Caledon Forest
,
Snowden Drifts
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
Trembling Song
is the
kodan
cultural representative selected to attend the
burning of the effigies
during the first annual
Dragon Bash
and place memorials of kin killed by the dragons on the pyre. Trembling Song runs off when one of the pyres is sabotaged to explode. She is later found at
Lost Child's Sorrow
in
Snowden Drifts
. She believes her offering was tampered with while she slept by the fire of travelers she met on the way to Lion's Arch. She is persuaded to return to Lion's Arch, where she was found innocent of the sabotage.

Shiverpeak Mountains

No More Secrets
Dialogue
Speaking to Trembling Song after convincing him to return to Lion's Arch
I should have recognized their voices as the ones I heard by the fire that night. The killers are unmasked and my walk home will be lighter knowing they'll be punished.
Take care.

PAGE: Tribune Fierhan Sparwind
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tribune_Fierhan_Sparwind
======================================================================

Tribune Fierhan Sparwind
Race
Charr
Location
Imperator's Core
(
Black Citadel
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Image(s)
Interactive map
Tribune Fierhan Sparwind
is a
tribune
of the
Blood Legion
who took part in the
defense quorum
convened by the three allied
High Legions
to fight against the
Molten Alliance
.
Biography
Early years
After becoming the
tribune
of the
Blood Legion
, Fierhan Sparwind served his legion dutifully in the
Black Citadel
under the jurisdiction of
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
of the
Iron Legion
. He was relegated to handling paperwork and taking in reports from his
centurions
, a task he sometimes lamented as he would have wanted to spend more time on the field.
Personal story
In 1325
AE
, Fierhan witnessed fellow tribune
Rytlock Brimstone
instructing
a recently promoted
Legionnaire
to track down
Rage Steeltongue
. Rage had once been a member of the Legionnaire's warband but had since defected to the
Flame Legion
out of spite and needed to be stopped before his actions and information could threaten the Black Citadel.
Living World Season 1
Following the
Molten Alliance
's incursions into
Ascalon
and their subsequent capture of
charr
as slave labor for their
molten facilities
in 1326
AE
, Imperator Smodur
called
for every tribune in the Citadel, including Fierhan, to attend a
defense quorum
to discuss the
High Legions
' response to these acts of aggression. Fierhan and Rytlock collaborated with managing the
Blood Legion
's counterattack and learned about the actions of the
gladium
Rox
, the
norn
Braham
and the
Pact Commander
which had led to the dismantling of the Molten Alliance's operations in the region.
Location
Ascalon
Black Citadel
Imperator's Core
Story involvement
Personal story

Rage Suppression
Living World Season 1
Episode 1:
Flame and Frost
New Friends, New Enemies
Living World Season 1 (historical)
Episode 3:
Flame and Frost: The Razing
Rox: Critical Mission

PAGE: Trybulus Griefblade
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Trybulus_Griefblade
======================================================================

Trybulus Griefblade
Race
Charr
Level
10
Locations
The Flame Advances
(
Ruins of Rin
,
Junker's Scrapyard
)
Quick and Quiet
(
Victor's Presidium
)
Organization
Ash Legion
Flame Legion
“
Not bad, little mice! You just earned my full attention, and it's going to hurt!
— Trybulus Griefblade
Trybulus Griefblade
is an
Ash Legion
traitor who joined the
Flame Legion
. He killed his
warband
in cold blood and set their deaths up to look like an accident before joining the Flame Legion. The Ash Legion refer to him as a sociopath but a genius and one of the Flame Legion's best spymasters. After climbing the ranks, he led a series of spies to infiltrate the
Black Citadel
via murdering workers and having his men take their place. With
Flame Centurion Kagaar
and
Flame Saboteur Ducco
leading the spies, Trybulus intended to set the
Blood Legion
and the
Iron Legion
against each other via framing. When his plans were stopped by the Ash Legion, he kidnapped and tortured
Fengar Cruelmane
and
Tribune Torga Desertgrave
, killing the former when he captured Torga. After Torga's rescue he returned to his main hideout at
Igni Castrum
where he was tracked down and killed by a strike team led by the
Legionnaire
and
Rytlock Brimstone
.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Getting the Band Back Together
The Flame Advances
Quick and Quiet

-

increased by 20%; stacks duration.

Might
- Increased outgoing
damage
; stacks intensity.

(At and below 25% health)

- Basic melee attack chain.

- Throws a dagger at the target.

-
Leap
and strike your foe. The less health your target has, the more damage you cause.

- Stabs the target while evading attacks.

- Bashes the target with his sword hilt.
Notes
Given that a
centurion
followed Trybulus' orders, Trybulus would have to be either primus centurion or a
tribune
. However, his rank is never mentioned.

PAGE: Twitchy Jake

Twitchy Jake
Race
Human
Rank
Veteran
Locations
On the Mend
(

,
Salma District
)
Desperate Medicine
(
Shaemoor Fields
,
Salma District
,
Bandithaunt Caverns
)
Organization
Bandits
Twitchy Jake
is a
bandit
belonging to
Big Nose Ted
's gang in the
Commoner's
personal story
. He is one of Ted's lieutenants, but he's unpopular with his fellow bandits for his unfair distribution of the gang's loot.
His first appearance is in
Innkeeper Andrew
and
Petra
's inn in the
Salma District
, causing trouble with the other gang members there. The hero of
Shaemoor
quickly deals with them, and
Logan Thackeray
arrives with the
Seraph
to arrest them. However, the
Ministry Guard
, led by
Commander Serentine
claim credit and interfere, arrest Jake and the others instead. At the
Bandithaunt Caverns
, after having been supposedly jailed by the Ministry Guard, Twitchy Jake is seen overseeing things, having just stolen some medical supplies belonging to the Seraph. He is defeated by Petra and the hero of Shaemoor and pretends to yield, only to ambush them while they're getting the medicine. He does not survive the conflict. His appearance there, despite having been arrested, is the first clue that Serentine is corrupt.

during the human
personal story
if they chose the

Notes
When champions were removed from low level areas, this also affected the champion in the first chapter of the Personal Story. However, some such as Two-Blade Pete that show up in steps before being a boss did not have their rank changed in the earlier steps. This effectively made them a champion and a veteran depending on location.

Uzolan
was the premier inventor, artisan, and toymaker of
Divinity's Reach
. His contraptions thrived under the patronage of
Minister Caudecus
, and they can be seen on display throughout the city.
In 1325
AE
, the
Order of Whispers
received word that he'd been using technology that, according to their previous information, should be above his competence level. It was discovered that Uzolan was working with enemies of
Kryta
, and planned on disrupting a party to celebrate the
human
-
charr
détente with his
new invention
, and kidnapping
Queen Jennah
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Valeria
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Valeria
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Valeria
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Zu Heltzer Historical Site
(
The Echovald Wilds
)
Mori Village
(
The Echovald Wilds
)
Arborstone
(
Cantha
)
Organization
House zu Heltzer
Kurzicks
Image(s)
Valeria in Mori Village.
Interactive map
Valeria
is a
Canthan
scholar looking into the fall of
House zu Heltzer
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Tracing the Tapestry
.
At the end of the
Tracing the Tapestry
collection, it is revealed that she is herself a Zu Heltzer, and she then adopts their name, showing as
Valeria zu Heltzer
.
Biography
Early years
Valeria was born in
Cantha
, unaware of her family's origins. Her father gave her a piece of old fabric which she held on to since although he did not elaborate on its history. Valeria got interested in archeology related to the fallen
Kurzicks
, especially the once famous
House zu Heltzer
, and spent her time studying ruins and relics in
the Echovald Wilds
. According to her own estimates, she spent an "embarrassing amount of time" sulking around the
Arborstone
library's occult section studying anything related to the Kurzicks, including their sealing rituals, in her youth.
End of Dragons
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
The Fate of House zu Heltzer
In 1845
CC
(1335
AE
), Cantha reopened its borders under
Empress Ihn
's decree, which led to many foreign visitors arriving on the
continent
and exploring various locations, including the Kurzick ruins. While digging near one of these ruins, Valeria ran into the
Pact Commander
and recognized them as an associate of
Detective Rama
. Valeria had overheard Rama talking about how he might be a surviving member of House zu Heltzer, and his statements convinced her that her suspicions of the house not having completely fallen in the
Ministry of Purity
's purges had merit. As
Speakers
were patrolling many of the ruins and attacking any trespassers, Valeria asked the Commander to search the ruins for any pristine zu Heltzer relics for further study.
What Lies Within
A few months later, Rama and the Commander
visited
Valeria in Arborstone. Valeria playfully scolded Rama for not having visited her more often after they had been revealed to be relatives, and Rama apologized for his tardiness. He also revealed his reason for visiting Valeria now, however: he needed information about Kurzick sealing rituals and believed that Valeria could be of use.
Locations
Cantha
The Echovald Wilds
Zu Heltzer Historical Site
House zu Heltzer
(First location)
Mori Village
(Second location on south end of ground level with
Master Weaver Kubo
and
Trader Chung-Cha
)
Arborstone
(Ground floor mid-room, north side; Final location)
Story involvement
End of Dragons story
What Lies Within
Emotional Release
(as Valeria)

Dialogue
Shipwreck Strand
Wayfinder! Great seeing you take action, as always. Anything I can do to help you out?
Anything I should know about rifts on Castora?
Still figuring that out, but they're not like the ones we're used to. Local wildlife emerge from them; couple of scouts said that an Inquest ambush fell from another. The Heart of the Obscure can still seal them, nonetheless.
Odd. I'll keep an eye out.

A breath of fresh air, when you ignore the circumstances that brought us here. I'm eager to explore; it already reminds me so much of home. So much so that I'm feeling kind of homesick.
I hope that you get to visit home again soon.
Nothing at the moment. Thanks.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vaolt Sharpstrike
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Vaolt_Sharpstrike
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Vaolt Sharpstrike
Race
Charr
Profession
Warrior
Rank
Veteran
Elite
Location
Rusty Meadows
(
Grothmar Valley
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Renegades
Vaolt Sharpstrike
is a
charr
warrior
from the
Blood Legion
. He views charr as the supreme race on Tyria and sneers at anyone who has friendly interactions with other races.
Biography
Vaolt Sharpstrike embraced the ideals of
Blood Legion
while growing up, viewing charr of his legion as supreme warriors. After meeting with like-minded charr, he began viewing any charr who had friendly relations with other races as lesser than the "true" charr that he represented. These ideas would eventually lead him to mingle with the extremist
Renegades
.
During the
All-Legion Rally
in
Grothmar Valley
in 1332
AE
, Vaolt's little sister and other Blood Legion youths spotted the aged
Erasmus Fangstorm
assisting the visiting
asura
Gorrik
in defending the cattle of
Fangstorm's Farmhouse
from invasive
devourers
. They viewed Erasmus's collaboration with an asura distasteful and decided to teach him a lesson by trespassing on the old farmer's lawn and insulting him. After passing visitors helped Erasmus shoo off the young troublemakers, Vaolt's sister threatened to find his brother, who had just been promoted, and that Vaolt would make Erasmus and the outsiders sorry.
The sister found Vaolt, who had gotten drunk while celebrating his promotion into
legionnaire
, and told him what had happened. The enraged Vaolt rushed to confront Erasmus and shouted that the farmer believed himself to be better than his fellow charr. When Erasmus tried to explain that Vaolt's sister had trespassed on his property, the angry legionnaire used a counterargument that all of Grothmar Valley was charr territory and that he did not view Erasmus as a real charr who deserved a piece of land.
The two charr clashed, and Vaolt mocked Erasmus for being weak after being away from the battlefield for so long. However, the visitors helped Erasmus once again by defeating the drunk legionnaire. Humiliated and angry, the retreating Vaolt stated that Erasmus had been lucky to have friends around to save him but those friends would not be around all the time to save him from further assaults.
Some time later during the celebration, Vaolt and a number of Renegades led by
Veteran Ulixes Swiftreaver
met discreetly with
Centurion
Ryland Steelcatcher
who informed them that he would be leading an expedition north with
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
. Before leaving, Ryland ordered the group to kidnap the
Pact Commander
's friend Gorrik and search his datapad for intel on the Commander's
Elder Dragon
ally
Aurene
and if Aurene had any strengths or weaknesses. The centurion also reminded them to stay put and not act against the other imperators attending the rally until Bangar had evened the odds against them.
The group found Gorrik and "encouraged" him to accompany them. After escorting the asura to the
war room
in the
Blood Keep
, they confiscated his datapad and tried to search it for the information they were after, only to discover hundreds of drawings of spiders instead much to their confusion. While studying the datapad, they realized that Gorrik was using a remote communicator to speak with someone, so they quickly smashed it to prevent further discussions.
Before the Renegades could decide what to do with Gorrik, they saw Ryland arriving at the war room. Although the Renegades were confused at first why Ryland was asking them about the expedition which he had mentioned earlier, they soon came to the conclusion that Ryland was suffering from a hangover after spending the day drinking with the Commander's
norn
ally
Braham Eirsson
.
Unbeknownst to the Renegades, however, the centurion who had come for Gorrik was not actually Ryland but the Pact Commander under a
mesmer
illusion. The Commander had the option to either play along and lie about taking Gorrik with him and ostensibly finish off the asura out of sight, or use violence against Gorrik's captors to free him.
Regardless of how the disguised Commander dealt with the Renegades, Vaolt and his companions would not lay a hand on Gorrik again while being oblivious that they had been tricked.
Location
Ascalon
Grothmar Valley
Rusty Meadows
Soul's Hearth
(only during related events)
Story involvement
The Icebrood Saga
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Deeper and Deeper

======================================================================
PAGE: Varkk
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Varkk
======================================================================

This article is about a
krewe
chief.  For a member of the
Arcane Council
, see
Councilor Vark
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Varkk
Race
Asura
Locations
Snaff Would Be Proud
(
The Hinterlabs
,
Jeztar Falls
)
In Snaff's Footsteps
(
The Hinterlabs
)
The Snaff Prize
(
The Hinterlabs
)
Political Homicide
(
Council Level
)
Organization
College of Synergetics
“
I taught you everything you know, but I didn't teach you everything I know.
—
Varkk
Varkk
is a member of the
College of Synergetics
and chief of a
krewe
which includes
Pol
and the
Snaff Savant
. He desires a position in the
Arcane Council
and is willing to go to any lengths to get it.
Biography
Personal story
Varkk led the
krewe
including
Pol
and the soon-to-be
Snaff Savant
in attempting to obtain the
Snaff Prize
. In 1325
AE
, the eventual Savant helped to battle an
Inquest
prototype
for a
golem
with
Zojja
, a former
Destiny's Edge
member and the founder of the Snaff Prize. This irked Varkk, who sent a letter to the Savant, noting that while altruism was all well and good, they still had a prize to win. He also demonstrated insecurity about his height.
The Savant arrived at the grounds the Snaff Prize would be awarded at and Varkk noted that the
College of Synergetics
was last in the competition, behind the other
colleges
and the Inquest. The group then, after the Inquest's presentation led by
Teyo
, presented their Interspacial Translocator, a teleporter that could transport creatures without a receiving gate, which won the competition. However, Teyo and the Inquest krewe claimed that they had designed the Interspacial Translocator.
After this, Varkk declared that the
Arcane Council
's decision was foolish and that he would not be recorded as the only Snaff Prize winner to receive an asterisk next to his name. Zojja told him to calm down and it was noted by other members of the krewe that the speed of the council's decision was suspicious. Zojja was then kidnapped and subsequently a
prototype incinerator golem
attacked the krewe and was defeated. Zojja then explained that Varkk was working with the Inquest and could not be trusted.
Zojja gave the Savant two choices: get revenge on Varkk or rescue Pol, who had been kidnapped by Inquest. If the Savant chose to attack Varkk, they traveled to the
Council's meeting chambers
where Varkk was being groomed by
Councillor Haia
for an unknown reason. Varkk used the teleportation technology the krewe had developed to teleport Inquest around the council chambers and was eventually killed after a fierce battle. If the Savant chose to rescue Pol, the Savant and Zojja traveled to an Inquest lab in
Metrica Province
and rescued Pol, who was then notified that Varkk was a traitor. However, when the Savant chose this path, Varkk most likely survived and became councillor.
Legacy
Living World Season 4
The Inquest began testing the technology developed by Varkk and his krewe in certain locations such as
Fahranur
and
Rata Primus
, where they would become a crucial part of
Palawa Joko
's invasion of
Central Tyria
.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Experiments Gone Wrong
Statics path
Snaff Would Be Proud
Dynamics path
In Snaff's Footsteps
Synergetics path
The Snaff Prize
Political Homicide

======================================================================
PAGE: Ventari
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ventari
======================================================================

This article is about the historical figure.  For the
Revenant
legend
, see
Legendary Centaur Stance
.
Ventari
Race
Legend
,
Vision
Location
Fighting the Nightmare
(
The Sylvari Dream
)
Organization
Harathi
Ventari
was a
centaur
who planted the
Pale Tree
with
Ronan
in what would become
the Grove
. He etched the tenets of his philosophy upon
Ventari's Tablet
before passing away. The Pale Tree used the tablet to teach morals to
sylvari
, ensuring that Ventari's legacy lives on. A
vision
of him can be seen teaching dreamers during the
Sylvari
story mission
Fighting the Nightmare
.
Biography
Ventari was an elder of the
Harathi
centaurs
who once lived in the
Maguuma Jungle
. In his youth he was a proud warrior who fought against
humans
, however over time he grew to abhor the violence that engulfed the two species. He eventually set up
a camp
beneath the largest
Stonewood tree
in the arid tops of the Maguuma Jungle.
However, in 1072
AE
, violence once more engulfed Ventari's home in the form of the
Krytan civil war
. To escape the conflict, Ventari fled south, to the
Tarnished Coast
, where he befriended a human named
Ronan
, with whom he planted the
Pale Tree
and founded
a sanctuary
of peace for all races seeking it that would eventually become
the Grove
. Along with Ronan, and after the human's death, he raised and nurtured the tree.
Ventari died in 1180
AE
, having written down his life experiences on an artifact that would later be known as
Ventari's Tablet
and placed it at the foot of the Pale Tree. In 1302
AE
, the
Firstborn
of the
sylvari
sprouted forth from the tree, and most of the race now holds Ventari's views in high regard.
Ventari's teachings are also highly similar to those of the
Maguuma
centaurs, an offshoot of the Harathi that believe in peace. Despite this resemblance, and their current leader
Ehrgen Cragstrider
sharing names with one of Ventari's followers,
Ehrgen Windmane
, the Maguuma centaurs claim ignorance about Ventari.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 1: Following the Dream
Fighting the Nightmare
Quotes
Forging serenity within the heart is more difficult than any physical battle.
I did not know that such a small seed could begin such a very large journey...
Life can be painful, but do not fear the trials you will face. Hard ground makes stronger roots.
Live well and fully, and waste nothing. Each day is a gift.
You cannot expect the world to give you peace. You must find it... within yourself.
Never leave a wrong to ripen into evil or sorrow.
Trivia
Ventari uses an ethereal
Modniir Hunter
model. This looks nothing like his
Guild Wars
model. However,
Toy Ventari
has a much closer look to the original one.
Some of his quotes seem to be inspired by those of
Buddha
.
Chiron
was a centaur in Greek mythology who, unlike the rest of his crude and bestial race, was a great scholar, healer, prophet, and mentor to great heroes.
See also
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Ventari
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Verahd
CATEGORIES: Sea of Sorrows characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Verahd
======================================================================

“
Magic
isn't always predictable, Captain. We must accept the thunder along with the rain.
— Verahd
Verahd
was an
elementalist
crewmate of
Isaye
. He was thin to the point of fragility, tall, with long fingers that resembled a bird's talons, and wore a pair of wire-rimmed glasses perched on his nose. Red-streaked brown hair hung in thin strands around his head and shoulders, half-covering the man's narrow face. He did not wear a bright robe, but instead wrapped himself from head to toe in strange, bandage-like strips of black and green fabric, with each strip embroidered with magical sigils of power.
According to
Cobiah Marriner
, he was astonishingly powerful. He served aboard the
Pride
after aiding in the defeat of the
Harbinger
, and then later aboard the
Nomad
with
Isaye
. Although reflective and often very strange, Verahd had a mind to rival that of an
asura
, was singularly loyal, and unwaveringly brave. His intelligence helped
Macha
develop the
sextant
, but it was his loyalty that cost him his life, as he boarded the burning wreckage of the
Nomad
after an assassination attempt, in order to rescue Cobiah and Isaye.

Veteran Free Awakened Recruiter Orin
Race
Awakened
Level
80
Rank
Veteran
Location
The Necropolis
(
Domain of Vabbi
)
Organization
Free Awakened
Image(s)
Interactive map
Veteran Free Awakened Recruiter Orin
is an
Awakened
found in
the Necropolis
. Orin attempts to recruit more Awakened to the
Free Awakened
faction while struggling with people being afraid of speaking to him. Upon receiving word from
Taimi
, he contributes to
Aurene
's nest by providing the
Pact Commander
with a
music skull
once the Commander has helped the local Awakened deal with the death of
Palawa Joko
. He later assists the Commander in the battle against the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
in
Thunderhead Peaks
.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Vabbi
The Necropolis
(since activating
Good, Better, Nest
)
Story involvement
Living World Season 4
Episode 5:
All or Nothing
The Crystal Dragon
Dialogue
You there! Let me tell you about the
Free Awakened
. Oh wait—
you
and
your dragon
freed us
!
What are you up to here?
Enlightening those who haven't heard about this new era of freedom! But, well… People seem afraid of speaking to me.
Keep up the good work anyway.
Did
Taimi
reach out to you?
(If
Good, Better, Nest
is active.)
She did! And we'll do anything for our hero. Got an idea, but can't make it happen yet.
(Before completing
Help the living and the dead at the Necropolis
and
Help train the newly Awakened
.)
Why not?
Need something from the folks around here. But they're lackadaisical now that
Joko
's gone. Even a bit depressed. Can you tidy up all their messy feelings? Train 'em, comfort 'em, that kind of thing?
I can try.
Yes, and you'll never guess what we're making for Aurene! Someone donated an old
public service announcement skull
. We're turning it into a
music box
.
(After completing
Help the living and the dead at the Necropolis
and
Help train the newly Awakened
.)
What?
Music kept us brave when we faced the
Forged
. Maybe it will comfort your
dragon
too. We'll drop it off when it's done.
I'm sure she'll love it.
Glad to see you too. Carry on.
Related achievements

:

—
Collect at least 5 gifts for the nest
Taimi
is building.

In Lion's Arch
The Molten Alliance has dissolved! Celebrate with us–whip out yer spirits and enjoy the bonfire.

Here is hope. Here is victory! May out lands regain some semblance of peace with the Molten forces gone.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vidkun
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Vidkun
======================================================================

Vidkun
Race
Norn
Level
10
Rank
Champion
Location
Minotaur Rampant
(
Hunter's Lake
)
Organization
Sons of Svanir
“
My thanks for delivering the Minotaur spirit! My dragon beast needs a full meal before I turn it loose on Hoelbrak.
—
Vidkun
Vidkun
is a fanatical
Son of Svanir
who has been feeding
minotaurs
to
the Dragon Beast
in hopes of drawing out the
Minotaur Spirit
to feed it to
Jormag
.
Biography
After joining the
Sons of Svanir
, Vidkun eventually rose to a high position in their ranks while serving the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
faithfully. His dedication to the Elder Dragon was well known even among the fanatical Sons of Svanir as was his anger at those who failed him.
In 1325
AE
, Vidkun put a plan in motion to capture live
minotaurs
that would be fed to a powerful
Icebrood
dragon champion
known as
the Dragon Beast
. After feeding enough minotaurs to the beast, he decided to have his minions track down and capture
Minotaur
, a lesser
Spirit of the Wild
, to feed it to the Dragon Beast as well to empower Jormag, after which the dragon champion would be powerful enough to be unleashed on
Hoelbrak
and slaughter the weak in Jormag's name.
The
Slayer of Issormir
began investigating the
unusually aggressive behavior of minotaurs
after the Great Hunt of 1325 and came across Vidkun's minion
Hafdan the Black
who boasted about the Sons' plans for the Minotaur spirit. Alerted to Vidkun's ambitions, the Slayer sought the help of famed
norn
heroine
Eir Stegalkin
and saved both the Spirit of the Wild and the minotaurs from the Sons while Vidkun was busy looking after the Dragon Beast.
The norn pair, along with Minotaur, eventually tracked down Vidkun and his hunting party to
Hunter's Lake
where they drew him and the dragon champion out after slaying the rest of the party. Ever boastful, Vidkun belittled the pair's efforts and mockingly thanked them for bringing Minotaur to the Dragon Beast, threatening to unleash the beast on Hoelbrak after the pair had been disposed of. Due to his bond with the Dragon Beast, Vidkun kept reviving each time he was struck down, which forced the norn heroes and Minotaur to change their tactics and strike down the dragon champion first. Vidkun fell for the last time after the Dragon Beast had been defeated, ending the threat to the minotaurs who returned to their old ways.
Story involvement

Vidkun wears a mix between

Trivia
During the
May 20th, 2014
update, champion foes in the chapter 1
personal story
instances were changed to veterans except for Vidkun.
Vidkun is the
Norwegian
younger form of
Old Norse
'Víðkunnr' which means 'famous'. Vidkun's name and siding with Jormag may also be a reference to
Vidkun Quisling
, a Norwegian politician who was a Nazi collaborator and the nominal head of government in the occupied Norway during World War II.

Gallery
Core appearances
Trivia
Asura and male sylvari Vigil Recruits can only be found in
The Silverwastes
starting with
Living World Season 2
.
During Living World Season 2, they used core appearances, with their weapons changed from
Vigil's Honor weapons
to
Pact Fleet weapons
.
With the release of
Heart of Thorns
, they no longer used core appearances and instead adopted generic Vigil models based on existing
crusader
,
marksman
, and
tactician
appearances.
In
Living World Season 4
:
All or Nothing
, they continued to use the Heart of Thorns appearances, but their weapons were updated to
Dragonsblood weapons
.
See also
Corrupted Vigil Recruit

======================================================================
PAGE: Vladok
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Vladok
======================================================================

Vladok
Race
Dredge
Level
26
Locations
Code Breaker
(
Shiverspur Front
,
Underburg
)
Sabotage
(
Hunter's Hearth
,
Underburg
)
“
We never should have listened to Vyacheslav! Killing the last dwarf isn't worth our lives!
—
Vladok
Vladok
is a
dredge
researcher working on the
Sonic Vaporizer
who discovered that the weapon attracted
Icebrood
to its location whenever it was fired. He realized that his superior
Vyacheslav
had become mad with power and would not stop using the weapon despite endangering several dredge lives in the process.
After the
Slayer of Issormir
and a representative from one of the
orders
had saved Vladok from either the Icebrood or his fellow dredge who had turned on him for voicing his concerns, the grateful researcher decided to betray the interests of the
Moletariate
and provide crucial information about the weapon and its intended target so the orders could put an end to Vyacheslav's reign and stop more dredge from dying.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 3: Death from Below
Code Breaker
Sabotage
Trivia
Vladok is
voiced
by
George Coe
.

Vost
was the bosun of the
Indomitable
. During the
Rising of Orr
, him and the whole crew were killed and turned into
Risen
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vova
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Vova
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
.
Vova
is a female
dredge
and one of the higher-ups of the
Molten Alliance
. During the Molten Alliance raids in 1326
AE
, she became anxious whenever the alliance's mysterious benefactor
"B"
was around.
Vova noticed how her comrade
Gigarg
had begun giving the dredge of the alliance orders from "B" and worried how Gigarg was distancing himself from his fellow dredge and viewing himself as superior. As a result, Vova and Gigarg were no longer on good terms and argued over resources as well as Gigarg's newfound friendship with the misogynistic
Rodion the Vile
of the
Flame Legion
.
Notes
Vova did not exist in the
original Flame and Frost release
; she was retroactively written in for the
2022 re-release of Flame and Frost
.

skritt

The Feral Dens
. When the
Destroyer Queen
assaults their
Scratch
, Vrackan helps defending against the destroyers, he may die or survive during the fight. If he survives he will run to the entrance of the scratch and disappear.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wade Samuelsson
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Wade_Samuelsson
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Ghosts of Ascalon
and the
personal story
.
Wade Samuelsson
Race
Human
Level
31
Rank
Veteran
Location
Stronghold of Ebonhawke
(
Fields of Ruin
)
Organization
Ebon Vanguard
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
My forefathers were the kings of Ascalon, and I'll be damned if I let those renegades destroy their last legacy!
—
Wade Samuelsson
Wade Samuelsson
is the
Duke of Ebonhawke
,
Commander of Ascalon
, and leader of the
Ebon Vanguard
who is an avid supporter of the
Ebonhawke Treaty
between
humans
and
charr
. His forefathers were the kings of
Ascalon
.
Biography
Early years
Wade Samuelsson's forefathers were the kings of
Ascalon
.
He lived as a farmer in the
Stronghold of Ebonhawke
. Wade joined the
Ebon Vanguard
to make sure that Ascalonian farmlands would be safe from the
charr
High Legions
that had taken over most of Ascalon. He turned out to have knack for giving orders and was eventually promoted to captain. As time passed, he became the Commander of Ascalon and leader of the
Ebon Vanguard
, successfully defending Ebonhawke from the besieging charr during the
human-charr conflict
. He ruled the city as the Duke of Ebonhawke.
Ghosts of Ascalon
In 1324
AE
, Wade was informed that
Kranxx
, an
asura
tactician from the
Vigil
who had been looking after the city's
asura gate
, had brought a charr test subject to Ebonhawke ostensibly for testing new ways to kill charr with the commander's permission.
However, Kranxx had lied about informing the commander about the experiment as the asura was actually following
General Almorra Soulkeeper
's orders to help
Dougal Keane
's group, which included the charr "prisoner"
Ember Doomforge
, to reach
Ascalon City Ruins
and discreetly retrieve the
Claw of the Khan-Ur
for planned truce negotiations between peace-supporting
humans
and charr factions. The group could not reveal the true purpose of their mission out of fear that any anti-truce factions from either side tried to stop them from completing their mission. Despite the secrecy, the leaders of the truce factions had managed to influence decision-making on both sides so that the siege had been quiet for a few months with no assault from the charr lines and no patrols from Ebonhawke although many soldiers expected the sudden calm to precede a large charr assault.
To keep the Ebon Vanguard distracted and allow him and Dougal's group an easier time to sneak out of the city, Kranxx set up an explosion in his shop, which drew the Vanguard away from the group's destination. Upon hearing his officer's report about Kranxx's claim, Wade realizing that the asura had acted without his permission and had helped the charr prisoner escape. In response, the commander sent his soldiers to search for the fugitives. He would eventually learn that Dougal's group had killed a Vanguard patrol chasing after them and successfully escaped from the city via the sewers.
Interim years
Dougal's team was ultimately successful in retrieving the Claw of the Khan-Ur for Almorra and
Queen Jennah
, setting the stage for the negotiations for the truce that would be known as the
Ebonhawke Treaty
. While Wade was uncertain if the truce factions could find enough consensus to get the treaty signed, he avidly supported the treaty as he was tired of killing and wanted to focus on making something grow again.
As the talks led to a temporary armistice between the charr and humans, rogue groups from both sides refused to stop fighting and formed the charr
Renegades
and the human
Separatists
, respectively. Wade had the Vanguard collaborate with their new charr allies and battle both insurgent factions to ensure continued truce negotiations.
Personal story
In 1325, the Vigil got further involved in the Ebonhawke Treaty as the Renegades were threatening negotiations between Kryta and the
Iron Legion
.
Warmaster Forgal Kernsson
and a recent Vigil
recruit
arrived in Ebonhawke in state of battle.
Renegades had smuggled in explosives to blow up the outer walls. This was successfully prevented, but their forces were attacking the city. The invasion was beaten back after defeating
Gram Skullsplitter
in the central square of Ebonhawke.
Wade told the Vigil troops that the Renegades were getting bolder, because they had a new commander
Ajax Anvilburn
. The Renegade commander had set camp nearby the city, but the Ebon Vanguard could not spare troops at the moment as they were busy guarding the treaty location.
The Vigil promised to take a look at the camp.
Location
Ascalon
Fields of Ruin
Stronghold of Ebonhawke
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 4: Order Neophyte
A Fragile Peace
(as Commander Samuelsson)
Dialogue
Hard to imagine this farm was the site of a bloody battle two hundred years ago. We still find bones and bits of weaponry when we dig for crops each year.
Find anything interesting?
Dug up a blackened
charr
skull. That was more unsettling than anything. A friend of mine found a rusty shield. Not worth anything. Just a bit of history, I suppose.
So, what do you do out here, when you're not digging up relics?
Not much. Just run the
Ebon Vanguard
. Wade Samuelsson, captain of the Ebon Vanguard, at your service.
You seem casual for a military officer.
I wasn't born to be a warrior. I'm just a farmer. I joined the Vanguard to make sure our farmlands would be safe. Turned out I had a knack for giving orders. Before I know it, I'm the captain.
What will you do now after the
treaty
gets signed?
If the treaty gets signed. Farming has taught me that nothing's certain until harvest. But if it happens, I'll gladly hang up my sword. Tired of killing. I just want to make something grow again.
Hopefully you'll get your wish. Take care.
Take care.
Nice to meet you. Good-bye.
Take care.
Interesting.
Quotes
Honor the past.
May the
Gods
protect you.
May you find the path home.
Notes
Both Wade and two
guards
stationed at the
Desert Gate
refer to him as "Captain Samuelsson," whereas Wade introduces himself in the
personal story
as "Commander Samuelsson" and is referred to with the same title in
Ghosts of Ascalon
.
During the
Get the animals back in their pens
event he runs out of the south fields and enters the
Fallen Angels
stronghold.
Legend
says that if at least one of the twin swords
Magdaer
and
Sohothin
is returned to
Ascalon
by the rightful heir of the throne, the
Foefire ghosts
will finally be laid to rest. If his claims are true, Commander Samuelsson may be a possible candidate to fulfill this
prophecy
.
See also
Tyrian royalty family tree
(* relation disputable / not known)

Waine as a child.
Waine
is a
human

's squire when young. When Riannoc went to face

and was overwhelmed, Waine took
Caladbolg
and fled in fear, leaving Riannoc to die. Since then he joined a group of

AE

Watchknight
technology to fight in the
Queen's Gauntlet
.

War Minister Shukov
Race

“
All of this is the beginning of the New Era, of the great opening of the world for the dredge people. With this great opening, we will spill from our mountain homes, and with the aid of our new allies, will come to dominate the lesser races of this land.
— War Minister Shukov
War Minister Shukov
is the leader of the
dredge
found in the
Sorrow's Embrace
. A high-ranking member of the
moletariate
, he is known for being corrupted and a tyrant, overworking the miners like the
Stone Summit
did 250 years ago.

Shukov is not actually fought during Path 2 - he has two golems;
Champion Mark I Golem
and
Champion Mark II Golem
to fight for him.
Trivia
Georgy Zhukov
was a Soviet general and Marshal of the Soviet Union. During the Second World War, Zhukov oversaw some of the Red Army's most decisive victories.

PAGE: Warbeast (Forged)
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Warbeast_(Forged)
======================================================================

This article is about a
Forged
creation.  For other uses, see
Warbeast
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
.
Warbeast
Race
Forged
Level
80
Rank
Epic
Location
Beast of War
(
Forum Highlands
)
Image(s)
Portrait.
“
The Crystal Dragon dares to fight back? Ha! My warbeast is supreme!
—
Balthazar
The
Warbeast
is a giant machine created by the
Forged
, controlled directly by
Balthazar
and designed with the sole purpose of confronting the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. Pieces of it can be found, pre-construction, during
Night of Fires
and
Crystalline Memories
, forshadowing the Warbeast's later appearance. The machine is powered by
Branded
crystals, siphoning
magic
from them. Originally intended to imprison
Glint
's
scion
Vlast
, it was retrofitted to hold the other scion,
Aurene
, after Vlast's demise. During the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
, the Warbeast siphons power from Aurene to paralyze and damage Kralkatorrik before it is destroyed by the
Pact Commander
.
Story involvement
Path of Fire story
Act III
Beast of War
Combat abilities
Skills
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing skills, descriptions.
Beam Attack
-
Engulfing Flames
- You take damage while standing in the aftermath of this creatures beam attacks.
Crush
- Used when the character is under the belly of the beast. Will instantly down the character.
Gallery
Render by
Ronald Kury
.
Render by Ronald Kury.
Trivia
The use of the
track
"Battle in Draconis Mons" in the clash with the Warbeast may be musically referring to the Warbeast as Balthazar's third "hound" as the same track is also used in the battle against the hounds
Temar
and
Tegon
in the
Heart of the Volcano
story instance in
Living World Season 3
.

Warden Illyra

Warden Illyra
is a scholar who focuses her studies on the
Forgotten
.
Location
Ruins of Orr
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)
Nave of Arah
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 8: Victory or Death
The Source of Orr
Dialogue
The culmination of Warden Illyra's
life's work
. She calls it
"Twitchy."
In The Ruined City of Arah
I have a theory about the
Forgotten
and
Glint
. I believe I can show how Glint was freed of the control of the
Elder Dragon
.
The Forgotten?
They were a race of serpent people. Legends connect them to Glint, and I think they were in part responsible for freeing her.
What can you tell me about Glint?
(Same as "Glint?" )
How exactly do you believe Glint was freed?
(Same as "How do you believe Glint was freed?" )
That's an intriguing theory. Thank you.
Glint?
Glint, once known as Glaust, was a
champion
of the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. She was, until recently, a friend to all living things.
How did a champion of an Elder Dragon become a friend to living things?
(Same as "How do you believe Glint was freed?" )
You said until recently. Is she dead?
Yes. When Kralkatorrik awoke, he hunted her down and killed her. Even
Destiny's Edge
could not protect her.
What was it that brought you here to begin with?
(goes back to beginning)
What a loss for our people.
Thank you for the information.
How do you believe Glint was freed?
The Forgotten discovered a great spell that granted Glint a will free from control by the Elder Dragon. She then concealed many of the
elder races
from the dragon.
Who were the Forgotten?
(Same as "The Forgotten?" )
What can you tell me about Glint?
(Same as "Glint?" )
Do you know the spell that they used?
Not yet, but I am close. I believe the spell must be worked here in Arah. I must find that location.
I'm sorry, but can you remind me again what the point of your theory was?
That sounds like a worthwhile quest.
That's an amazing story.
That sounds intriguing. Good luck.
Trivia
She is the author of the book
The Forgotten Not Forgotten
found in the
Durmand Priory
's
Special Collections
.
She wears
Seer Boots
, a

Warmarshal Rairon

serving

Risen

named

was to blame for all of the problems she was having as well as for causing the horrible incident involving the Pact Commander.

======================================================================
PAGE: Warmaster Forgal Kernsson
CATEGORIES: Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Warmaster_Forgal_Kernsson
======================================================================

Warmaster Forgal Kernsson
Race
Norn
Rank
Champion
Location
Tyria
(
Tyria
)
Organization
Vigil
Image(s)
Character introduction cinematic.
“
I am Forgal, son of Kern. My father was the last Dolyak Shaman! I am a Warmaster of the Vigil! You will never make me kneel!
—
Forgal Kernsson
Forgal Kernsson
is a
warmaster
of the
Vigil
. He is the
mentor
for
characters
who choose to join the
Vigil
as part of their
personal story
, and accompanies them throughout a large portion of their story.
Forgal is a stern old
norn
who, unlike some of his kin, embraces the military discipline of his order. He joined the Vigil after losing his wife and children to
Icebrood
. His father was the last shaman of
Ox
. Although at first dismissing the player character's status as a hero of their people and demanding their actions to speak for them instead of rumors, as the player proves their worth and moves up in the ranks of the Vigil, Forgal comes to respect his subordinate and eventual partner as the finest warmaster he has ever met.
During
Forearmed Is Forewarned
, if spoken to,
Warmaster Efut
can tell the player character that Forgal thinks of the player character as a good friend and "the kid I should've had."
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
During
The Battle of Claw Island
, Forgal sacrifices himself to hold off the
Risen
long enough to allow the player and
Trahearne
to evacuate the wounded to
Lion's Arch
.

PAGE: Watch Commander Talon
CATEGORIES: Story characters
